notes: Interception: On 1 January 2024, Hamas militants fired a barrage of about 27 rockets toward southern and central Israel, including Ramla (HaMerkaz) and surrounding areas. The iron dome intercepted 18 rockets, some of them likely over the area of Ramla. The other nine rockets fell in open areas.

On 2 January 2024, Israeli forces opened fire at a Palestinian near Nablus road in Qalqilyah city (Qalqilya, West Bank). Israeli forces also physically assaulted the Palestinian after he was shot. Israeli forces opened fire at a Palestinian ambulance that arrived to treat the injured civilian.

On 2 January 2024, protesters closed down the oil field (means not specified) in El Sharara (Wadi al Hayat, South), calling for the government to improve public service delivery, employ graduates from the region, build a private hospital in the Fezzan, and restructure the Fezzan Reconstruction Fund.

actor1: UFDD: Union of Forces for Democracy and Development
notes: Change to armed group: Around 2 January 2024 (as reported), more than 900 UFDD militants deposed their weapons to Chadian military forces in Faya-Largeau (Borkou, Borkou) in the application of a peace treaty agreement signed in Doha in August 2022.

actor2: Military Forces of the Democratic Republic of Congo (2019-)
notes: On 2 January 2024, FARDC clashed with Mobondo between Betanie and Tika Ngayi, at the 'longue histoire' farm, and 'Mapanda', in Nganda Bangala (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). Casualties unknown.

actor2: Twirwaneho Ethnic Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
notes: On 2 January 2024, Mai-Mai Biloze Bishambuke clashed with the Twirwaneho Banyamulenge in the village of Kivogero, 7 km west of Mikenge, in the Itombwe sector (Itombwe, Mwenga, Sud-Kivu). There were fatalities on both sides, coded to 3. The Mai-Mai Biloze Bishambuke were heading towards Kipupu when they fell into an ambush set by Twrwaneho fighters.

Members of the Kamaka Indigenous people and other Indigenous groups have lived for seven years in two houses on the area, and on 1 January they also occupied a third one.

actor1: Military Forces of Brazil (2023-) Military Police
notes: On 3 January 2024, in Governador Valadares (Minas Gerais), three drug traffickers exchanged fire with military police during their arrest operation in the Turmalina neighborhood. All suspects were arrested. The trio had shot a man to death on 29 December 2023 (coded separately). There were no fatalities.
On 2 January 2024, in Juiz de Fora (Minas Gerais), bus collectors carried out a march to protest against the suppression of their jobs and to demand better working conditions.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), in Vicosa (Minas Gerais), a man was killed and decapitated by unknown individuals and his body was found in the Corrego Sao Joao region with gunshot wounds. The victim had been missing since 26 December 2023. Circumstances of death are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 1 January 2024, in Belem (Para), two armed individuals shot and killed two men in the Guama neighborhood. Two more people were injured by stray bullets. Police described the attack as an execution. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 2 fatalities.
On 2 January 2024, in Belem (Para), residents in the Telegrafo neighborhood blocked streets by setting obstacles on fire to demonstrate against poor trash pickup services.

On 4 January 2024, in Benevides (Para), an armed individual shot and killed a man in the Maguari neighborhood. The suspect fled with an accomplice in a car. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 1 January 2024, in Castanhal (Para), two armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Santa Lidia neighborhood. Two women were injured by stray bullets. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 4 January 2024, in Itaituba (Para), two armed individuals on a motorcycle killed a member of a drug trafficking group in a drive-by shooting in the Bom Remedio neighborhood. A civilian man was injured by stray bullets. 1 fatality.
On 4 January 2024, in Pacaja (Para), two armed individuals shot and killed a man in his private residence on the BR-230 highway. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 3 January 2024, in Porto de Moz (Para), two armed individuals shot and injured a man in the Praiao neighborhood. The victim had been the target of a previous attempt, circumstances not specified. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. There were no fatalities.

On 2 January 2024, in Joao Pessoa (Paraiba), four unidentified armed individuals shot and killed a teenage boy in the Roger neighborhood. The suspects fired over ten shots against the victim (likely an execution). The suspects escaped. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 3 January 2024, in Pombal (Paraiba), two unidentified armed suspects shot and killed a woman in a drive-by shooting. The suspects were on a motorcycle when approached the victim and fired against her. The woman was accompanied by a man, who was not injured. The suspects escaped. Police believe the attack may be related to drug trafficking disputes. The woman was preparing to leave the town. 1 fatality.
On 5 January 2024, in Diamante D'Oeste (Parana), armed individuals blew up a bank. No values were taken. There were no fatalities.

On 5 January 2024, in Recife (Pernambuco), local residents staged a demonstration in the Caxanga Avenue against the lack of energy in the Madalena neighbourhood. The group burned tires and blocked the traffic on the road.

On 5 January 2024, near Recife (Pernambuco), local residents staged a demonstration in the BR-101 highway against the lack of energy in the Nova Descoberta neighbourhood. The group burned tires and blocked the traffic on the road.

Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), in Buriti dos Lopes (Piaui), two men were shot and killed and their bodies found with gunshot wounds in a wooded area of Macambira neighborhood. According to the police investigation, they were selling drugs when they were killed by unknown perpetrators. Police recovered a firearm and portions of drugs in the area. 2 fatalities.

On 3 January 2024, in Parnaiba (Piaui), two unidentified armed individuals shot and killed a man in a drive-by shooting in the Loteamento Santa Luzia 2 neighborhood. The suspects were on a motorcycle when they chased and fired against the victim, who had been attacked on 25 December with a bladed weapon (not coded). The suspects escaped. Motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), in Teresina (Piaui), a man was shot and killed by unknown individuals in the south zone of the city. The victim's body was found with tied hands and feet in a remote area. The victim was last seen on 2 January, when he left home with unidentified people. There is no information about the perpetrators. Circumstances of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.
On 3 January 2024, in Arraial do Cabo (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Morro do Coca-Cola community. Casualties unknown.

notes: On 4 January 2024, in Belford Roxo - Areia Branca (Rio de Janeiro), the military police exchanged fire with drug traffickers in the Castelar community, Piam neighborhood. One suspect was arrested, and military police seized a rifle and portions of drugs. There were no fatalities.
On 2 January 2024, in Niteroi - North (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Caramujo neighborhood. Casualties unknown.
On 1 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - North Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Bras de Pina neighborhood. Casualties unknown.

On 5 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - North Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in Morro do Fuba community, and in the Cascadura, Jacarezinho, and Bonsucesso neighborhoods. Casualties unknown.

Around 2 January 2024 (as reported), in Rio de Janeiro - West Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Bateau Mouche community, in the Praca Seca neighborhood, and Vila Valqueire neighborhood. Casualties unknown.

On 3 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - West Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Cidade de Deus neighborhood. An unknown number of people were injured. As a result, the residents set fire to furniture and trash. Casualties unknown.

On 3 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - West Zone (Rio de Janeiro), residents set fire to furniture and trash in the Cidade de Deus neighborhood, following a shoot-out between unknown shooters in the area. No other information was provided by the source.
On 4 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - West Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Tanque neighborhood. Casualties unknown.
On 5 January 2024, in Rio de Janeiro - West Zone (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the 48 community, Bangu neighborhood. Casualties unknown.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), in Sao Goncalo - Sao Goncalo (Rio de Janeiro), a man was killed and his burnt body was found in the Jardim Alcantara neighborhood. Motivation and perpetrators unknown. 1 fatality.
On 3 January 2024, in Sao Joao de Meriti (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Vila Norma neighborhood. Casualties unknown.

actor1: Military Forces of Brazil (2023-) Military Police
notes: On 4 January 2024, near Canguaretama (Rio Grande do Norte), military police officers shot and killed a passenger of a taxi after the driver did not obey a stop order, according to the officers. The officers approached the car and fired against it, after receiving reports that the car had been stolen. The driver, however, was a private driver with a rented car, taking clients from Paraiba state to the Rio Grande do Norte state. The owner of the rented car believed the vehicle was being robbed. Military police is investigating the case. 1 fatality.
On 3 January 2024, in Camaqua (Rio Grande do Sul), hundreds of people gathered at the BR-116 highway to protest against increase in toll prices.

On 2 January 2024, in Canoas (Rio Grande do Sul), two armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Niteroi neighborhood. Police believe the killing was motivated by drug trafficking disputes, since the deceased had a police record for drug trafficking. 1 fatality.

Around 5 January 2024 (as reported), in Canoas (Rio Grande do Sul), four armed individuals in a car injured two men in a drive-by shooting in the Guajuviras neighborhood. Both the perpetrators and the targets of the attack had a police record for drug trafficking. The perpetrators were arrested. There were no fatalities.

Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), in Sao Leopoldo (Rio Grande do Sul), a woman was killed and dismembered by unknown individuals and her body parts were found inside a suitcase in the Vicentina neighborhood. Circumstances of death are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 1 January 2024, in Ariquemes (Rondonia), two armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Jardim Vitoria neighborhood. The victim's daughter was injured by stray bullets. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 4 January 2024, in Porto Velho (Rondonia), two armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Cunia neighborhood. The victim was wearing a monitoring anklet, charges not specified. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.
On 4 January 2024, in Porto Velho (Rondonia), armed individuals opened fire against rivals (not specified if from criminal groups) in the Santa Barbara housing complex. There were no fatalities.

On 1 January 2024, in Ararangua (Santa Catarina), three armed individuals invaded a private residence in the Divineia neighborhood, shot and killed a teenage boy. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

notes: On 3 January 2024, in Itu (Sao Paulo), the civil police exchanged fire with drug traffickers in the Vila Lucinda neighborhood during a police operation. One suspect was shot and killed while another was arrested. Police seized portions of drugs. 1 fatality.
On 4 January 2024, in Sao Paulo - Central Zone (Sao Paulo), a group of students under the UNE organization marched on Paulista Avenue against the rise in the city's bus prices.

actor1: ORT: Wenteche Catrileo Territorial Resistance Organization
source: Biobio Chile; Cooperativa (Chile); El Austral Temuco; La Tercera (Chile)
notes: Property destruction: On 4 January 2024, near Comunidad Lefiman (Araucania), armed and hooded members of the CAM-affiliated Wenteche Catrileo Territorial Resistance Organization (ORT) threatened forestry workers before setting four trucks and a crane on fire on land owned by the Bosques Cautin forestry company. The ORT, which claimed the attack online, referenced a Mapuche student who was shot dead by police on 3 January 2008 and demanded both the liberation of imprisoned CAM members and the eviction of forestry companies from Mapuche territory. The attack occurred the day before a visit by the Interior Subsecretary to the Araucania region.

On 3 January 2024, in Concepcion (Biobio), around 50 CAM supporters, most of them women, blocked road 150 near the El Manzano prison to demand the liberation of Mapuche prisoners, who they claimed had been detained arbitrarily or for racist reasons. Demonstrators threw stones at police, who dispersed them using water cannons. At least two people were arrested. Several Mapuche individuals convicted of attacks in the Macrozona Sur region are held at El Manzano prison.

On 2 January 2024, in Salamanca (Coquimbo), friends and relatives of an Ecuadorian who was stabbed to death by a Colombian beat the latter and vandalized his house before taking him to the local hospital, where he was arrested.

On 5 January 2024, near Panguipulli (Los Rios), residents of the Campamento del Medio area blocked the road between the commune and Conaripe to demand improvement work on a 3 Km-portion. One disrupted driver ran over a protester, who was subsequently hit by a second vehicle. The protester was injured, but there were no arrests. Protesters held banners and signs.

source: 24 Horas (Chile); Biobio Chile; Cooperativa (Chile); La Nacion (Chile)
notes: On 4 January 2024, in Puente Alto (Metropolitana), neighbors of a man who mistreated his dog until it died gathered outside his house in the Villa Andes neighborhood. Police had to intervene to prevent the man from being lynched, but nobody was arrested. The group also spraypainted and damaged the man's house.

On 3 January 2024, in Santiago - Nunoa (Metropolitana), hundreds of teachers, and legal guardians, as well as the President of the College of Teachers Union, marched from schools to the municipal hall to protest against the dismissal of 278 educational workers employed by the Municipal Corporation for Social Development during the final week of 2023.

On 2 January 2024, in Santiago (Metropolitana), public kindergarten workers (Junji) affiliated with the Association of Administrative, Auxiliary, Technical and Professional Workers at Junji (Aprojunji) marched to the Moneda presidential palace to deliver a letter demanding unpaid end-of-year bonuses, as well as other payments. The march was part of a national strike (workers also protested in Iquique (coded separately)).

source: El Mercurio (Chile); La Estrella de Iquique
notes: On 2 January 2024, in Iquique (Tarapaca), around 60 public kindergarten workers (Junji) affiliated with the Association of Administrative, Auxiliary, Technical and Professional Workers at Junji (Aprojunji) marched to the offices of the Regional Delegation to demand end-of-year payments they were yet to receive. A march also took place in Santiago (coded separately) as part of a nationwide strike.

On 2 January 2024, in Hanga Roa (Valparaiso), residents of the Rapa Nui Island protested outside the offices of the Latam airline at the international airport over the unavailability of discounted tickets to mainland Chile for residents. A 2019 agreement between Latam and the Commission for the Development of Easter Island (Codeipa) provided for 15 such tickets per day, but residents, including members of the Ma'ohi community, in a statement said that these were impossible to book. There were three-month waiting lists for the discounted tickets.

source: El Mercurio Valparaiso; La Estrella De Valparaiso
notes: On 3 January 2024, in Valparaiso (Valparaiso), health workers at the Salvador Psychiatric Hospital affiliated with CONFEDEPRUSS and accompanied by the President of the Congressional Health Commission (AH) and another Congressmember (PRSD) protested outside the establishment over the dismissal of twelve nurses at the end of 2023. Protesters stated that the dismissals occurred abruptly and contradicted pledges made informally by management. They also expressed frustration with personnel and psychiatric bed shortfalls.

source: Biobio Chile; Cooperativa (Chile); El Mercurio Valparaiso; La Estrella De Valparaiso; La Nacion (Chile)
notes: On 3 January 2024, in Valparaiso (Valparaiso), port workers set tires on fire on Blanco street and Errazuriz avenue to demonstrate against unpaid extra hours forced upon them by their employers. Rioters threw heavy objects at police before taking refuge in the headquarters of the Union of Stevedores to avoid the latter's teargas.

source: El Mercurio Valparaiso; La Estrella De Valparaiso
notes: On 3 January 2024, in Vina del Mar (Valparaiso), colleagues and relatives of an Uber driver stabbed to death in April 2023 protested outside Vina del Mar's Court to demand that the two accused receive the harshest possible sentence. Protesters, who held signs, also sought to raise awareness about the violence to which drivers were frequently subjected.

On 4 January 2024, in Medellin - Southwest (Antioquia), residents in the Belen Rincon neighborhood beat up and stripped naked a man who attempted to sexually abuse a girl after breaking into her house. The man was arrested by the police and taken to a hospital for the treatment of his injuries. There were no fatalities.
On 2 January 2024, in Barranquilla (Atlantico), a group of residents in El Prado neighborhood beat up a group of suspected thieves who broke into an apartment to steal. There were no fatalities.

On 5 January 2024, in Barranquilla (Atlantico), unidentified armed individuals on a motorcycle shot and killed a retired police officer in a drive-by shooting in the Ciudad Modesto neighborhood. There is no further information on the motives of the attack. 1 fatality.

On 5 January 2024, in Barranquilla (Atlantico), mothers protested outside the Integral Development Center (CDI) El Eden to demand repairs to the center's infrastructure after rains and flood caused damages in November 2023, leaving 300 students affected.

On 1 January 2024, in Soledad (Atlantico), a group of citizens beat and killed a man accused of having committed a robbery moments earlier in Las Colonias neighborhood. The victim was attacked with sticks and stones. Two more people were injured in unclear circumstances. 1 fatality.

Around 2 January 2024 (as reported), in Montecristo (Bolivar), a farmer was stabbed and killed and his body was found in El Cerrito neighborhood. The victim had gone missing since 31 December 2023. Authorities are investigating if the attack was related to a settling of scores. There is no further information on the motives and perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of the Gulf Clan in the area. 1 fatality.

On 4 January 2024, in Badillo (Cesar), a group of residents protested outside the local hospital to demand the new municipal administration improve the medical service at the hospital. They demanded an emergency service, an ambulance, and a permanent doctor. The protest took place after a man died from a heart attack while being transported to a nearby town over a lack of capacity and oxygen at the local hospital.

On 1 January 2024, in the rural area of El Perro corregimiento (Cesar), a group of people beat up and killed a man using sticks and stones. The man had shot and killed a man and injured another minutes earlier during an argument. Police officers arrived at the scene and found the attacker killed by the community in a wooded area. 1 fatality.

On 5 January 2024, in Hato Nuevo municipality (coded to Hatonuevo, La Guajira), Wayuu Indigenous people blocked the highway in the Guamachito sector to demand the payment of salaries owed to them by the School Food Program (PAE) contractor Canasta Alimentaria - Araurayu.

On 5 January 2024, in Mayapo (La Guajira), Wayuu Indigenous people blocked the Riohacha - Maicao road with tires and tree branches to repudiate eviction operations carried out in the previous days and in December 2023.

On 5 January 2024, in Riohacha (La Guajira), unemployed residents protested in front of the Ecopetrol - Hocol company to demand that the company hire locals for their projects. They said that it often hires personnel from outside the department.

On 1 January 2024, in Puerto Santander (Norte de Santander), unidentified armed individuals shot and killed a man in the 23 de Abril neighborhood. The victim was a former member of the military who had dropped out of the forces in March 2023. There is no further information on the motives and perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of armed groups in the area. 1 fatality.

On 4 January 2024, in Jipijapa (Manabi), construction and office workers of the local government blocked the highway to Guayaquil as a demonstration to demand payment of salaries and Christmas bonuses that had been owed since October.

On 4 January 2024, in Villarrica (Guaira), around 200 nurses from the Regional Hospital and members of the Guaira Nurses Union (SIEG) protested by intermittently blocking the passage at the Villarrica-Paraguari intersection of the PY08 route to reject the massive layoffs and to demand their reinstatement to their jobs. The protesters pointed out that the dismissals were due to political issues.

On 2 January 2024, in San Juan Bautista (Misiones), 45 dismissed health workers from different hospitals in Misiones protested in front of the office of the VIII Health Region demanding their reinstatement.

On 5 January 2024, in San Juan Bautista (Misiones), workers of the Ministry of Health, both healthcare and other workers, protested in front of the local office of the institution, demanding their reinstatement. Two of the protesters chained themselves to the building's entrance gate.

On 5 January 2024, in Pilar (Neembucu), dismissed Social Security Institute (IPS) workers, including both healthcare and other workers, to notes to justify both AAs marched to the residence of the Vice President of the Republic to demand the reinstatement of their jobs.

On 4 January 2024, in Chimbote (Ancash), teachers hired during the positions expansion for secondary schools staged a demonstration to demand payment of their salaries that have been owed for two months.

On 4 January 2024, in San Juan de Marcona (Ica), around 800 residents marched through the city as a demonstration to voice their grievances about the unfulfillment of the local government of repairing the potable water, electricity and sewage systems.

On 3 January 2024, in Maracay (Aragua), a group of residents of the Mata Redonda neighborhood gathered in protest to demand solutions for their housing problems related to the rise in water level of the Valencia Lake. These problems have been going on for at least 15 years.

On 5 January 2024, in Maracay (Aragua), a group of residents of the Pinonal and the Las Acacias neighborhood protested over power outages going on for at least two days. They demanded action from the Corpoelec power company.

On 3 January 2024, in Ciudad Guayana (Bolivar), a group of retired workers at companies belonging to the state-owned Venezuelan Corporation of Guayana (CVG), pensioners, and unionists, gathered outside the CVG headquarters to demand the restoration of their medical insurance and burial plans (HCM), better medical attention, and easier access to medicines. This protest comes after the struggles faced by a representative of the Bolivar state Federation of Associations of Retired Workers and Pensioners (FEDAJUPEBOL) in seeking medical attention, after suffering a stroke (CVA) in the days prior.

On 5 January 2024, in Caracas - Libertador (Distrito Capital), a group of demonstrators, including retired state workers, pensioners, teachers, and representatives of the SITRA-Ensenanza and the Distrito Capital SINVEMA-FVM teachers' unions, and the National Center for Retired Workers and Pensioners, protested to demand better pay, pensions, and working conditions, and payment of owed stipends corresponding to two months of food bonuses. They gathered outside the Public Ministry Office in Carabobo Park.

On 1 January 2024, in El Vinculo (Falcon), several members of the Los Betos Gang injured a woman with a machete, and partially looted her house. It is thought that the son of the victim had an unpaid debt with the suspects. The woman was taken to hospital for treatment. There were no fatalities.

source: Cactus 24; El Impulso; La Patilla; VPItv
notes: On 3 January 2024, in Boca de Aroa (Falcon), a group of residents blocked the Troncal 3 Moron - Coro road with branches and debris in protest over power outages going on for several days. On 3 January 2024, in Tucacas (Falcon), a group of residents of the Brisas del Mar neighborhood blocked the Troncal 3 Moron - Coro road in protest over power outages going on for several days. The protest was lifted in the evening, following negotiations with Corpoelec power company officials.

On 3 January 2024, in Barquisimeto (Lara), a group of recently laid-off workers at the Municipal Institute for Public Sanitation (IMAUBAR) protested to demand their reinstatement in the company, and payment of owed stipends. They gathered by the Labor Ministry Office.

Around 1 January 2024 (as reported), in Caracas - Sucre (Miranda), a member of the Wilexis Gang was shot and killed, and his body found in the Jose Felix Ribas neighborhood, Petare parish. It is thought that the deceased had been intercepted by rival gang members in the early hours, during the New Year's celebrations, and shot dead. There is no further information on the identity of the perpetrators. 1 fatality.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), in Maracaibo (Zulia), a vigilante group of residents of the Teotiste de Gallegos neighborhood, Coquivacoa parish, attempted to lynch a man who they accused of sexually abusing a girl. Police arrived at the scene and found the man tied up and bruised. The suspect was arrested and taken to hospital for treatment. There were no fatalities.

notes: On 4 January 2024, in Maracaibo (Zulia), several members of rival unidentified criminal groups exchanged gunfire, during an armed clash in the Santa Rosa de Agua neighborhood, Coquivacoa parish. There were no fatalities.

source: Diario Version Final (Venezuela); Noticia al Minuto
notes: Property destruction: Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), in San Francisco (Zulia), two members of the Ye Nava Gang opened fire on a house in an extortion-related attack in the Negro Primero neighborhood. The owner of the house and his relatives had received death threats in the days prior, and had refused to pay a 500 USD fee. The attack caused some property damage on the residence and the victims' private vehicle. Before escaping, the suspects left behind an extortion letter signed by the Ye Nava Gang, demanding payment of the 500 USD within the following 24 hours. Both perpetrators were arrested hours later, one of them was an adolescent boy.

Around 1 January 2024 (as reported), Turkish gendarmerie captured and detained 39 migrants that entered Turkey illegally during an operation carried out in Didim district, Aydin.

Around 1 January 2024 (as reported), 28 migrants attempting to cross the border illegally to Greece via boats were captured off the coast of Bodrum, Mugla and detained by the Coast Guard Command of the Turkish army.

Around 2 January 2024 (as reported), Turkish police forces captured and detained 27 migrants (Afghanistan, Iraq, Syria and Pakistan nationals) that entered Turkey illegally during an operation carried out in Edirne town.

On 2 January 2024, DEM members and executives (including an MP) staged a demonstration in front of the university Agri Ibrahim Cecen University, in Agri town, to protest against an Assistant Professor, who, on the 12th anniversary of the bombing in the village of Roboski in Sirnak's Qileban (Uludere) district, had made racist posts on social media, using discriminatory language against Kurds.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), Turkish gendarmerie forces detained and captured 50 migrants who entered Turkey illegally, in Bodrum, Ula and Datca districts of Mugla province (coded to Bodrum district).
On 3 January 2024, union affiliated workers staged a demonstration in Bilecik town to protest against the assault on a teacher and cafeteria worker that occurred on 29 December 2023.

On 3 January 2024, protesters staged a demonstration in Kadikoy, Istanbul, to protest the government's economy policies that result in widening the gap between rich and poor. Representatives of DISK attended the protest.

On 3 January 2024, TIP members and executives continued to protest at the Ataturk Park in Seyhan, Adana, to protest Court of Cassation for not recognizing the Constitutional Court's (AYM) second decision regarding the violation of rights in the case of imprisoned Hatay deputy Can Atalay.

Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), the Turkish Coast Guard intercepted and detained 48 migrants attempting to cross the border illegally to Italy on a boat, off the coast of Seferihisar and Urla districts of Izmir province (coded to Seferihisar).

On 3 January 2024, protesters staged a demonstration at the Hz. Omer mosque in Ipekyolu, Van to condemn the assassination of senior Hamas member Salih al-Arouri and to protest against Israel's ongoing attacks in Gaza.

On 4 January 2024, families who claim their children have been kidnapped by the PKK staged a protest outside the HDP's provincial building in Ipekyolu, Van. The families accused HDP of working with PKK, and demanded their children be returned.

Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), Turkish police forces arrested 49 migrants, who entered Turkey illegally, at a police checkpoint on the Erzurum-Erzincan highway, near Erzincan town.

On 2 January 2024, members of the KCTU Korean Public Service And Transportation Workers' Union Education Workers Solidarity Division (KPTU EDUSOL) North Chungcheong branch held a press conference with banners in front of the Chungcheongbuk-do Office of Education (Cheongju). They protested against the 'unfair' termination of the labor contract of a high school dormitory inspector, citing excessive disciplinary action.

On 2 January 2024, members of the Solidarity Against Disability Discrimination (SADD) held a protest at Hyehwa Station (Seoul City - Jongno), urging the Seoul mayor to withdraw abolishing public jobs for people with disabilities. Police blocked and dispersed the protesters as they attempted to enter the subway to begin a subway-riding protest.

On 2 January 2024, members of the Jeonju Chamber of Commerce and Industry (JCCI, the Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry affiliate) held a press conference with a banner at the JCCI Office (Jeonju), urging the current JCCI chairperson to normalize and implement the process of the 25th chairperson election.

On 1 January 2024, residents from Hampyeong-gun staged a protest at the Yeonsil-bong (Geumgye-ri, Hampyeong). They argued that the mountain's name is Moaksan Mountain. However, at the same time and venue, Yeonggwang-gun residents held a counter-protest and argued that the mountain should be called as Bulgap-san.

On 3 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Chungbuk Conference of Civil Groups and families of victims of the Cheongju snow sledding facility collapse, held a press conference with a banner and picketed at Cheongju City Hall (Cheongju). They urged the authorities to thoroughly investigate to identify the cause and officially apologize for the incident.

On 3 January 2024, protesters, including members of PSPD, Minbyun (lawyers' group), and Citizens' Coalition for Democratic Media, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Korea Broadcasting Hall (Seoul City - Yangcheon). They urged the Korea Communications Standards Commission chairperson to step down for his alleged manipulation of civil complaints.

On 3 January 2024, Korean Teachers and Education Workers Union (under KCTU) Gyeongnam branch members held a press conference with a banner at the Gyeongnam Provincial Office of Education (Changwon). They urged the authorities to withdraw their plan to expand the Neulbom School system, a full-time child-care system, citing it would bring confusion and conflict at schools due to unpreparedness.

On 3 January 2024, protesters staged the 1629th 'Wednesday demonstration' organized by the Korean Council near the former Japanese embassy in Korea (Seoul City - Jongno). They called for an official apology and compensation from the Japanese government for Japanese sex slavery and comfort women.

On 3 January 2024, KCTU-affiliated Rider Union members held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Office of the President (Seoul City - Yongsan). They urged the authorities to implement measures to promote the safety of delivery workers.

On 4 January 2024, protesters, including members of the National Task Force of Victims of Jeonse Rental Fraud, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the authorities to revise the special bill to help victims of housing rental scams ('jeonse scams') with adequate support.

On 4 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union held a press conference with a banner in front of the KB Kookmin Bank headquarters (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the bank to guarantee the job succession of bank call center employees on childcare leave.

On 4 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Government Employees' Union Gwangju branch held a press conference and picketed at Gwangju Nam-gu Office (Gwangju - Nam). They urged the authorities to dismiss and punish high-level government workers who allegedly abused their power and harassed other employees.

On 4 January 2024, DPK-affiliated preliminary candidates for general election held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Chungcheongbuk-do Provincial Council (Cheongju). They criticized the recent attack against the DPK leader and urged the authorities for a strict investigation of the incident.

On 4 January 2024, members of the Pan-Citizens Countermeasures Committee for the relocation of POSCO Holdings to Pohang held a press conference with a banner at Pohang City Hall (Pohang). They welcomed the failure of the POSCO Group chairman to serve his third term, opposing the company's plan to relocate a future technology research institute to Seongnam.

On 3 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Construction Workers' Union Jeonbuk branch held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Korea Rural Community Corporation Jeonbuk Office (Jeonju). They urged the authorities to take responsibility for the recent accident at the civil engineering work site in Gunsan that was ordered by the Korea Rural Community Corporation Jeonbuk Office.

On 3 January 2024, members of the FKTU-affiliated Busan Port Security Corporation labor union held a press conference with a banner in front of the Busan Port Authority (Busan - Jung). They urged for the better treatment of labor union members.

On 3 January 2024, members of the Korea Association for Animal Protection (KAAP) held a press conference and picketed in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They condemned the authorities for the revised bill of the Wildlife Protection And Management Act that bans feeding harmful wild animals, including pigeons.

On 3 January 2024, protesters held a press conference with a banner at the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the authorities to enact a law that could promote the safety of the post-delivery workers.

On 3 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Seoul Alliance of Parents Association and Civic Alliance for Saving Namsan Forest, staged a protest in front of the Seoul Metropolitan Office of Education (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the city government to retract their plan to install gondola lifts in Namsan Mountain, citing it would negatively affect the environment and neighborhood school students' educational rights.

On 3 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Korea Association for Animal Protection (KAAP) and 15 Million Animal Companions, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They called for a legislative ban on the trade and consumption of dog meat.

On 4 January 2024, DPK youth members held a press conference with a banner at the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They condemned the former DPK leader for his plan to leave the DPK and launch a new political party.

On 4 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union staged a sit-in at Jeonju City Hall (Jeonju). They condemned the Jeonju Recycling Town for their alleged labor union oppression.

On 4 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Public Solidarity Trade Union Ulsan branch members staged a protest in front of the Road Traffic Safety Management Corporation (Wonju). They urged the Road Traffic Safety Management Corporation to provide better working conditions for their employees, including a raise in meal allowance.

On 5 January 2024, victims of the toxic humidifier sterilizer and environmental activists held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the Gwanghwamun Plaza (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the authorities to convict the manufacturer of toxic humidifier sterilizers and compensate the victims.

On 4 January 2024, Jeonse Rental Fraud victims held a press conference and picketed in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the authorities to revise the special bill to help victims of housing rental scams ('jeonse scams') with adequate support.

On 4 January 2024, unionized workers staged a demonstration in front of the Bupyeong-gu Office (Incheon - Bupyeong). They protested against the authorities, condemning their administration for imposing the task of an enormous waste disposal to a new subcontractor.

On 5 January 2024, members of Gwangju Jeonnam Candlelight Action held a press conference with a banner in front of Gwangju Metropolitan Police Agency (Gwangju - Gwangsan). They protested against the police, condemning its excessive investigation of a suspect who uploaded a threatening post about the chairman of the PPP's emergency committee, the former minister of Justice.

On 5 January 2024, Progressive Party Jeonbuk branch members held a press conference with a banner at Jeollabuk-do Provincial Council (Jeonju). They protested against the president's veto of the bill on a special investigation over the development scandal in Daejang-dong and the first lady's alleged involvement in stock manipulation.

On 5 January 2024, protesters held a candlelight demonstration at Seoul National University Hospital (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the authorities to investigate the recent attack on the DPK chairman, thoroughly.
On 1 January 2024, dozens of migrants marched to the state Capitol in Denver (Colorado) to call for safe zones for encampments, a halt to planned sweeps and work permits for recent arrivals.

On 1 January 2024, about 50 people, organized by the Colorado Springs People's Coalition, held a pro-Palestine rally at Acacia Park in Colorado Springs (Colorado) to support Palestinian civilians, condemn Israel, and to call for a ceasefire and end of US aid to Israel. A separate group of pro-Israel people gathered to show their solidarity with Israel.

On 1 January 2024, demonstrators rallied at the Century Center in South Bend (Indiana) to protest the inauguration of the new mayor and demand increased representation of and opportunity funding for minority populations, particularly Black citizens. Those present included members of BLM and NAACP.

On 2 January 2024, demonstrators staged a rally and press conference outside City Hall in Chicago - Central (Illinois) to demand they be allowed to march on the day before the Democratic National Convention (DNC) in August 2024 and demand the Democratic party do more to push LGBTQ+ and abortion rights issues. Those present included members of Chicago For Abortion Rights, Stop-Trans Genocide, Reproductive Transparency Now, Chicago Abortion Fund, Gay Liberation Network, and Bodies Outside of Unjust Laws: Coalition for Reproductive Justice and LGBTQ+ Liberation.

On 3 January 2024, demonstrators rallied at a Nelson County School Board Meeting in Bardstown (Kentucky) to support the district Superintendent, and protest the vote for his potential dismissal amid questions related to school closures. The Superintendent was not ultimately dismissed.

On 3 January 2024, workers with AFSCME Council 31 and Local 370 rallied outside the Southern Illinois University (SIU) School of Medicine in Springfield (Illinois) to demand higher wages and an improved contract.

On 4 January 2024, workers with Stand Up KC demonstrated at a Stadium Improvements Committee Hearing in Kansas City (Missouri) to demand a collective bargaining agreement be created to support union workers before tax money goes towards building a new sports stadium.

On 3 January 2024, demonstrators rallied outside the site of a proposed McDonald's in Wilmette (Illinois) to protest its planned building and demand an upcoming Village Board vote rule against it, citing traffic and pollution issues.

On 3 January 2024, around 20 demonstrators staged a rally and banner drop over a bridge in Austin (Texas) to support Palestine, to call for an end to Israeli 'occupation,' and to demand Austin City Council call for a ceasefire amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Those present included members of Students for a Democratic Society and Austin for Palestine Coalition.

On 4 January 2024, a few hundred pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied at Union Station in Chicago - West Side (Illinois) to demand a ceasefire in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Demonstrators also called for an end to Israeli 'aggressions' in Gaza. Those present included members of Students for Justice in Palestine.

On 3 January 2024, a 68-year-old man attacked his Palestinian neighbor in Orland Park (Illinois), punching and yelling anti-Palestinian slurs. The victim was moving trash cans from one part of a communal driveway to another when the perpetrator confronted him about his actions. The argument escalated into the perpetrator punching the victim twice in the face. The perpetrator was charged with a felony hate crime and two misdemeanor counts of battery for the incident.

On 4 January 2024, demonstrators with the Council on American-Islamic Relations staged a press conference and rally in Chicago - Central (Illinois) to demand humanitarian aid be allowed into Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 2 January 2024, people, including the mother of an inmate who was killed by another inmate in his cell at the Broward County Jail, protested in Fort Lauderdale (Florida) demanding an investigation. Surveillance video showed that it took Broward Sheriff's Office deputies over 20 minutes to respond.

On 3 January 2024, residents and members of the Sierra Club Florida gathered outside the Alachua County Commission in Gainesville (Florida) to demand the closing of the Florence Landfill due to environmental and financial concerns.

On 2 January 2024, 3 protesters staged a rally outside Her Majesty's Penitentiary in St. John's (Newfoundland and Labrador) to call on the government to replace the jail with a better facility for rehabilitation.

On 3 January 2024, two dozen people held a pro-Palestinian rally in Ottawa (Ontario) to call for a permanent ceasefire and a 'free Palestine' in the Israel-Hamas war. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Demonstrators also condemned the 'genocide' in Gaza.

Property destruction: On 3 January 2024, an unknown individual or group set the Jewish-owned store International Delicatessen Foods on fire in Toronto - North York (Ontario). No one was injured, and the fires were set in the early morning when no one was inside the building. The words 'Free Palestine' were seen spray-painted on the outside of the building. The Toronto police arson squad and hate crimes unit are investigating. This event took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 1 January 2024, people held a rally on the Avenue Rd. overpass of Hwy. 401 near Jewish neighborhood in Toronto - Old Toronto (Ontario) to call for a permanent ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 1 January 2024, a group of cyclists and pro-Palestine protesters rode with signs and Palestinian flags through Louisville (Kentucky) to demonstrate their support for Palestine, 'call attention to the war in the middle east,' and call for a ceasefire amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. The demonstration was organized by the 'Say Her Name Bike Group' and was held in solidarity with the Gaza Sunbirds, a paracycling team from Gaza.

source: KSAT12; News 4 San Antonio; San Antonio Express-News
notes: On 1 January 2024, almost 100 family members and friends gathered in Woodlawn Lake Park in San Antonio (Texas) to mourn the death of a 22-year-old man who was killed alongside a pregnant 18-year-old woman the day after Christmas and to call for justice for the victims.

On 1 January 2024, pro-Palestinian protesters marched across Brooklyn Bridge into Manhattan and in the Financial District in New York - Manhattan (New York) to support Palestine, to call for a ceasefire, and to oppose 'Zionism' and the state of Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Some protesters also blocked a Starbucks for what they claim is the administration's support of Israel.

source: CBS13 (Maine); Channel 8 WMTW; Maine Beacon; WCSH6
notes: On 2 January 2024, dozens of protesters rallied at the Casco Bay Bridge in Portland (Maine) to support unhoused individuals and to protect their encampment from a scheduled sweep. Authorities also towed about 20 vehicles which were parked in a no-parking zone to block the access to the encampment.

On 2 January 2024, pro-Palestinian protesters rallied at the Bridgeport City Council chambers ahead of the session in Bridgeport (Connecticut) to call on the city for a resolution supporting a ceasefire in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 3 January 2024, more than a dozen pro-Palestinian protesters and members of the Maine Coalition for Palestine rallied outside General Dynamics' (a weapons company) facility in Saco (Maine) to call for a ceasefire in Gaza, and end to what they claimed is 'genocide,' and to oppose the company for supplying weapons to Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 3 January 2024, hundreds of local residents rallied in the Hall of Flags at the State House in Augusta (Maine) to call for an end to gun violence, for a ban on assault rifles, and to support stricter gun laws in the wake of the Lewiston mass shooting. The Maine Gun Safety Coalition organized the rally.
On 3 January 2024, dozens of local residents demonstrated at the State House in Montpelier (Vermont) to support an omnibus bill which would allocate $85 million to flood recovery and prevention.

On 4 January 2024, local residents and members of Community Action for Safe Apartments (CASA) demonstrated at Bronx Housing Court in New York - Bronx (New York) to oppose evictions and to call on the city for support with housing.

On 4 January 2024, protesters and a Reverend rallied in front of a billionaire's office in New York - Manhattan (New York) to condemn him for the comments he made against former Harvard President who then resigned. Protesters claimed that opposition to the former president was due to racism.

On 4 January 2024, protesters and members of Albany 518 SNUG rallied at Second St. and Judson St. in Albany (New York) to call for an end to gun violence after a 44-year-old man was fatally shot on New Year's Day.

On 4 January 2024, workers and members of UAW Local 259 rallied outside of City World Ford in New York - Bronx (New York) as part of a strike to demand that the management recognize unionization and bargain in good faith.

On 5 January 2024, more than 100 protesters and members of over 30 anti-gun violence groups held a vigil and demonstrated on Sutter Avenue in Brownsville and marched to in New York - Brooklyn (New York) to oppose gun and gang violence, as well as to support a 27-year-old outreach worker who was shot while working. Members of Brownsville Think Tank Matters and Brownsville in Violence Out joined the rally.

Property destruction: On 2 January 2024, an unknown perpetrator entered Sandy Bottom Farm, stole a pride flag, slashed holes in three greenhouses, and left an American flag and a letter using homophobic language in Isle La Motte (Vermont).

On 5 January 2024, pro-Palestinian protesters rallied in front of the office of US Rep. Gregory Meeks in Arverne in New York - Queens (New York) to call on the representative to support a permanent ceasefire and to support Palestine amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Protesters also condemned the US for sponsoring what they claim is 'genocide.'

On 4 January 2024, pro-Palestinian protesters surrounded the car of the Secretary of State and covered it with fake blood outside of his home in Arlington (Virginia) to demand that the US stop the 'genocide in Gaza.' Protesters chanted 'shame on you' and 'war criminal.' The demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 2 January 2024, dozens of people, including members of Trans Maryland and Maryland Safe Haven, gathered outside of the Harford County Circuit Court in Baltimore (Maryland) to remember a transgender woman who was killed outside of her apartment. Demonstrators also called for justice for the victim and an investigation into if the killing was a hate crime.

On 2 January 2024, nearly 100 pro-Palestinian people, including Reverends and Imams, gathered outside of the state capitol in Charleston (West Virginia) to demand a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas and to remember the lives lost in the conflict. Demonstrators held pro-Palestinian signs. The demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 4 January 2024, about two dozen people gathered outside the New Mexico Gas Company headquarters in Albuquerque (New Mexico) to demand it withdraw its application to the New Mexico Public Regulation Commission to build a new liquefied natural gas (LNG) storage facility in Rio Rancho due to environmental concerns.

On 5 January 2024, a group of people rallied in downtown Sacramento (California) to demand justice after a child shot and killed another child, and it was announced the perpetrator would not face charges. The rally included Voice of the Youth and the family of the dead child, who was Black.

On 4 January 2024, pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied near the Palm Springs Film Festival in Palm Springs (California) to demand a permanent ceasefire in Palestine, an end to Israel's 'genocide' and to raise visibility for Palestinians amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. They were organized by Coachella Valley for Palestine.

On 1 January 2024, about 65 pro-Palestinian demonstrators temporarily blocked the Rose Parade near Colorado Boulevard and Lake Avenue in Pasadena (California) to call for a ceasefire, a 'Free Palestine', and an end to US military support for Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. They were organized by Latino-Muslim Unity, JVP, Community Solidarity Project, Prayers for Palestine, and SJP and were joined by Palestinian-Americans and the Pasadena Mennonite Church.

On 1 January 2024, about 100 pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied outside US Representative Nancy Pelosi's home in San Francisco (California) to demand a ceasefire in Gaza, support for Palestinian civilians, and an end to US aid for Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 4 January 2024, nurses rallied outside Antelope Valley Medical Center in Lancaster (California) to call for better staffing to address high workloads and poor patient care. They were organized by the California Nurses Association (affiliated with NNU).

On 3 January 2024, hundreds of pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied in the California State Assembly in Sacramento (California) to demand a ceasefire in Gaza, support for Palestinian civilians, and an end to US support for Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. The demonstration was organized by JVP, IfNotNow, and the International Jewish Anti-Zionist Network.

On 3 January 2024, around 50 to 100 demonstrators occupied People's Park in Berkeley (California) to demand the university and police not block off the park with shipping containers so that the university can build student housing. The occupation continued into the next morning (coded separately). The rally was organized by a coalition including Restore People's Park and included students.

On 1 January 2024, several demonstrators gathered in Lancaster (California) to demand justice and a 'full transparent investigation' after a deputy shot and killed a Black woman in early December after she called for help in a domestic dispute. The demonstration included the African American Leadership Coalition and a local bishop. This demonstration was in solidarity with the Black Lives Matter movement.

On 4 January 2024, a DPP legislative candidate and two others held a press conference with placards in New Taipei City (New Taipei City). They presented evidence pointing to a KMT legislative candidate's election bribery .

On 2 January 2024, a DPP legislator and others gathered to protest with placards in front of the Changhua District Prosecutor's Office in Yuanlin City (Changhua). They accused a KMT candidate for smearing the DPP legislator and violating election laws.

On 4 January 2024, three DPP Taichung City councilors and two others held a press conference with placards and fists in Taichung City (Taichung City). They criticized the mayor of Taichung city for not being impartial in her administration, and offering disproportionate credit to the KMT for achievements won by both parties.

On 4 January 2024, a DPP legislator and three others gathered to protest in front of the Control Yuan in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They criticized the KMT presidential candidate and the New Taipei City government for covering up the illegally-constructed estate of a KMT legislative candidate.

On 1 January 2024, the Malaysian immigration department and police forces arrested nearly 100 undocumented migrants during a raid along Thamby Abdullah road in Kuala Lumpur city (Federation of Kuala Lumpur). They did not have valid travel documents.

On 4 January 2024, a DPP candidate, former Hakka committee chairman, and others held a press conference with placards in Kaohsiung City (Kaohsiung City). They criticized the KMT for not caring about the Hakka community, and expressed the desires to support Hakka television and academic research in the future.

actor2: Police Forces of Papua New Guinea (2019-)
notes: Around 1 January 2024 (between 31 December - 2 January), a group of unidentified men armed with axes and other weapons attacked a police station commander and 2 police officers in Tomba (Western Highlands). The policemen were conducting awareness on liquor restrictions along the Tomba and Wabag route when they were attacked. The police station commander was hit on the head, the two officers were seriously injured, and the police vehicle was damaged. The motive of the attack is unclear.

actor2: Police Forces of Papua New Guinea (2019-)
notes: Around 1 January 2024 (between 31 December - 2 January), a group of unidentified men armed with rocks attacked and injured a rural police commander in Baiyer Station (Western Highlands). The motive of the attack is unclear.

On 2 January 2024, people, including Diet members and members of the City Assembly from the JCP, protested in Sendai, Miyagi against what they perceived as financial corruption within the political practices of the LDP.

On 1 January 2024, people, including Diet members and a member of the City Assembly from the JCP, protested in Kyoto, Kyoto against what they perceived as financial corruption within the political practices of the LDP. They also called for an electoral breakthrough of the JCP.
On 1 January 2024, people, including a member of the House of Representatives from the JCP, protested in the Atsuta ward in Nagoya, Aichi, against the political management overseen by the LDP.

On 1 January 2024, people, including Diet members, a member of the Tokyo Metropolitan Assembly, and members of the City Assembly from the JCP, protested in Hachioji, Tokyo against what they perceived as financial corruption within the political practices of the LDP. They advocated for an electoral breakthrough of the JCP.

On 2 January 2024, a barangay tanod chief was killed by an assailant in Barangay Cogunan (Nasugbu, Batangas, Calabarzon). Authorities said that the victim was about to report for duty when the attack happened. The motive for the attack was unclear.

On 1 January 2024, about 250 people, including members of the Helicopter Base Opposition Group and Diet members of the opposition parties of the All Okinawa Council, including a member of the House of Representatives from the JCP, protested in Henoko, Nago, Okinawa against the construction of a U.S. military base in Henoko.

On 1 January 2024, people, including a former member of the House of Representatives and members of the Chino and Suwa City Assembly from the JCP, protested in front of Suwa Taisha in Suwa, Nagano against the financial corruption within the governance of the LDP.

On 1 January 2024, people, including current and former members of the House of Councilors and a former member of the City Assembly from the JCP, protested in Sapporo, Hokkaido against what they perceived as financial corruption within the political practices of the LDP.

On 3 January 2024, three DPP members held a press conference with placards in Taipei City (Taipei City). They presented evidence of China's interference within the 2024 Taiwanese elections, and criticized the KMT for colluding with the Chinese government.

On 1 January 2024, people, including current and former members of the Diet and the City Assembly from the JCP, protested in Kawasaki ward, Kawasaki, Kanagawa against the political management overseen by the LDP.

On 1 January 2024, people, including a former member of the House of Representatives and members of the Prefectural and City Assemblies from the JCP, protested in Fukuyama, Hiroshima, against war, military expansion, and tax increases.

On 2 January 2024, pro-Palestinian activists sprayed graffiti on the WA Labor Party's office on Eastbrook Terrace, Perth - Inner East, Bassendean, Western Australia, to accuse the party of alleged genocide in Gaza. A protester pray-painted 'Free Palestine' and 'New Year Same Genocidal ALP' on the office's walls.

On 1 January 2024, people, including former Diet members and a current member of the House of Representatives from the JCP, protested in Kita ward in Osaka, Osaka, advocating for transparent politics. Additionally, they opposed the international exposition planned for 2025 and the government's proposal to establish a casino in Osaka.

On 3 January 2024, three members of the Animal Welfare Department and Tainan City Veterinary Medical Association held a press conference with placards at the Tainan City municipal center in Anding district (Tainan City). They addressed the sharp recent uptake in ferret badger bitings, gave precautionary information to the public on how to proceed when encountering such animals, and urged people not to taunt them.

On 1 January 2024, people, including Diet members, members of the City Assembly, and a former member of the House of Representatives from the JCP, protested in Saitama, Saitama against the Kishida administration for several reasons. The concerns included an alleged undocumented political fund scandal, perceived inadequacies in birthrate reduction measures, proposed abolition of the current health card system, and the administration's stance on the armed conflict between Hamas and Israel.

On 5 January 2024, people protested in front of Shin Urayasu station in Urayasu, Chiba, against the corrupt politics of the LDP. Members of the SDPJ and New Socialist Party, a former member of the Ichikawa City Assembly from the JCP, and CDP and independent members of the Urayasu City Assembly attended the event.

On 4 January 2024, local district chiefs and members of a resident group held a press conference with banners and placards in Cigu district (Tainan City). They called on presidential candidates to take a stance on the issue of sex trafficking in Cigu district.

On 4 January 2024, less than 40 nurses protested outside the Royal Perth Hospital in Perth - Central, Western Australia, to encourage fellow nurses to decline a proposed 3 percent pay increase put forth by the State Government. The protesters claimed that their labor union did not adequately represent their interests.

On 2 January 2024, members of the Muhammadiyah Students Association (IMM) protested in the Paser regency (coded as Tanah Grogot district, Paser regency, East Kalimantan province). They opposed the coal mining activities in Batu Sopang district over environmental and security concerns.

On 2 January 2024, seven members from various news agencies and research institutes gathered to hold a press conference while displaying fists in Taipei City (Taipei City). They demanded that presidential candidates take a clear stance on Taiwanese sovereignty.

On 4 January 2024, a group of people from the JSKK organization peacefully protested in front of the presidential palace in Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). They demanded the resolution of human rights violation cases. It was a part of a weekly protest Thursday Act.

On 4 January 2024, three KMT candidates and three others held a press conference with banners at the Taichung City council in Taichung City (Taichung City). They accused the Taichung District Prosecutor's Office of being unable to maintain political partiality, and of using threatening and humiliating tactics during investigations of Chinese infiltration.

On 3 January 2024, dozens of truck drivers, accompanied by their families, peacefully protested in front of the council building in Tanah Grogot district (Paser district, East Kalimantan province). They opposed the ban of coal trucks from passing Batu Kajang village, claiming it negatively affected their operations. The ban was imposed due to safety concerns.

On 4 January 2024, shareholders from a game development company held a protest with placards outside of the Taipei District Prosecutor's Office in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). This protest accompanied the shareholders' filing of a lawsuit against the company for engaging in a share exchange that went against the interests of the shareholders.

On 2 January 2024, two members from different labor groups and a legislative candidate from the TRP gathered to hold a press conference with a banner at the DPP headquarters in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They criticized the DPP vaccine policy, claiming that it killed many people.

On 3 January 2024, a NP legislative candidate and two others gathered to protest with placards in front of the DPP campaign headquarters in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They accused a DPP legislator for doing business in China.

On 4 January 2024, three NPP members, including the chairman, held a press conference with placards in Taipei City (Taipei City). They criticized the KMT vice-presidential candidate for not withdrawing his shares from the Broadcasting Corporation of China after stepping down as chairman.

On 2 January 2024, hundreds of members of the Labour Party (PB) protested in front of the Indonesian Election Supervisory Agency (Bawaslu) office in Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). They opposed the removal of around 32 Labour Party legislative candidates in 13 provinces from the Permanent Candidate List (DCT).

On 2 January 2024, PISTON members staged a protest at their terminal in Pasig City (Metropolitan Manila, National Capital Region) to oppose the Public Utility Vehicle Modernization Program (PUVMP), which would phaseout traditional jeepneys.

On 4 January 2024, around 100 students held a peaceful protest in front of the Puspemkab Bireun building in Juang city (Bireun regency, Aceh province). They demanded the relocation of Rohingya refugees from the area.

On 5 January 2024, a group of Catholic students from the PMKRI student organization peacefully protested in front of a police station in Kefamenanu district (North Central Timor regency, East Nusa Tenggara province). They demanded a corruption investigation within the police forces.

On 4 January 2024, members of the NSHSTU and other education' groups gathered to protest with placards at the Ministry of Education in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They demanded that the government immediately review its safety procedures in schools in the wake of the stabbing incident at a New Taipei City high school.

source: Citizens That Don't Allow Biased Broadcasting Toward Okinawa
notes: On 4 January 2024, about 60 people gathered in front of the Ministry of Defense in Tokyo - Shinjuku, to protest against the government's decision to make a 'substitute execution,' wherein it grants approval on behalf of the Okinawa government for the proposed design modifications related to the planned U.S. military base in Henoko. The event was organised by Executive Committee that Doesn't Allow the Construction of Military Bases at Henoko.

On 5 January 2024, environmental activists held a protest with banners and placards outside of the Legislative Yuan in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). Protesters threw shoes at a placard displaying the face of the former Environmental Protection Administration chief, criticizing the way that previous carbon tax policies had been implemented.

On 3 January 2024, dozens of people from the Constitution Savior Forum (FPK) civic movement peacefully protested in front of the administrative court in Cakung district, East Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - East). They demanded an improvement in the Constitutional Court leadership during the 2024 election period.

source: Sapporo Action that Asks for the Immediate Cancellation of Landfill of Henoko

On 5 January 2024, AWC Youth members protested in front of the Itochu Corporation's Osaka headquarters in Osaka, Osaka, to urge ITOCHU Aviation Co., Ltd. to sever its business ties with the Israeli military industry. They expressed their support with Palestine by displaying its flag.
On 5 January 2024, people protested in front of the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry in Tokyo - Chiyoda against the use of nuclear plants.

On 5 January 2024, people protested in front of the Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology in Tokyo - Chiyoda, to call on the government to apply free high school education to Korean schools too.

On 3 January 2024, dozens of people from the South Solok Civic Alliance peacefully protested in front of the public prosecutor's office in Pauah Duo district (South Solok regency, West Sumatra province). They demanded a corruption investigation within the regency administration.

On 2 January 2024, a barangay councilor was killed by an assailant in Barangay Salay (Mangaldan, Pangasinan, Ilocos Region). The assailant was then apprehended by patrolling police officers after a firefight, during which he was shot and wounded. The motive for the attack was unclear.

On 5 January 2024, dozens of people peacefully protested in front of the sub-district administration building in Banyuroto sub-district, Nanggulan district (Kulon Progo regency, Yogyakarta province). They criticized the sub-district head and his staff, claiming they were not neutral during the 2024 election campaign period.

On 2 January 2024, the NAPPA held a press conference with banners outside of the Ministry of Education in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They demanded the improvement of campus safety measures both in schools and in the legal system in response to the fatal stabbing of a student in New Taipei City on 25 December 2023.

On 5 January 2024, hundreds of people from the Anti-Corruption Network (Jakor) civic movement peacefully protested in front of the governor's office in Palembang city (South Sumatra province). They criticized the Ogan Komering Ilir regent's leadership.

On 5 January 2024, Malaysian police and immigration arrested 238 foreigners from Myanmar, Bangladesh, Indonesia, Nepal, Thailand, Pakistan, and India during a raid on Kota Baru Road, Kuala Terengganu (Kuala Nerus district, Terengganu state). They did not have valid staying permits.
On 3 January 2024, taxi drivers engaged in a protest ride with their taxis through Banja Luka, demanding support from the city council to provide them with proper taxi stands.

On 3 January 2024, on the first day of the trial of 11 defendants held responsible for the collapse of Isias hotel in Adiyaman in Turkey (in which 35 Turkish Cypriots, including 24 children, were killed in February 2023), Turkish Cypriot teachers, organized by KTOEOS, rallied near the Prime Minister's office in Northern Nicosia demanding the defendants be charged with intentionally killing the children, finding insufficient the charges of 'causing death by negligence'.

On 3 January 2024, 3 activists of Oyan Kazakhstan group attempted to protest in support of fellow activists who were detained days earlier by Monument to Independence near Almaly district of Almaty city. Police detained all of them.

On 3 January 2024, workers of the West Oil Software company protested on the premises in the town of Zhetibay in Karakiya district of Mangystau region demanding that the authorities integrate West Oil Software into the state-run Kazmunaygaz enterprise.

On 3 January 2024, employees of the municipality gathered in the Skopje neighborhood of Karposh to protest against what they claim is the irresponsible behavior of the local authorities who failed to approve a local budget for 2024 due to political infighting. On 4 January 2024, residents of Razgrad protested against the recently announced increase in water prices, claiming that the higher prices are unfair and that the quality of the water supply -- which has a darker color and distinct smell -- makes it not worth paying for in the first place.

On 4 January 2024, 1000-2000 students, teachers, university employees and other activists gathered for a march in Prague, commemorating the recent mass shooting at the Faculty of Arts of Charles University. They marched from the rectorate seat to the main faculty building, commemorating the victims and calling for solidarity, understanding and empathy.
On 4 January 2024, activists gathered near the US embassy in Prague, protesting against what they see as a 'colour revolution' being organized by the White House in Serbia.

On 4 January 2024, several locals protested in Debrecen by crossing a street repeatedly, condemning the lack of reforms in education and the authorization of the building of a car battery plant near the city.
On 4 January 2024, residents of Zhyldyz-3 neighborhood gathered outside their homes in Atyrau city in protest of unstable supply of power demanding that the city authorities resolve the issue.

On 4 January 2024, around 20 demonstrators attempted to disrupt the defense and aerospace company's event at the Munch Museum in Oslo to condemn the company's involvement in supplying arms to the Israeli Defense Forces. The activists were stopped by the security guards and police personnel.

On 4 January 2024, Palestinian community members continued their demonstration at a protest camp outside of the Parliament of Norway in Oslo to show support and solidarity with Palestine in response to the Israeli offensive in Gaza. Police later dispersed the demonstrators and dismantled the camp. The camp has been in place since 11 November, 2023.
