On 2 January 2024, truck drivers during the 2nd day of the strike, burned tires and held a demonstration in Patna city (Bihar) as part of a 2 day nationwide strike called by the AIMTC against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 2 January 2024, truck drivers, private bus operators along with taxi drivers also joined the 2nd day of the strike, set ablaze a police vehicle, clashed and threw stones at police personnel in Bhinay block (Ajmer, Rajasthan) during a demonstration against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 3 January 2024, scores of people tried to vandalize the bus involved in a fatal accident near Laxmi Talkies in Sambalpur city (Odisha) during a demonstration over the entry of a bus into a crowded area. Police had to resort to lathi-charge and detain a dozen of demonstrators to disperse the crowd.

On 3 January 2024, family members of a deceased undertrial refused to take custody of his body and held a protest at V S Hospital in Ahmedabad city (Ahmadabad, Gujarat) claiming custodial torture leading to the death (coded separately).

On 5 January 2024, residents blocked the road under Bihta police station in Patna city (Bihar) to protest against the killing of a woman (coded separately) over a land dispute. She was also the prime witness in her son's murder.

On 3 January 2024, members of the All Parties Hurriyat Conference Azad Jammu and Kashmir (APHC-AJK) chapter held a protest demonstration outside the Indian High Commission in Islamabad city (Islamabad, FCT), against the Indian government over its decision to repeal Article 370 of the Indian constitution, ending the special status for India administered Jammu & Kashmir, and bifurcate the state into two union territories.

On 5 January 2024, members of Sunni Ulema Council held a protest demonstration outside the PIMS Hospital in Islamabad city (Islamabad, FCT), demanding arrest of the assailant for killing their leader (coded separately).

On 2 January 2024, a large number of men, women and children staged a protest demonstration at Quetta road in Jacobabad city (Jacobabad, Sindh), against unscheduled and prolonged load shedding of Sui gas.
On 4 January 2024, activists of SUP held a protest demonstration in Kandiaro town (Naushahro Feroze, Sindh), against gas load shedding.
On 1 January 2024, unidentified armed men opened fire at the party office, injuring 3 activists of Sunni Tehreek in Moosa colony in Karachi - Gulberg town (Karachi City, Sindh).

On 1 January 2024, teachers, staff and students of Shah Abdul Latif University (SALU) held a protest in Khairpur city (Khairpur, Sindh), demanding removal of the Vice Chancellor due to his 'indecent' video leaked on social media.

On 2 January 2024, members of AAC along with various political groups, civil society organisations, trade unions, transport and tourism sectors staged a large protest a protest in Khaplu town (Ghanche, GB) against the hike in subsidized wheat prices from Rs. 20 to Rs. 36 per kg. The AAC called for daily sit-ins until their charter of demands, which includes restoring the price of wheat to the 2022 level and addressing the prolonged electricity crisis, is accepted.

On 2 January 2024, members of AAC along with various political groups, civil society organisations, trade unions, transport and tourism sectors staged a large protest a protest in Kharmang town (Kharmang, GB) against the hike in subsidized wheat prices from Rs. 20 to Rs. 36 per kg. The AAC called for daily sit-ins until their charter of demands, which includes restoring the price of wheat to the 2022 level and addressing the prolonged electricity crisis, is accepted.

On 3 January 2024, Islami Jamiat Talaba protesters staged a sit-in at the Pak-Austria Fachhochschule Institute of Applied Sciences and Technology in Mang area (Haripur) against a nighttime musical event. Despite attempts by local officials to disperse them, the sit-in continued into the night. The IJT had lodged a formal complaint demanding the event's cancellation, which they claimed violated local cultural values.

On 2 January 2024, oil tanker drivers under the banner of All India Motor Transport Congress held a protest by staging a blockade and not moving out their vehicles at Haldia city (Purba Medinipur district, WB) as part of a 2 day nationwide strike against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 3 January 2024, truck drivers under the banner of All India Motor Transport Congress held a protest at Narayangarh city (Paschim Medinipur district, WB) as part of a 2 day nationwide strike called by the AIMTC against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

Detonation: On 2 January 2024, a bomb exploded while being manufactured by BJP supporters at a house in Bhupatinagar town (Purba Medinipur district, WB). 2 BJP supporters were left injured in the explosion. Police later arrested them in injured condition.

On 4 January 2024, residents staged a protest and blocked the Karakoram Highway, in Perhana area in Manshera district (Mansehra, KPK) demanding the arrest of the officers involved in a fatal incident which occurred on the Hazara Motorway where a man was killed, and another injured after their vehicle exploded following police gunfire during a chase.

On 2 January 2024, BJKM members held a protest march that started from Fatapukur and ended at Rajganj (coded as Rajganj town (Jalpaiguri district, WB) in support of 9-point demands including retrieving government land and halting illegal sand mining from rivers.
On 4 January 2024, a bomb planted by an unidentified group exploded when a minor student attempted to play with it at Daulatabad town (Murshidabad district, WB). He was killed in the explosion.
On 2 January 2024, members of the Communist Party held a protest demonstration in Multan city (Multan, Punjab), for the acceptance of their demands. No further details reported.

On 5 January 2024, transporters blocked the Quetta-Karachi main highway near Lak Pass area (Mastung district, Balochistan) during a protest to demand action for the retrieval of 4000 vehicles stranded in Chaman due to a protest sit-in. They urged authorities to intervene and facilitate the movement of their vehicles.

On 2 January 2024, 8-10 militants (coded as TTP) attempted to attack a police checkpost at Khan Khawar area in Peshawar city (KP), but were prevented by police forces (likely by preventive firing), causing the militants to flee. There were no casualties.
On 2 January 2024, unidentified assailants fired at a doctor on his way back home at Pir Alizai town (Killa Abdullah district, Balochistan). She was killed in the attack.

On 3 January 2024, family and residents of Jungle Pir Alizai area staged a protest at the Quetta-Chaman Highway in Qilla Abdullah (Killa Abdullah, BA) against the killing of a doctor by unknown assailants. Traffic on the highway remained suspended for several hours during the protest. Shops, markets and business centres also remained shut as a mark of protest against the shooting.

On 3 January 2024, owners, waiters, salesmen of eateries along with members of the Restaurant Catering Sweets and Bakers Association held a protest near Liaquat Bagh in Rawalpindi city (Rawalpindi, Punjab), demanding withdrawal of an increase in gas tariff.

On 1 January 2024, hundreds of residents of Shabqadar blocked traffic and staged a sit-in protest at the local grid station (Charsadda, KPK) against excessive power outages, demanding immediate action to resolve the issue.

On 2 January 2024, members of AAC along with various political groups, civil society organisations, trade unions, transport and tourism sectors staged a large protest a protest in Shigar town (Shigar, GB) against the hike in subsidized wheat prices from Rs. 20 to Rs. 36 per kg. The AAC has called for daily sit-ins until their charter of demands, which includes restoring the price of wheat to the 2022 level and addressing the prolonged electricity crisis, is accepted.

On 2 January 2024, members of AAC along with various political groups, civil society organisations, trade unions, transport and tourism sectors staged a large protest a protest at Yadgar-i-Shuhada in Skardu city (Baltistan, GB) against the hike in subsidized wheat prices from Rs. 20 to Rs. 36 per kg. The AAC called for daily sit-ins until their charter of demands, which includes restoring the price of wheat to the 2022 level and addressing the prolonged electricity crisis, is accepted.

On 1 January 2024, oil tank drivers under the banner of All India Motor Transport Congress held a protest march at Jammu city (J&K) as part of a 2 day nationwide strike called by the AIMTC against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 2 January 2024, transporters and drivers under the banner of All India Motor Transport Congress held a protest march as part of a nationwide strike at Jammu city (J&K) as part of a 2 day nationwide strike called by the AIMTC against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 1 January 2024, locals pelted stones and vandalized a police vehicle at the Tulsipur-Ghorahi road section in Tulsipur city (Dang district), following the death of a child after being hit by a bus in a road accident. The incident occurred after the police tried to pick up the dead child's body.

On 3 January 2024, police intervened by arresting seven members and leaders of the AJP who were staging a protest in front of the Customs Department in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district). The protesters were demanding the repealment of the 45 percent commission provision in the customs law. A person sustained injuries during the incident.

On 1 January 2024, ANNFSU and YAN staged a protest in Jumla town (Jumla district) in response to the deaths of two demonstrators who had failed the South Korea Employment Permit System (EPS) language examination for the shipbuilding sector at Balkumari locality in Lalitpur city (coded as a separate event). The protesters were demanding local employment opportunities, expressing dissatisfaction with the government's failure to generate jobs within the country.

On 4 January 2024, members of the BJP staged a protest outside the Deputy Commissioner's office in Yadgir city (Karnataka) against the arrest of a Hindutva activist involved in Hubballi riots in 1992 ahead of the inauguration of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. The protesters accused the INC-led state government of attempting to appease one community and conspiring against Hindus.

On 3 January 2024, members of the BJP staged protests across Karnataka state (coded to Bengaluru, Bengaluru Urban) against the arrest of a Hindutva activist involved in Hubballi riots in 1992 ahead of the inauguration of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya.

On 3 January 2024, members of the BJP including MLAs, a former MLA, other legislators and members of the SSK Samaj (Hindu group) staged a protest in front of the Town Police Station in Hubballi city (Dharwad, Karnataka) against the arrest of a Hindutva activist involved in Hubballi riots in 1992 ahead of the inauguration of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. Police detained 150 protesters.

On 3 January 2024, hundreds of farmers gathered under the banner of BKU (Ugrahan), led by the BKU (Ugrahan) General secretary, and staged a dharna (sit-in protest), raising slogans, in front of the District Administrative Complex (DAC) in Sangrur city (Punjab) against the state government for failing to fulfill its promise of carrying out 'intkal' (change of ownership) of a piece of land in the name of two farmers.

actor1: CPN(RM): Communist Party of Nepal (Revolutionary Maoist)
notes: Land seizure: Around 4 January 2024 (as reported), the CPN(RM) captured 35 ropani of land in a wetland area that belonged to the government in Prabhas locality of Tansen municipality (Palpa district). The party claimed the land seizure was aimed at protecting natural resources and wetlands, opposing the land mafias engaged in activities such as filling ponds and encroaching on wetlands.

On 1 January 2024, police intervened by arresting three members of the CPN(RM), who were staging a protest in front of the office of the Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district), for chanting anti-government slogans.

On 1 January 2024, dairy farmers staged a protest at the Domlur Veterinary Hospital in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) against the INC-led state government's decision to close down the hospital. The protesters brought two dozen heads of cattle to the hospital to mark their protest.

Around 3 January 2024 (as reported), a group of farmers belonging to the Walava Farmers' Union held a protest at the Agricultural Industrial and Insurance Board in Embilipitiya town (Ratnapura, Sabaragamuwa), demanding crop damage compensation and fertilizer subsidy.

On 1 January 2024, ex-servicemen (ESM) gathered under the banner of United Front of ESM and staged a 'black day' protest in Baldwara village (Mandi, Himachal Pradesh) against the central government whom the protesters accused of failing to address the issues in the 'One Rank One Pension-2' scheme.

On 4 January 2024, local people, led by a Councillor (also Civil Society President), staged a protest, raising slogans, at the Municipal Corporation (MC) office in Hisar city (Haryana) demanding the implementation of the Right to Service Act and the resolution of various civic issues related to property tax, sewerage, and stray animal menace.

On 4 January 2024, around 100 nurses along with a CPI(M) MLA staged a protest and attempted to lay siege to the Collectorate in Kannur city (Kerala) demanding the BJP-led Union government to grant the state government it's financial share, sort out ration issues, grant nurse promotions, leave allowances, and clear salary revision arrears.

On 3 January 2024, doctors and other employees of government hospitals including members of the Karnataka State Government Medical Officers Association staged a flash protest in Athani town (Belagavi, Karnataka) demanding the arrest of a social activist who attacked a doctor on duty. The protesters also submitted a memorandum to the police officers demanding the arrest of the accused.

On 2 January 2024, supporters of an Awami League candidate vandalized the campaign venue of an independent candidate, and assaulted his supporters in Nayabazar area, Fenchuganj upazila (Sylhet), over electoral rivalry, ahead of upcoming national elections.

On 4 January 2024, members of Awami League assaulted and injured supporters of an independent candidate twice in Chandina upazila (Comilla, Chittagong), over electoral rivalry, ahead of upcoming national elections. At least 17 people, including 3 journalists were injured in the assault.

On 4 January 2024, supporters of an Awami League candidate assaulted and injured at least 4 supporters of an independent candidate in Mirsharai upazila (Chittagong), over electoral rivalry, ahead of upcoming national elections.

On 2 January 2024, health workers in the Koshi province staged a protest outside the Koshi state assembly building in Biratnagar city (Morang district). On 1 January 2024, 210 medical students and residential doctors, including non-clinical employees, staged a sit-in protest at Kathmandu Medical College in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district). On 5 January 2024, members of Hindu organizations staged a protest, raising slogans along the Tanakpur Road in Pilibhit city (Uttar Pradesh) against the suspected encroachment of government land near an ancient religious site.

On 4 January 2024, hundreds of employees and pensioners gathered under the banner of the Joint Struggle Committee of employees and engineers and staged a protest at the State Electricity Board headquarters in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) demanding the payment of pending salaries and pensions.

On 3 January 2024, Pepsu Road Transport Corporation (PRTC) drivers staged a protest at the intersection outside the interstate bus terminus in Patiala city (Punjab) over various demands (which likely includes the withdrawal of the strict provisions applicable to truck operators and commercial drivers stated within the Motor Vehicle Act 2023 and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (Indian Justice Code) - given the nationwide strike over the same issue that concluded on 2 January).

The protesters were demanding justice for the two youths who died in the Balkumari incident in Lalitpur city (coded as a separate event), while also raising questions about various corruption cases.

On 7 January 2024, a suspected Fulani militia together with a Jukun militia attacked civilians at Mchia (Logo, Benue) coded to Ugba. The attack happened in the morning and in the evening, resulting in at least 5 persons killed and several others wounded in the attack in the morning, and at least 2 persons killed and scores of persons missing in the evening. 7 fatalities coded.
On 9 January 2024, an unidentified armed group of three suspected to be assassins killed a traditional chief in Shagamu (Shagamu, Ogun). The reason for the attack is unknown.

On 7 January 2024, a clash between farmers and herders left one man dead and two others injured in Damutawa village (Jahun, Jigawa). The clash erupted when the herders were passing through cattle routes and had an encounter with the farmers. There was one fatality.

On 11 January 2024, a mob attacked and injured three people accused of being responsible for the death of a missing toddler in Itori (Ewekoro, Ogun). The victims were taken to the hospital for treatment.

On 10 January 2024, in Santiago del Estero (Capital, Santiago del Estero), artists and cultural workers protested near Del Pueblo Theater against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70/2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy, and against the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State and a reduction of financing to cultural activities. The mobilization occurred within the framework of the national protest day named 'Cultural Cacerolazo.'

On 10 January 2024, in Tandil (Buenos Aires), artists and cultural workers protested against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70/2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy, and against the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State and a reduction of the budget or the closure of cultural entities. The mobilization occurred within the framework of the national protest day named 'Cultural Cacerolazo'.

On 10 January 2024, in Villa Gesell (Buenos Aires), artists and cultural workers protested against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70/2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy, and against the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State and a reduction of the budget or the closure of cultural entities. The mobilization occurred within the framework of the national protest day named 'Cultural Cacerolazo'.
On 11 January 2024, in La Rioja (Capital, La Rioja), active and retired police officers protested in 25 de Mayo Square to demand better salaries.

On 9 January 2024, in Trelew (Rawson, Chubut), members of the Construction Workers' Union of Argentina (UOCRA) marched to the Municipality to protest against the dismissal of 500 construction workers who had lost their jobs due to the federal government's lack of financing.

On 7 January 2024, in Rio Gallegos (Guer Aike, Santa Cruz), relatives and friends of the teenager who died after being run over at the intersection of Avenida Eva Peron and 17 de Octubre Highway demonstrated in the place to demand justice for the case.
On 8 January 2024, friends and relatives of a femicide victim marched from Rawson Square through the streets of the town to demand justice for the crime.
On 9 January 2024, in Comodoro Rivadavia (Escalante, Chubut), a group of families protested in front of the Municipality to demand the delivery of housing lots assigned to them through a resolution.

source: El Diario de la Republica; El Territorio
notes: On 11 January 2024, in San Luis (Juan Martin de Pueyrredon, San Luis), members of a football team demonstrated in front of the headquarters of the Sanluisena Soccer League to protest against the provincial government's intervention in the club. Participants demanded the disqualification of various teams from competing in national tournaments.

On 12 January 2024, in Villa La Ribera (Iriondo, Santa Fe), residents protested against the continuous passage of trucks on National Route 11 and Provincial Route 91. They demanded more road safety in the area.

On 12 January 2024, in La Rioja (Capital, La Rioja), active and retired police officers rioted in 25 de Mayo Square to demand better salaries. Participants burned tires on the road. Participants demanded not to be sanctioned for their collective action.

On 6 January 2024, in La Paz (La Paz), health workers blocked different streets as a demonstration to demand justice in the case of a doctor that was found killed in his house in Yanacachi sector. They demanded security for them to carry out their jobs.

On 9 January 2024, in La Paz (La Paz), unionized transportation workers members of the Yungas Transport Federation blocked the entrance and exit of the MINASA terminal to demand maintenance of highways by the Bolivian Highway Management (ABC).

On 11 January 2024, in La Guardia (Santa Cruz), parents of students and neighbors blocked the 17KM of a highway as a demonstration to demand the immediate removal of the Education District Director due to the lack of will of carrying out school boards.

source: El Deber; La Razon (Bolivia); Los Tiempos (Bolivia); Opinion (Bolivia)
notes: On 11 January 2024, in Sipe Sipe (Cochabamba), residents and leaders of the Viloma Cala Cala, Sause Rancho, and Parotani took control of the Municipal Council offices as a demonstration to demand the resignation from two female council members that they claim have not audit the municipal government. They forced the two council members to sign an absence license.

On 12 January 2024, in La Guardia (Santa Cruz), for the second consecutive day, parents of students and neighbors blocked the 17KM of a highway as a demonstration to demand the immediate removal of the Education District Director due to the lack of will of carrying out school boards.

source: El Deber; La Patria en Linea; Los Tiempos (Bolivia)
notes: On 7 January 2024, in Challapata (Oruro), more than 200 residents of the south of the city captured, beat up, and killed a man that had previously robbed the parts of a car. 1 fatality.

On 9 January 2024, in Melipilla (Metropolitana), unionized workers at the Maitenes Clinic off road 78 protested outside the establishment after its owners refused to pay out both bonuses negotiated in 2020 and a contribution to their union. The company (Network of Regional Clinics, a subsidiary of the Chilean Security Association (AChS)) had taken over this and other clinics in August 2023. Protesters, who were on strike, stated that the new owners had attempted to weaken their union following massive layoffs.

On 12 January 2024, in Concepcion (Biobio), workers at the local Health Service belonging to FENPRUSS and FENATS occupied the establishment to demand that a shortfall of 30 employees be filled and that authorities act more honestly and transparently. Protesters also expressed their concern over the continued employment of health workers hired during the coronavirus pandemic.

On 8 January 2024, in Pozo Almonte (Tarapaca), taxi drivers operating at the border between Chile and Bolivia, including members of the Santo Tomas driver group, protested outside the offices of the Provincial Delegation against the introduction of a shorter window for pedestrian crossings at the Colchane border crossing on 24 December. Protesters, who demanded a meeting with the Provincial Delegate, stated that pedestrians could now only cross in the morning, leading to considerably lower demand and thus lost revenue.

source: El Mercurio Valparaiso; La Estrella De Valparaiso; Revista Minera Crisol
notes: On 10 January 2024, in Valparaiso (Valparaiso), between around 300 and 450 workers at the Cerro Negro mining company in Cabildo commune, including members of the Federation of Mining Trade Unions of Petorca Province, protested alongside the mayors of Cabildo (PDC) and La Ligua (Ind.) and a Congressman (PS) outside the offices of the Regional Government to demand that the Committee of Ministers consider conditionally approving the company's continued operations after 3 of 18 scrutinizing agencies raised environmental concerns. Protesters stated that the mining operation provided over 500 jobs.

On 5 January 2024, in Concepcion (Biobio), residents of the Barrio Norte neighborhood, as well as members of the Committee for the Defense of Flora and Fauna (CODEFF), members of the Let's Protect Lo Mendez group and other neighborhood organizations, protested on Abdon Cifuentes square to raise awareness about the fact that the waters of the Lo Mendez lagoon had turned bright green and to demand that authorities urgently address the issue.

source: Biobio Chile; La Cuarta (Chile); La Nacion (Chile)
notes: On 7 January 2024, in Santiago - Los Cerrillos (Metropolitana), around 40 locals attacked a man accused of raping a girl. The rioters, many of whom were the girl's relatives, also threw heavy objects at the victim and police when the latter intervened at the junction between Las Torres and Alaska streets. The victim, who was suspected of sustaining a fractured skull, was hospitalized and arrested.

source: Biobio Chile; Cooperativa (Chile); La Cuarta (Chile); Lider San Antonio
notes: On 10 January 2024, in San Antonio (Valparaiso), residents kicked a woman and beat her with a wooden stick after she was accused of attempting to kidnap a baby in the informal settlement (megatoma) located in the Cerro La Virgen (also known as Cerro Centinela) area. Rioters, some of whom reported other such attempted kidnappings, subsequently damaged the woman's car and set it on fire at the entrance to the settlement. Police arrested the woman.

On 9 January 2024, near Lleu Lleu (Biobio), unidentified individuals burned tires on road P-72-S and threw stones at vehicles to demonstrate against the arrest of three suspected RML members (coded separately). Police dispersed the rioters.

On 9 January 2024, in Calbuco (Los Lagos), around 20 legal guardians of students at the commune's Rural Pargua School, as well as other local residents, set tires on fire and waved signs to demand that a school inspector be taken into custody during the investigation of allegations that he had raped a student. The suspect was under house arrest.

source: Biobio Chile; El Austral Temuco; El Mercurio (Chile); La Tercera (Chile)
notes: Property destruction: On 8 January 2024, near Cullinco (Araucania), unidentified individuals threw incendiary devices at an administrator's house on the Cullinco estate off road R-827 at around Km 11. The reason for the attack, which resulted in the building's total destruction, was unknown, but the Araucania region frequently experiences similar attacks perpetrated by Indigenous militias demanding land restitution.

actor1: ACSN: Self-Defense Conquerors of Sierra Nevada
notes: Around 11 January 2024 (as reported), in the rural area of Cienaga municipality (Magdalena), ACSN members kidnapped 16 farmers from the Toribio River. The victims' whereabouts are unknown.

actor1: FARC Dissident - Jaime Martinez: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia Dissident Faction (Jaime Martinez)
notes: Around 5 January 2024 (as reported), in the rural area of Buenaventura municipality (Valle del Cauca), two men were killed and their bodies were found in the Dagua River. The victims had been missing for two days, were killed by suspected members of the Jaime Martinez FARC dissident group. They are also investigating whether the victims were connected to any armed group or criminal gangs operating in the area. 2 fatalities.

On 9 January 2024, in La Pascuala (Bolivar), six Gulf Clan members shot at a social leader at his house after subduing the bodyguards and taking their guns away. The leader was unharmed. The perpetrators escaped and clashed with security forces shortly after (coded separately). There were no casualties.

On 9 January 2024, in Valledupar (Cesar), suspected Gulf Clan members shot and killed two men and injured another in the Vista Hermosa irregular settlement. One of the deceased victims was a Venezuelan citizen, as well as the injured man. A Gulf Clan pamphlet was found at the scene. Authorities are investigating whether the case is related to land disputes since the victims had a discussion with a woman moments before the attack over land in the settlement. 2 fatalities.

On 9 January 2024, in La Pascuala (Bolivar), six Gulf Clan members clashed with military forces after perpetrating an attack against a social leader (coded separately). One Gulf Clan member was injured. There were no fatalities.

actor2: FARC Dissident - 33rd Front: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia Dissident Faction (33rd Front)
notes: Property destruction: Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), in the rural area of Cucuta municipality (Norte de Santander), military forces destroyed a cocaine production laboratory owned by the FARC dissident group 33rd Front.

On 12 January 2024, in Cucuta - Central (Norte de Santander), JAC members protested outside the Mayor's Office to repudiate violence and killings in the city. This comes after a social leader and JAC member was shot dead in the previous days (coded separately). They also demanded guarantees and protection for social leaders.

On 9 January 2024, in Riohacha (La Guajira), around 50 workers at the gas extraction industry and members of the National Board of the Union of Workers and the Association of Non-Renewable Energy Workers of La Guajira blocked 15th Street (National Route 90) in front of the Ecopetrol - Hocol facilities to demand employment opportunities from energy sector companies. The demonstrators demanded their right to work, arguing that these companies have been prioritizing labor from other regions instead of local workers.

On 12 January 2024, in Bucaramanga (Santander), taxi drivers protested in the city center because municipal authorities had not yet defined new taxi fares for 2024. Although the date to establish the increase in fares was set for 23 January, taxi drivers disagreed claiming that the date was not soon enough.

On 12 January 2024, in Guayabetal (Cundinamarca), residents and truck drivers blocked the Bogota - Villavicencio highway to demand the National Infrastructure Agency (ANI) and the Coviandina consortium to take measures to improve vehicular mobility in the area. The highway has been with restrictions in the circulation of vehicles since the end of December when a truck exploded after an accident. Protesters also demanded that the government conduct works to stabilize the mountains since they are constantly affected by landslides that cause the closure of the road.

On 12 January 2024, in Monterredondo (Cundinamarca), residents and truck drivers blocked the Bogota - Villavicencio highway to demand the National Infrastructure Agency (ANI) and the Coviandina consortium to take measures to improve vehicular mobility in the area. The highway has been with restrictions in the circulation of vehicles since the end of December when a truck exploded after an accident. Protesters also demanded that the government conduct works to stabilize the mountains since they are constantly affected by landslides that cause the closure of the road.

Around 11 January 2024 (as reported), in Cucuta (Norte de Santander), UNP bodyguards protested to repudiate the decrease in the travel expenses money paid to them. Also, they demanded that the amount of money agreed upon in their labor contract be considered a base to define the increase.
On 9 January 2024, between Mosquera and Funza municipalities (coded to Mosquera, Cundinamarca), citizens held a protest. There is no further information about the motives of the demonstration.

On 9 January 2024, in Santa Marta (Magdalena), dozens of people beat up a suspected thief in the Bavaria neighborhood. The police intervened to prevent the man from being lynched and he was arrested. The rioters then proceeded to set the suspect's motorcycle on fire. There were no casualties.

On 10 January 2024, between Neiva and Baraya municipalities (coded to Neiva, Huila), a group of people beat up and stabbed a motorcycle driver who moments earlier had accidentally run over an elderly woman and was assisting her. The attackers were relatives of the woman who died. The driver was killed by the mob. 1 fatality.

On 8 January 2024, in Cucuta - North (Norte de Santander), residents in the Cerro Norte neighborhood threw stones and glass bottles at police officers to prevent the arrest of a man who was wanted by authorities for several criminal activities. The suspect was arrested. There were no casualties.

On 9 January 2024, in Puerto Gaitan (Meta), irregular settlers who had occupied a school and municipality property clashed with police officers during an eviction operation. The rioters attacked municipal officials and workers at the Public Ministry and the Colombian Institute of Family Welfare (ICBF). 13 rioters were arrested by the police. There were no fatalities.

On 4 January 2024, in Cucuta - North (Norte de Santander), unidentified assailants shot and killed a man in the Colinas del Tunal informal settlement. There is no further information about the motives or the perpetrators of the attack but the area is known for the presence of the ELN and local criminal gangs. 1 fatality.

Around 5 January 2024 (as reported), in Banco De Arenas corregimiento (Norte de Santander), a man was shot and killed and his body was found near the local cemetery. There is no further information about the motives or the perpetrators but there is presence of armed groups in the area. 1 fatality.

On 5 January 2024, in the rural area of Ipiales municipality (Narino), unidentified perpetrators shot and killed a farmer in the Llano Grande vereda. There is no further information about the motives or the perpetrators of the attack. 1 fatality.

On 6 January 2024, in Jordania (Meta), unidentified assailants shot and killed a former FARC member. There is no further information about the motives or the perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of FARC dissidents groups and local criminal gangs in the area. 1 fatality.

Around 7 January 2024 (as reported), around Banco De Arenas corregimiento (Norte de Santander), a man was shot and killed and his body was found on the road that leads to the El Infierno sector. There is no further information on the motives and perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of armed groups in the area. 1 fatality.

On 7 January 2024, in Girardot (Cundinamarca), unidentified armed men on a motorcycle (drive-by shooting) opened fire on the vehicle in which a local councilman (CR) was traveling on Ferrocarril Avenue. The victim, who had just left a council session, was injured in one of his arms and taken to hospital. There were no fatalities.

On 9 January 2024, in Maicao (La Guajira), unidentified armed individuals on a motorcycle shot and injured a Wayuu Indigenous man who was taken to hospital for treatment. The attack is suspected to be related to the victim's involvement in a theft. There were no fatalities.

Around 9 January 2024 (as reported), in the rural area of Mutata municipality (Antioquia), an Indigenous man was shot and killed and his body was found near the La 14 sector. Another body was found near the Surrambay River. There is no further information on the motives or perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of the Gulf Clan in the area. 2 fatalities.

Around 9 January 2024 (as reported), in Sincelejo (Sucre), unidentified perpetrators shot and killed a man in the Santander Park. The attack is suspected to be related to a retaliation between criminal groups and gangs for the control of drug trafficking. The victim had criminal records for multiple crimes. There is presence of the Gulf Clan and local criminal gangs in the area. 1 fatality.

Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), in Cucuta (Norte de Santander), unidentified individuals beat up and injured an LGBTQ+ activist and members of an organization for victims of the armed conflict after forcing him to get into a car. The victim was released shortly after and taken to hospital. The victim had received threats in the past. There were no fatalities.

On 10 January 2024, in Malambo (Atlantico), unidentified armed individuals opened fire at the house of a woman social leader when she was receiving a visit from a councilman for the Renascent Colombia Party in the Villa Bertha neighborhood. The social leader was also a supporter of the councilman's party. There were no casualties.

Property destruction: Around 12 January 2024 (as reported), in El Banco (Magdalena), unidentified individuals set on fire the car of a cattle farmer that was parked outside his home. The attack is suspected to be related to extortion threats by individuals who identified themselves as members of an armed group.

On 12 January 2024, in San Francisco (Valle del Cauca), unidentified perpetrators shot and killed three people. At least one of the victims had a criminal record. There is no further information on the motives and perpetrators of the attack but the area is known for the presence of armed groups. 3 fatalities.

Around 9 January 2024 (as reported), around Banco De Arenas corregimiento (Norte de Santander), a Venezuelan man was shot and killed and his body was found on the road that leads to the El Infierno sector. There is no further information on the motives and perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of armed groups in the area. 1 fatality.
On 8 January 2024, in Pichincha (Manabi), unionized local government workers staged a demonstration to demand payment of their salaries that have been pending since October.

On 9 January 2024, in Ayolas (Misiones), members of peasant organizations intermittently blocked route PY01 to demand improvements to local roads, agricultural assistance, and the reinstatement of 47 public health workers whose contracts were not renewed for the new year.

On 7 January 2024, in Arroyos y Esteros (Cordillera), residents of Mainumby district protested against the establishment of a landfill in the area. The protesters denounced that the landfill would endanger the population's health and cause environmental damage.

On 8 January 2024, in Ayolas (Misiones), residents of the San Ignacio district protested in front of the regional office of the Ministry of Public Works and Communications (MOPC) to demand the repair of local roads and bridges.

On 8 January 2024, in Remansito (Villa Hayes, Presidente Hayes), neighbors protested against the construction of a wall that affected the Paraguay River. The protesters claimed that the construction harms the ecosystem and causes flooding on rainy days.

On 5 January 2024, in Punchana (Loreto), unionized health workers of the regional health management (GERESA) staged a demonstration in front of their offices to demand the fulfillment of their demands from last year, that include the immediate payment of workers, and the change of technical team and administrative workers of the entity.

On 6 January 2024, in Punchana (Loreto), unionized health workers of the regional health management (GERESA) staged a demonstration in front of their offices to demand the fulfillment of their demands from last year, that include the immediate payment of workers, and the change of technical team and administrative workers of the entity.

On 7 January 2024, in Pisco (Ica), retired and laid off health workers at San Juan de Dios hospital and teachers staged a demonstration to voice their grievances about the salaries for the last month that have not been paid.

On 9 January 2024, in Pisco (Ica), retired and laid off health workers at San Juan de Dios hospital, and teachers of the Local Education Management Unit staged a demonstration to voice their grievances about the salaries for the last month that have not been paid.

On 9 January 2024, around Puno province (Puno), civil construction workers, merchants at Union y Dignidad market, and local residents marched as a demonstration to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 9 January 2024, around Tacna province (Tacna), members of citizens' organizations staged a demonstration to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 9 January 2024, around Arequipa Province (Arequipa), supporters of the former president Pedro Castillo (Free Peru) marched as a demonstration to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 7 January 2024, in San Miguel (Puno), residents and family members of the victims of the riots of 2023 in Juliaca staged a demonstration at Armas square to demand justice in the case and the resignation of president Dina Boluarte. This demonstration was held as commemoration of the anniversary of the riots.

On 9 January 2024, in Ayaviri district (Puno), members of the United Neighborhoods Association and local residents marched as a demonstration to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 9 January 2024, in Lima - Lima District (Lima), members of citizens' organizations and local residents marched to San Martin square as a demonstration to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 10 January 2024, in Cusco (Cusco), members of citizens' organizations and local residents staged a demonstration at 28 de Julio avenue to demand the resignation of president Dina Boluarte, the closure of congress and early presidential elections to be called.

On 11 January 2024, in Chimbote (Ancash), residents of the Miramar Bajo neighborhood blocked a the Enrique Meiggs avenue as a demonstration to demand local authorities to place a speed bump and traffic lights in the avenue for cars to go through their sector slower.

On 11 January 2024, in Lima - Jesus Maria (Lima), foreign residents who work informally as windshield cleaners attacked municipal workers who tried to evict them from where they work. Police officers intervened and arrested one of the foreigners.

On 9 January 2024, in Bajo Pichanaqui (Junin), transportation workers clashed to compete for passengers on the marginal highway besides the bus station. A local (sereno) and national police officer and a transit inspector intervened and stopped the fight.

On 9 January 2024, in Montevideo (Montevideo), workers at a delivery services company, along with representatives of the Inter-Union Plenary of Workers - National Convention of Workers (PIT-CNT) and the Uruguayan Federation of Commerce and Services Employees (Fuecys), protested in front of the Ministry of Labor and Social Security (MTSS) against the dismissal of 250 workers.
On 5 January 2024, in Salto (Salto), neighbors protested by blocking access to the municipal landfill to denounce the environmental pollution and affectations on public health.
On 8 January 2024, Turkish airstrikes targeted PKK positions in the Seedan area of Duhok. Casualties unknown.

On 9 January 2024, Turkish army forces conducted drone strikes against PKK militants in Sida (Seedan) area of Al Amadiya district, Duhok in conjunction with a targeting with a Turkish suicide drone. HPG (PKK) claimed that Turkish forces used bombs containing poisonous gas in its airstrikes, but this was not confirmed by other sources. Casualties unknown.
On 6 January 2024, Turkish army drones carried out airstrikes in Wesan village (Kurdish name) in Choman area of Rawanduz district, Erbil. Casualties unknown.
On 6 January 2024, Turkish army drones carried out airstrikes in Pirane Res and Zewke villages (Kurdish names) in Pishdar region of Al Sulaymaniyah. Casualties unknown.
On 7 January 2024, Turkish airstrikes targeted Jabal Biradawst mountain in Erbil. Casualties unknown.
Around 10 January 2024 (between 7 - 10 January), Turkish army soldiers attack, presumably via firearms, to PKK positions in Sida (Seedan) area of Al Amadiya district, Duhok. Casualties unknown.

On 8 January 2024, Iraqi police attempted to arrest a wanted individual in an unspecified location in the As Samawah area, Muthanna. The wanted person attacked the police with a grenade and small arms fire. Police shot and injured the wanted person. Police seized weapons and narcotics. The person was wanted for narcotics trafficking and robbery. There were no fatalities.

On 12 January 2024, Iraqi security forces attempted to arrest a narcotics smuggler in a house north of As Samawah city. The arrest led to an armed clash with the smuggler. Iraqi security forces sustained 1 fatality and 8 injured.

On 10 January 2024, Diwaniya police conducted an operation to rescue a female abduction victim in Nahiyat ad Dagharah district, Qadissiya. The victim was abducted on an unspecified date. The raid resulted in an armed clash. 1 abductor was killed, 2 were injured, and 4 (including 2 women) were arrested. 4 policemen were killed and 2 were injured. 5 fatalities and 4 injured in total.

actor1: Police Forces of Iraq (2022-) Falcons Intelligence Cell
notes: On 7 January 2024, the Iraqi Falcons Cell conducted a raid leading to armed clashes with narcotics traffickers in the Sumer neighborhood of Nassriya city. 1 member of the cell was injured and he succumbed to his wounds on 8 January. 1 fatality.
On 6 January 2024, Erbil counter narcotics arrested 3 narcotic smugglers and seized 5kg of crystal meth in an unspecified location in Erbil city.
On 12 January 2024, Diyala counter narcotics detained a narcotics smuggler with 1 kg of Crystal meth in an unspecified location in Diyala. Coded as Baquba city.
On 8 January 2024, unknown tribal militants shot and killed 2 civilians in Khair district, Maysan. The attack was motivated by a pre-existing tribal dispute.

Security measures: On 9 January 2024, Iraqi security force declared a state of alert in the Diyala countryside. The state of alert was declared in response to an IED attack an Iraqi army patrol vehicle in the Rawajih area of Muhammad Sarkan district, 25 KM southwest of Baquba city. Coded as Al Husseiniya, Baghdad.

actor1: Police Forces of Iraq (2022-) Rapid Reaction Force
notes: Security measures: On 12 January 2024, Iraqi Rapid Reaction forces deployed to Baghdad International Airport. The deployment assumed security responsibilities from the Canadian security firm Business Intel after a report by the Organization for Combating Organized Crime and Corruption found the company had no prior experience in the security sector and many of its employees had not been paid.

Weapons seizure: On 9 January 2024, Iraqi National Security Service and Army discovered and seized a VBIED workshop belonging to unknown militants in the Albu Ghantus orchard of At Tarmiyah district, Baghdad. The workshop contained various materials used to manufacture VBIEDs as well as 5 VESTS ready for use, 4 hand grenades, detonators, and an unspecified quantity of explosives.

actor1: Military Forces of Iraq (2022-) Popular Mobilization Forces
notes: Defusal: On 5 January 2024, the 19th Brigade of the Popular Mobilization Forces discovered and dismantled 8 explosive devices belonging to unknown militants planted along the Iraqi-Syrian border strip, west of Anbar. Coded as Ramadi City.

actor1: Military Forces of Iraq (2022-) Popular Mobilization Forces
notes: Defusal: On 10 January 2024, the Popular Mobilization Forces discovered and defused an IED attached to a power transmission tower on the Anbar - Salah Al Din line in western Anbar province. Coded as Ramadi city.
On 7 January 2024, law graduates protested in an unspecified location in Muthanna province. The protesters demanded job opportunities. Coded as As Samawah city.

On 7 January 2024, dozens of college graduates protested in front of the Federal Health Service building in As Samawah city. The protesters demanded job opportunities in the health and education departments.
On 7 January 2024, bakers protested in Erbil city. The protesters opposed a new price ceiling on bread limiting the price of 10 loaves to 1,000 Iraqi Dinars (about 0.75 USD).

On 7 January 2024, hundreds of youths protested in front of provincial building in Nassriya city. The protest coincided with the arrival of PM Sudani to inaugurate the Ya Hussein Road. The protesters called on the PM to provide 9,000 job opportunities adding they have demanded this for 3 years.
On 8 January 2024, bakers protested in Erbil city. The protesters opposed a new price ceiling on bread limiting the price of 10 loaves to 1,000 Iraqi Dinars (about 0.75 USD).

On 8 January 2024, private generator operators protested in front of the Oil Products Distribution Company in Kut city. The protesters demanded an increase in the fuel quota and the cancellation of the sponsorship requirement. The operators currently receive 5 liters of fuel but stated they need at least 10 liters to keep providing electricity during the designated hours.

On 10 January 2024, non-lecturing graduates protested in Kut city. The protesters the protesters organized a sit-in in front of the provincial building and demanded job opportunities in reconstruction projects. The protesters threatened to escalate their methods if their demands are not met.

On 11 January 2024, law school graduates protested in AS Samawah city. The protesters demanded job opportunities in the public sector. The protesters stated they will continue to demonstrate until their demands are met.

On 7 January 2024, students and teachers protested at the Agricultural High School in an unspecified location in Muthanna province. The protesters opposed plans to convert a part of the school building in toa regimental headquarters for the police. Coded as As Samawah city.
On 7 January 2024, teachers of Koya protested in Koisnjaq, Erbil. The protesters demanded unpaid wages and to be put on permanent contracts.
On 8 January 2024, teachers and educational staff protested in Halabja city. The protesters demanded to be paid their salaries on time and the appointment of lecturers to permanent positions.
On 8 January 2024, teachers and educational staff protested in Kalar city. The protesters demanded to be paid their salaries on time and the appointment of lecturers to permanent positions.

On 11 January 2024, a landmine planted by Unknown Armed Group detonated killing 1 civilian and injuring 4 in an unspecified location on the Iraqi side of the Iran border in northern Iraq. The explosion took place after Afghans crossed the border illegally and triggered a mine. Coded as Erbil city.

On 9 January 2024, a landmine from a previous but unspecified conflict planted by Unknown Armed Group detonated injuring a 36 year-old woman and her daughter in the Abu Ghar area of the southern Thi Qar desert. The victims are Bedouin and were herding livestock when the blast occurred. There were no fatalities. Coded as Nassriya city.
On 9 January 2024, unknown militants detonated an IED destroying a power transmission tower on the Anbar - Salah Al Din line in western Anbar province. Coded as Ramadi city.

On 8 January 2024, unknown militants detonated an explosive device targeting an Iraqi army patrol vehicle in the Rawajih area of Muhammad Sarkan district, 25 KM southwest of Baquba city. The blast injured 2. There were no fatalities. Coded as Al Husseiniya, Baghdad.

On 8 January 2024, Turkish army shelled PKK positions in Seedan area in Al Amadiya district, Duhok. HPG (PKK) claimed that Turkish forces used bombs containing poisonous gas during the strikes, but this was not confirmed by other sources. Casualties unknown.
On 11 January 2024, unknown militants targeted Turkish forces with rockets at the Zilkan base in Bashiqah, Ninewa. Casualties unknown.

On 7 January 2024, graduates from different sectors demonstrated in As Samawah city. The demonstrators blocked streets with burning tires. Around 6 January 2024 (weekend of), in Sao Joao de Meriti (Rio de Janeiro), CV and TCP members exchanged fire during a territorial dispute in the Trio de Ouro community. Casualties unknown.
On 12 January 2024, in Teresopolis (Rio de Janeiro), unknown shooters exchanged fire in the Corte da Barra neighborhood. Casualties unknown.

On 7 January 2024, in Ielmo Marinho (Rio Grande do Norte), five unidentified armed individuals shot and killed two people in a supermarket. The suspects arrived at the location in a vehicle, and four of them got out and fired against the supermarket's security guard, who was the target of the attack. The suspects fired multiple times, even after the victim was already on the floor. A second victim, who was an off-duty military police officer, was also shot and killed. The suspects escaped. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 2 fatalities.

On 6 January 2024, in Natal (Rio Grande do Norte), local residents burned down a bus on the Igapo Avenue, blocking the traffic. The group demonstrated against the killing of young victims in the Beira Rio community during a police operation, the circumstances of which are unknown.

On 8 January 2024, in Natal (Rio Grande do Norte), local residents protested against the killing of young boys in the Beira Rio community. The protesters held signs and demanded justice. The protest followed the burning of a bus on the previous day (coded separately).

On 12 January 2024, in Pendencias (Rio Grande do Norte), around three or four armed individuals shot three people during a social event. The suspects entered the event's venue and fired against the three victims. One victim was shot and killed, and the two others were injured. The suspects escaped. Authorship and motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 8 January 2024, in Caxias do Sul (Rio Grande do Sul), three armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Desvio Rizzo neighborhood. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 9 January 2024, in Caxias do Sul (Rio Grande do Sul), unknown armed individuals shot and killed a man in the Primeiro de Maio neighborhood. The deceased had a police record for drug trafficking. 1 fatality.

Around 7 January 2024 (as reported), in Passo Fundo (Rio Grande do Sul), a man was killed by unknown individuals and his burnt body was found inside the trunk of a car in the Petropolis neighborhood. Circumstances of death are unknown. 1 fatality.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), in Passo Fundo (Rio Grande do Sul), a man was killed by unknown individuals and his partially burnt body was found in the Valinhos neighborhood, with stab wounds. The victim had a police record, charges not specified and was under house arrest. Circumstances of death are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 5 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), armed individuals shot and killed a woman in the Mario Quintana neighborhood. The deceased had a police record for drug trafficking, and her killing was carried out in the context of rivalries between drug trafficking groups. 1 fatality.

On 6 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), armed individuals shot and injured a man in the Mario Quintana neighborhood. The victim had a police record for drug trafficking, and the attack was carried out in the context of rivalries between drug trafficking groups. There were no fatalities.

On 8 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), more than 60 residents of a housing complex in the Rubem Berta neighborhood carried out a march to protest against the construction company responsible for the buildings. One of the buildings had a gas leak and an explosion destroyed part of it.

On 8 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), hundreds of people carried out a protest to support democratic institutions and to condemn the invasion of Congress on 8 January 2023 by supporters of former President Bolsonaro (PL). The former Governor (PT), members of the PV, CUT, CPERS union, pre-candidates for mayor from PSOL and PT participated.

On 12 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), people carried out a protest to demand the end of gender-based violence, after a Venezuelan female traveling circus artist was raped and then was found dead with signs of violence during a road trip.

On 11 January 2024, in Rio Grande (Rio Grande do Sul), dozens of labor unions, members of MST, members of social movements and city councilors carried out a protest against increase in toll prices. During the demonstration, vehicles were exempt of toll payment. A state congressman (PT) also took part in the protest.

On 7 January 2024, in Vilhena (Rondonia), an armed individual opened fire against a group of people on the BR-364 highway. One man was killed and four people were injured. The perpetrator fled with an accomplice on a motorcycle. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 10 January 2024, in Boa Vista (Roraima), business workers, students and teachers occupied a bus terminal to protest against increase in bus fares and to demand better public transportation services.

On 12 January 2024, in Boa Vista (Roraima), people carried out a protest to demand the end of gender-based violence, after a Venezuelan female traveling circus artist was raped and then was found dead with signs of violence during a road trip.
On 10 January 2024, in Normandia (Roraima), two members of a drug trafficking group stabbed and injured a member of a rival group. Both perpetrators were arrested. There were no fatalities.

On 6 January 2024, in Ituporanga (Santa Catarina), two armed individuals invaded a private residence in the Vila Nova neighborhood, shot and killed a man. A precision scale and evidence of drug consumption were found in the residence, but it is not known if the victim was a drug trafficker or drug user. 1 fatality.

actor1: Military Forces of Brazil (2023-) Military Police
notes: On 7 January 2024, in Lages (Santa Catarina), a drug trafficking suspect exchanged fire with military police in the Centenario neighborhood. The drug trafficker was killed. A second suspect was arrested. Police seized portions of drugs. 1 fatality.

On 7 January 2024, in Navegantes (Santa Catarina), two armed individuals invaded a private residence in the Sao Domingos neighborhood, shot and killed three men. Authorship and motivation of the attack are unknown. 3 fatalities.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), in Itapecerica da Serra (Sao Paulo), four men kidnapped another man, tied him up, beat him, locked him in the car's trunk and took him to the Guarapiranga dam to be killed in a 'crime tribunal'. The reason for his execution was that he had killed a man because of his jealousy towards a woman. However, before they were going to kill him, the suspects received a call telling them not to kill the victim, since his godfather is part of a drug trafficking group. Right after, the police was called and rescued the victim, who was then arrested for homicide. There were no fatalities.

Around 9 January 2024 (as reported), in Sao Paulo - South Zone (Sao Paulo), a woman was killed and her dismembered body was found inside a dumpster in front of the Capelinha terminal. Motivation and perpetrators unknown. 1 fatality.

Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), in Sao Paulo - West Zone (Sao Paulo), local residents set fire to tires and trash to demonstrate against the ongoing construction work in the region which is preventing the proper water drainage and causing floods.
On 12 January 2024, in Barrolandia (Tocantins), four armed individuals blew up a bank. There were no fatalities.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Amazonas state (coded to Puerto Ayacucho), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Anzoategui state (coded to Barcelona), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Aragua state (coded to Maracay), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

On 9 January 2024, in Maracay (Aragua), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired teachers, pensioners, and representatives of the Civil Association of Retired Teachers and Pensioners, protested to demand better pay, pensions, and living conditions, the renewal of their collective labor agreement, better medical attention, and payment of owed pensions for 20,000 pensioners living abroad that had these payments terminated in 2015. They gathered in the Bicentenaria Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.
On 11 January 2024, in Maracay (Aragua), a group of teachers of the Joaquin Avellaneda School protested to demand additional security in the area after several thefts.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Barinas state (coded to Barinas), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

On 9 January 2024, in Barinas (Barinas), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired teachers, other university workers, and unionists of the Los Llanos Ezequiel Zamora University (UNELLEZ), protested to demand better pay and working conditions, the repeal of the National Budget Office (ONAPRE) instruction, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They gathered in Punto Fresco roundabout. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 10 January 2024, in Barinas state (coded to Barinas), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, land, social security, and water.

On 6 January 2024, in Ciudad Guayana (Bolivar), four unidentified crime group suspects abducted a merchant, and member of the VP political party, in the vicinity of the Atlantico Avenue. Due to the heightened pressure of the security forces, the man was liberated by his captors the day after in the vicinity of the Sidor overpass. The identity or motivations of the kidnappers is unknown.

source: Diario Primicia; El Diario de Guayana
notes: On 9 January 2024, in Ciudad Guayana (Bolivar), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired health workers, teachers, and workers at companies belonging to the state-owned Venezuelan Corporation of Guayana (CVG), and representatives of the Bolivar state School of Nursing Professionals, the School of Teaching Professionals, the ITG workers' union, and the National Front of the Working Class Struggle (FNLCT), protested to demand better pay and working conditions, the renewal of their collective labor agreements, improvements in the medical infrastructure, trade union autonomy, the restoration of their medical insurance and burial plans (HCM), and the liberation of all the unionists imprisoned while defending their labor rights. They gathered in the CVG Monument Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 10 January 2024, in Ciudad Guayana (Bolivar), a group of retired workers at companies belonging to the state-owned Venezuelan Corporation of Guayana (CVG), pensioners, and representatives of the Bolivar state Federation of Associations of Retired Workers and Pensioners (FEDAJUPEBOL), and the CVG Carbonorca workers' union, gathered outside the CVG headquarters to demand payment of owed stipends, observance of their collective labor agreements, the restoration of their medical insurance and burial plans (HCM), and better medical attention.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Bolivar state (coded to Ciudad Bolivar), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

On 9 January 2024, in Ciudad Bolivar (Bolivar), a group of demonstrators, including state workers, and supporters of the Popular Will (VP) political party, marched in protest to demand better pay and working conditions. They marched from the Public Ministry Office, where they handed in a letter with their demands, to the Ombudsman Office. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 6 January 2024, in Mariara (Carabobo), suspected members of the Los Guaricheros Gang shot and killed a man, in an attack occurred in the morning. The killing is thought to be a settling of scores. There is no further information at this point in time. 1 fatality.

On 10 January 2024, in El Charal (Carabobo), CICPC police forces shot and killed the leader of the El Culita Gang during an armed clash. The suspect resisted authority, initiated the shooting, was injured in the gunfire, and pronounced dead in hospital. He was linked to homicides, extortion threats, and thefts. Police collected a gun. 1 fatality.

On 8 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired teachers, and representatives of the Carabobo state SINVEMA-FVM teachers' union, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, improvements in the educational infrastructure, and the restoration of their medical insurance and burial plans (HCM). They gathered in the Bolivar Avenue and then marched to the Education Secretary Office, where they handed in a letter with their demands.

On 8 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), a group workers at the state-owned Cardboard Corporation of Venezuela (CARTOVEN) protested to demand payment of owed stipends corresponding to three vacation bonuses, Christmas Basket handouts, food tickets, and a week of pay. They gathered outside the company's premises in the Sur II Municipal Industrial Zone.

On 9 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), for the second consecutive day, a group of demonstrators, including active and retired state workers, teachers, representatives of the SITRA-Ensenanza and the SUMA Carabobo teachers' unions, the National Front of the Working Class Struggle (FNLCT), and the CUTEC and the FETRACARABOBO workers' unions, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, the renewal of their collective labor agreements, and the repeal of the National Budget Office (ONAPRE) instruction. They gathered in the La Candelaria parish, and then marched to the intersection of the Lara and the Las Ferias avenues. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), a group of licensed merchants of the Los Guajiros Market protested to demand the relocation of the informal sellers that work in the area. According to them, these informal sellers block the access of customers to their shops, affecting the livelihoods of 3,000 merchants. They gathered outside the Valencia townhall, where they handed in a letter with their demands.

On 10 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), a group of residents of the El Trigal Sur neighborhood protested over water outages going on for at least six weeks, and to demand action from the Hidrocentro water company. They gathered in the vicinity of the residence of the Carabobo state governor.

On 11 January 2024, in Valencia (Carabobo), Carabobo state police forces shot and killed an unidentified crime group suspect, during an armed clash in the early hours in the Bicentenario sector, Negro Primero neighborhood. The deceased was thought to be one of the perpetrators of the theft-related killing of a public prosecutor in August 2023. Police collected a gun. 1 fatality.

On 9 January 2024, in San Carlos (Cojedes), a group of demonstrators, including teachers and health workers, marched in protest to demand better pay and working conditions. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Tucupita (Delta Amacuro), a group of teachers, summoned by the Delta Amacuro state School of Teaching Professionals, protested to demand better pay, pensions and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Caracas - Libertador (Distrito Capital), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

source: Efecto Cocuyo; El Pitazo; La Patilla
notes: On 9 January 2024, in Caracas - Libertador (Distrito Capital), hundreds of demonstrators, including active and retired state workers, pensioners, school teachers, health workers, Caracas Underground Tube workers, representatives of the Distrito Capital School of Nursing Professionals, the SITRA-Ensenanza teachers' union, and the Federation of Retired Education Sector Workers and Pensioners, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They gathered outside the Public Ministry Office, by Carabobo Park. Elsewhere, a group of active and retired workers at the Central University of Venezuela (UCV), pensioners, and representatives of the National Union Coalition, protested with similar demands by the gates of the Tamanaco entrance to the UCV campus, and then marched to Venezuela Square. These were two of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Punto Fijo (Falcon), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired state workers, school and Zulia University teachers, oil sector workers, health workers, representatives of the Falcon state SITRA-Ensenanza teachers' union, the Retired Oil Sector Workers' Association (AJIP), and other unionists, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and payment of owed stipends. They gathered in the Jose Leonardo Chirinos Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 8 January 2024, in Falcon state (coded to Coro), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to labor, justice, education, the environment, and healthcare.

On 9 January 2024, in Coro (Falcon), a group of demonstrators, including teachers, other active and retired state workers, pensioners, and representatives of the Intergremial trade union coalition, protested to demand better pay and working conditions. They gathered in the Sucre Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 12 January 2024, in Falcon state (coded to Coro), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to housing, labor, healthcare, and education.

On 8 January 2024, in San Juan de los Morros (Guarico), a group of demonstrators, including teachers, and representatives of the Teachers' Union Coalition, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They marched to the Education Zone Office.

On 9 January 2024, in San Juan de los Morros (Guarico), dozens of demonstrators, including active state workers, and representatives of the School of Nursing Professionals, protested to demand better pay, pensions, and working conditions, the renewal of their collective labor agreements, and improvements in healthcare. They gathered in the Los Samanes Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 12 January 2024, in Guarico state (coded to San Juan de los Morros), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to housing, labor, healthcare, and education.

On 9 January 2024, in Valle de La Pascua (Guarico), a group of demonstrators, including teachers and representatives of the Guarico state School of Teaching Professionals, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They gathered in the vicinity of the Francisco Lazo Marti School. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Maiquetia (La Guaira), a group of teachers protested to demand better pay and working conditions. They gathered in the Los Banos Street. Police forces were present but there was no intervention. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 7 January 2024, in Barquisimeto (Lara), a group of residents of the Brisas del Sur II neighborhood protested over water supply service problems going on for at least seven years, and to demand solutions from the Hidrolara water company.

On 8 January 2024, in Barquisimeto (Lara), a group of demonstrators, including active and retired teachers, pensioners, and representatives of the FENATEV teachers' union, protested outside the seat of the Lara state government to demand better pay, pensions, and working conditions, the repeal of the National Budget Office (ONAPRE) instruction, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement, and against changes to their pay slips carried out by the administration, which have led hundreds of teachers to lose certain benefits and pay. Elsewhere, another group of teachers and unionists protested outside the Education Zone Office with similar demands.

source: El Impulso; Radio Fe y Alegria
notes: On 9 January 2024, in Barquisimeto (Lara), for the second consecutive day, a group of demonstrators, including school and university teachers, health workers, Corpoelec power company employees and unionists, representatives of the Lara state Committee for Retired Workers and Pensioners, and the IVSS Health Workers' Union, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreements. They marched from the Altagracia Square to the Ombudsman Office. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social

Around 11 January 2024 (as reported), in Barquisimeto (Lara), a group of miners from the Gamelotal sector, Simon Planas municipality, and members of the UBCH organization (PSUV), protested outside the regional office of the National Land Institute (INTI) to demand limestone mining permits. They argue that the revocation of their previous licenses has led to the unemployment of at least 40 people. The protesters managed to meet with INTI officials.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social

notes: On 10 January 2024, in Carora (Lara), a group of residents of the Francisco Torres neighborhood, and relatives of an adolescent boy that had been shot and killed by a Lara state police officer the day before, in what may have been an accidental killing after the officer fired warning shots (coded separately), set fire to a Lara state police outpost in retaliation for the killing. There were no casualties.

On 8 January 2024, in Merida (Merida), a group of demonstrators, including teachers, other university workers, and unionists of the Los Andes University (ULA), protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They gathered in the Don Tulio Avenue, by the ULA administrative office.

On 9 January 2024, in Merida (Merida), a group of demonstrators, including elementary and high school teachers belonging to public and private schools, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, the repeal of the National Budget Office (ONAPRE) instruction, and the renewal of their collective labor agreements. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.
