On 8 January 2024, in Los Teques (Miranda), a group of teachers blocked the La Hoyada Avenue in protest to demand better pay and working conditions. Traffic remained blocked for over two hours.

On 9 January 2024, in Los Teques (Miranda), for the second consecutive day, a group of hundreds of demonstrators, including teachers and health workers protested to demand better pay and working conditions. They marched down the Bermudez Avenue to the Bolivar Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 4 January 2024, near Ocumare del Tuy village (Miranda), suspected members of the El Danielito and the Los Pampers gangs shot and killed a man, who they accused of being a police informant, in an attack in the vicinity of the Ocumarito dam. The victim was intercepted by the suspects in the Villa Florida sector, just outside his father's house, who he was visiting. They took his phone, examined it, and found several phone numbers belonging to police officers saved in it. They then forced the victim to go with them to a place close to the Ocumarito dam, where they shot him at least 10 times. Two suspects, an adolescent boy and a security guard of the nearby Simon Bolivar Hospital, were arrested shortly after. There is no further information at this point in time. 1 fatality.

On 9 January 2024, in Ocumare del Tuy (Miranda), a group of teachers marched in protest to demand better pay and working conditions. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

source: El Diario (Venezuela); El Oriental de Monagas; La Patilla
notes: On 9 January 2024, in Maturin (Monagas), a group of demonstrators, including teachers, other education sector workers, and representatives of the SERFETRASINED education sector workers' union, protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. They gathered in the El Estudiante Square and then marched to the Education Zone Office. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

They marched down the San Roque Boulevard and the Libertador Avenue to the Bolivar Square. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Araya (Sucre), a group of demonstrators, including teachers and other state workers protested to demand better pay and working conditions. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in Irapa (Sucre), a group of demonstrators, including teachers and other state workers protested to demand better pay and working conditions. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 9 January 2024, in San Felipe (Yaracuy), a group of demonstrators, including teachers and other state workers, protested to demand better pay and working conditions. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 10 January 2024, in Machiques (Zulia), a small group of teachers protested to demand better pay and working conditions, and against the low turnout that their protest had. They gathered in the Corazon de Jesus Square.

notes: On 9 January 2024, in Maracaibo (Zulia), dozens of demonstrators, including active and retired state workers, pensioners, teachers, and representatives of the Zulia state School of Nursing Professionals, the Zulia state School of Teaching Professionals, and the Zulia state Union of Retired Workers and Pensioners, protested to demand better pay, pensions, and working conditions. They gathered by the Nuestra Senora de Chiquinquira Basilica. This was one of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day.

On 11 January 2024, in Maracaibo (Zulia), a group of demonstrators, including relatives and colleagues of a recycling worker that died in police custody after not receiving medical attention following a heart attack in a San Francisco municipality police station, protested to demand justice for him. The protesters argue that the police officers were extorting him, causing a lot of distress to the detainee, which provoked his heart attack. They accuse the San Francisco municipality police of malpractice. The demonstrators gathered outside the Public Ministry Office.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 12 January 2024, in Zulia state (coded to Maracaibo), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to housing, labor, healthcare, and education.

On 8 January 2024, in Porto Alegre (Rio Grande do Sul), dozens of Bolsonaro supporters demonstrated in Matriz Square calling for freedom for prisoners detained for their involvement in the invasion and vandalism of public buildings by Bolsonaro (PL) supporters on 8 January 2023 (coded separately).

On 8 January 2024, protesters including unionists from the Union of External Transportation held a sit-in in outside the Tripoli port in El Mina (Tripoli), to protest against the fees and ban on unregistered motorcycles.
On 9 January 2024, the group of rented building owners held a sit-in at Riad Solh square in Beirut - Port (Beirut), in protest against the commercial rentals law and to demand its cancellation.

On 10 January 2024, protesters took to the streets of Al Zarqa Al Jadida (Az Zarqa) to demonstrate, in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 10 January 2024, protesters took to the streets of Irbid (Qasabat Irbid) to demonstrate, in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 12 January 2024, hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Aqaba (Qasabat al Aqabah) to demonstrate. Nationwide demonstrations erupted in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West Bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 12 January 2024, hundreds of protesters took to the streets outside Al Hashimi Mosque and marched towards the main square in Irbid (Qasabat Irbid) to demonstrate. Nationwide demonstrations erupted in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West Bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 12 January 2024, protesters took to the streets outside the Grand Mosque in Maan (Qasabat Maan) to demonstrate. Nationwide demonstrations erupted in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West Bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 12 January 2024, Palestinian refugee protesters took to the streets of Madaba refugee camp towards its main square (Qasabat Madaba) to demonstrate. Nationwide demonstrations erupted in support of the Palestinian people in Gaza and the West Bank, and to denounce Israeli aggression towards them.

On 3 January 2024, Fano ambushed ENDF at Dilb (location coded to Gashena, Wadla, North Wello Amhara). ENDF was traveling with three military trucks from Weldiya to Bahirdar. An unspecified number of fatalities coded as 3.

source: Deutsche Welle; EMS; ESAT; TIKVAH Ethiopia
notes: Security measures: On 4 January 2024, Debre Berhan and its surrounding State of Emergency Command Post (coded as the Government of Ethiopia) banned any movement of three-wheeled vehicles (commonly referred to as Bajaj) for an indefinite time in Debre Birhan (Debre Berhan town, North Shewa, Amhara) due to insecurity following Fano attack in the town (coded separately). The banning is effective from 5 January 2024.

On 7 January 2024, police arrested five Tritiya Sammelan Prastuti Committee (TSPC) cadres, including a sub-zonal commander, from the Tandwa Police Station area (Chatra district, Jharkhand). Police seized a rifle, two pistols, 14 live cartridges, a knife, and pamphlets.

On 9 January 2024, two persons attacked and killed a 55-year-old man with a blade in Tandapali village (Malkangiri district, Odisha) over suspicion of practicing sorcery. Police had arrested both the accused.

On 7 January 2024, unidentified persons shot and killed a BJP district vice-president in Pakhanjore town (Kanker district, Chhattisgarh) over political rivalry for the post of president of Pakhanjur Nagar Panchayat.

On 9 January 2024, an armed squad of CPI-Maoist rebels shot and killed a former Naxal-Maoist rebel near his house in the Jaraikela Thana area (West Singhbhum district, Jharkhand) over suspicion of being a police informer.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), unidentified assailants attacked two Dalit medical workers associated with the Makkalai Thedi Maruthuvam (Medicine in search of people) in Udappanpatti locality in Kottampatti village (Madurai, Tamil Nadu) over their caste identity. The victims were distributing tablets to the residents of the village when they were attacked.

On 5 January 2024, a Sarpanch (village chief) and 3 policemen (including a Station House Officer) were suspected to have killed a Gram Sachiv (village secretary) while under custody - by pushing him off a building - at Arya Nagar locality in Bahal village (Bhiwani, Haryana) in a suspected extortion attempt. The Gram Sachiv was earlier booked at the direction of a BJP state minister for failing to present complete Panchayat records.

On 6 January 2024, a police officer assaulted two men at Sakhuwagachi Chowk in Inaruwa municipality-3 (Sunsari district) for refusing to provide money (likely a bribe given in the context) during a vehicle inspection. One of the victims sustained injuries and is currently undergoing treatment in Dharan City.
On 6 January 2024, unidentified armed assailants opened fire at an INC leader at a farm near Dhanaula city (Barnala, Punjab) for unidentified reasons. The INC leader was hospitalized.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), unidentified car-borne assailants (on the orders of the village chief, based on the victim's claim) intercepted a motorcycle-borne village Panch (member of the local government) and assaulted him with iron rods, and threatened him with a gun, along the Delhi-Jaipur Highway near Salhawas village (Rewari, Haryana) in a suspected attempt to pressurize the village panch to sign a proposal document. The village panch suffered a broken arm and a leg.

On 1 January 2024, police officers detained and assaulted a TDP supporter in Palthur village (Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh) after the TDP supporter was called to the police station based on a complaint by members of the YSRCP over an incident where the YSRC party flag was burned in the village. The TDP suffered a rib-bone fracture due to the police assault.

On 7 January 2024, unidentified assailants attacked and killed an ex-Maoist member and the brother of an MLC and YRCP member near the RS Pendakal railway station in Pendekallu village in Tuggali Mandal (Kurnool, Andhra Pradesh) for unknown reasons. The assailants killed the victim by attacking him with stones near the railway station and fled.

Defusal: On 11 January 2024, a joint operation team comprising Police, Commando Battalion for Resolute Action (CoBRA), Jharkhand Jaguar, and Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) recovered an IED weighing 7 kilograms planted in the forest and hilly areas around the Meralgada village under the Goilkera Police Station area (West Singhbhum district, Jharkhand). The IED was suspected to be planted by the Naxal-Maoist cadres. BDS personnel subsequently defused the IED.

Defusal: On 10 January 2024, security forces (coded as police) recovered an IED weighing 5 kilograms planted in a forest between Hesabandh and Jojohatu villages under Muffasil police station (West Singhbhum district, Jharkhand). The IED was suspected to be planted by the Naxal-Maoist cadres. BDS personnel subsequently defused the IED.

actor1: Police Forces of India (2014-) Bomb Disposal Squad
notes: Defusal: On 5 January 2024, BDS defused a Chinese RPG suspectedly misfired during a training exercise at Rangat town (North and Middle Andaman district, A&NI).

Property destruction: On 6 January 2024, Naxal-Maoist cadres fired at least seven rounds in the air and targeted a construction site by setting ablaze a Poclain, JCB, and mixer machines at Bariatu in Chandwa block (Latehar district, Jharkhand).

Property destruction: On 9 January 2024, Naxal-Maoist cadres set ablaze several vehicles, including dumpers and trucks, parked near the Serangdag bauxite mine at the Ghaghra community development (CD) block (Gumla district, Jharkhand) over demand for 'levy' (extortion money).

Property destruction: On 7 January 2024, unidentified persons damaged four idols (Hindu) at a temple in Ranchi city (Jharkhand) for unknown reasons. Protests erupted in the city against the incident (coded separately).
Property destruction: On 7 January 2024, unidentified assailants vandalized a statue of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar (a Dalit champion) in Nihura village (Kanpur Nagar, Uttar Pradesh) for unidentified reasons.

On 9 January 2024, four assailants assaulted two youngsters at a KSRTC bus stand in Mudigere town (Chikmagalur, Karnataka) for talking to a Hindu girl. The assailants arrived in a car and attacked the two youths.

On 5 January 2024, unidentified persons assaulted the enforcement directorate officials during a raid at Bongaon town (North 24 Parganas district, WB) after they arrested a former TMC local body chairman.

On 11 January 2024, two groups clashed at Teesta Payasti village in Mekliganj police station area (Cooch Behar district, WB) over cattle trampling potato fields. One woman was killed and three others were left injured in the clash.

On 6 January 2024, five persons assaulted two on-duty Indore Municipal Corporation employees with tillers and hammers in the Malharganj police station area of Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) over cutting a tree illegally.
On 1 January 2024, three persons assaulted and paraded a man naked in the market in Basni town (Nagaur, Rajasthan) on suspicion of theft. Police arrested the three suspects.

On 6 January 2024, retrenched employees gave death threats to the employer, assaulted a person, and opened fire outside the Dollex sugar mill in Erai village in Barauni area (Datia, Madhya Pradesh) after some of them were fired from the sugar mill. They fled the scene afterward.

On 10 January 2024, Smarak Samiti (Memorial committee) workers, including an employee union leader, and administrative officers clashed with each other at Gomti Nagar area's Ambedkar Park in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) over a dispute regarding the hiring of contractual workers to carry out work - which the employees claimed they could themselves perform. The police intervened and detained some employees and the union leader.

On 8 January 2024, some members of the Muslim community threw stones, and attacked with swords Akshat Kalash Yatra, a religious procession of the Hindu community, organized ahead of the consecration ceremony of the Ram Temple, when it passed through a Muslim-dominated area in Shajapur city (Madhya Pradesh). One person was seriously injured while two others sustained minor injuries. Eight persons have been arrested in this incident. This incident led to a demonstration (coded separately).

On 5 January 2024, a mob of 30 to 40 people assaulted a police team in Dasada taluka under Zinzuvwada police station (Surendranagar district, Gujarat) against the detention of a man wanted in a prohibition case in the district. Two policemen, including a sub-inspector and a constable, were injured.

On 4 January 2024, local fishers and the labourers employed by the Honnavar Port Private Limited (HPPL) scuffled with each other in the Kasarkod Tonka area in Honavar town (Uttara Kannada, Karnataka) when the labourers were conducting a survey over the continuation of work on an asphalt road near the turtle nesting site. The local fishers opposed the work since it could endanger the nesting site of the Olive Ridley sea turtle and the labourers threatened the women of the fishing community and asked them vacate their homes, leading to a clash between the two groups.

On 6 January 2024, locals (likely from the Hindu community based on location) held a protest at Chabba Demote town (Ramban district, J&K) against the claimed desecration of a religious site by unidentified persons.

On 10 January 2024, private school owners, drivers, students and their guardians held a joint protest at Jammu city (J&K) against the J&K board of school education over the move of tagging students of the private schools.

On 10 January 2024, a large number of INC members staged a protest, carrying placards and raising slogans, in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) against the suspected involvement of local BJP leaders in the gangrape of a female student from Banaras Hindu University (BHU) on 1 November 2023 and claimed that the BJP central government was shielding the accused.

Around 12 January 2024 (as reported), members of the ABVP staged protests at the Central University of Kerala (CUK) in Kasaragod city (Kerala) demanding the suspension of a professor who was accused of taking a bribe from a guest lecturer.

On 5 January 2024, hundreds of Adivasis staged a protest at the weekly market in Araku town (Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh) against the YRSCP for appointing a outsider as the Araku constituency in-charge by replacing the sitting MLA. The protesters said that the new appointee was an outsider married to a non-tribal and that she never visited the region during her time as the Araku MP.

On 3 January 2024, farmers and members of AIKS staged a protest at the Jewar toll plaza along the Yamuna Expressway on the outskirts of Alawalpur village (Gautam Buddha Nagar, Uttar Pradesh) demanding the reformation of the state government's land acquisition and displacement policy in the district. The police prevented the protesters from freeing the toll.
On 3 January 2024, locals held a protest at Dhupguri city (Jalpaiguri district, WB) to demand that Dhupguri be declared as a new subdivision.

On 6 January 2024, BJP members held a protest as part of a state-wide campaign in Hooghly district (coded as Chinsurah city (Hooghly district, WB)) against the attacks on enforcement directorate teams in Bongaon and Sandeshkhali (coded separately).

On 6 January 2024, BJP members held protests as part of a state-wide campaign at Purulia district (WB) against the attacks on enforcement directorate teams in Bongaon and Sandeshkhali (coded separately).

On 6 January 2024, BJP members held protests as part of a state-wide campaign at Bankura district (WB) against the attacks on enforcement directorate teams in Bongaon and Sandeshkhali (coded separately).

On 7 January 2024, BJP members held a bike rally to protest in Hooghly district (coded as Chinsurah city (Hooghly district, WB)) against the attacks on enforcement directorate teams in Sandeshkhali (coded separately). Police blocked their path.

On 5 January 2024, members of the BJP staged protests across Karnataka state (coded to Bengaluru) against the arrest of a Hindutva member in connection with a 1992 criminal case. The protesters accused the INC-led state government of targeting members of Hindutva groups before the inauguration of Ram Temple in Ayodhya

On 9 January 2024, members of the BJP and the BJKM staged a protest and took out a march to the sub-collector's office in Markapur town (Prakasam, Andhra Pradesh) demanding the YSRCP-led state government to name the Veligonda canal project after the person who fought for the project and also demanded a compensation package for all those who were displaced while constructing the water body.

On 3 January 2024, members of the BJP including an MLA and former Union ministers staged a protest in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) against the INC-led state government for arresting a Hindutva member in Hubballi for his involvement in the riots following the Babri Masjid (mosque) demolition in 1992. The protesters accused the INC-led state government of targeting members of Hindutva groups before the inauguration of Ram Temple in Ayodhya.
On 7 January 2024, locals held a protest at Satialta village (Udhampur district, J&K) over a road that is heavily used by locals being closed for construction.

On 9 January 2024, BJP MLAs including the Leader of the Opposition and former Chief Ministers staged a protest at the Raj Bhavan in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) against the INC-led state government for the delay in the payment of drought relief amounts to farmers. The protesters said that over 600 farmers have died by suicide due to financial issues and submitted a petition to the Governor seeking his intervention in the matter.

On 6 January 2024, locals, including relatives of the deceased, staged a sit-in protest outside the court in Pokhara city (Kaski district). The protesters were demanding justice for two women who lost their lives in a scooter-car collision, voicing concerns regarding political intervention in the legal proceedings.
On 7 January 2024, youths staged a protest at Lagankhel locality in Lalitpur city (Lalitpur district), demanding the safe rescue of a youth who was held hostage by Hamas.

On 6 January 2024, locals staged a protest in Barahathawa municipality (Sarlahi district) following the death of a youth in a police firing during a demonstration (coded as a separate event) in Barahathawa municipality.

On 6 January 2024, locals staged a protest at Salempur town in Chakraghatta municipality (Sarlahi district), following the death of a youth in a police firing during a demonstration (coded as a separate event) in Barahathawa municipality.

On 6 January 2024, locals staged a protest in Kabilasi municipality (Sarlahi district), following the death of a youth in a police firing during a demonstration (coded as a separate event) in Barahathawa municipality.

On 7 January 2024, locals, including relatives of a deceased man, staged a protest at the Gulariya-Rajapur road section in Khairi Chandanpur village (Bardiya district), following the death of a cyclist after being hit by a van in an accident. The protesters were demanding action against the culprit and compensation following the death of the man.

On 6 January 2024, farmers under the banner of BKU staged protests at various BKU District headquarters in the western region of the Uttar Pradesh state (coded as Lucknow city) over numerous demands including prevention of stray animal menace, payment of dues from the last year's sugarcane procurement, declaration of sugarcane price (with a hike), and free electricity for irrigation.

On 9 January 2024, several farmers gathered under the banner of BKU (Ugrahan) and staged a dharna (sit-in protest) in front of the Sub-Divisional Magistrate's (SDM) office in Moonak town (Sangrur, Punjab) demanding the release of compensation to farmers for losses suffered due to crop failure - caused by the floods.

On 7 January 2024, the Chure (hill range) and Forest Conservation Campaign staged a protest march in Janakpur town (Dhanusha district). The protesters were making various demands, such as the ban on unauthorized river excavation, the construction of dams for water recharge in forest areas, and the enactment of federal laws to curb the export of river materials abroad.
On 9 January 2024, locals staged a protest rally in Gulariya municipality (Bardiya district), demanding the reconstruction of the damaged Jabdighat bridge over the Babai River.

On 7 January 2024, a group of Christian youngsters staged a protest at the St. Lourdes Church in Bommidi town (Dharmapuri, Tamil Nadu) when a BJP leader and former MLA along with his supporters attempted to enter the church to garland the statue of Mary during his ' En Mann En Makkal' (My Soil, My People) rally. The protesters objected to the BJP leader's entry into the church and raised slogans to mark their protest.
On 11 January 2024, 200-250 locals staged a protest in Sunawarshi municipality (Morang district), following the death of a boy after being hit by a tractor in an accident.

On 10 January 2024, employees of the Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) along with members of the CITU and the Anna Thozhir Sangam (Anna Labor Union) launched an indefinite protest in Chennai city (Tamil Nadu) as part of a state-wide strike by the Tamil Nadu State Transport Workers' Unions demanding the revival of the old pension scheme, increased wages, the filling up of vacancies and the allocation of a fund to meet the losses incurred by the transport corporation.

On 9 January 2024, victims of microfinance institutions staged a protest rally in Janakpur city (Dhanusha district) against the Governor of Nepal Rastra Bank (national bank). The protesters were also demanding the abolition of microfinance institutions, debt forgiveness, a pro-people monetary policy, and compensation for victims of financial exploitation.

On 12 January 2024, depositors of the Sano Paila Savings and Credit Cooperative Organization staged a protest in Birganj city (Parsa district). The protesters were demanding action against the cooperative owner, accused of embezzling a substantial sum of money.

On 8 January 2024, the Chure (hill range) and Forest Conservation Campaign staged a protest march in Jaleswar municipality (Mahottari district). The protesters were making demands, such as ending the destruction of the Chure Hills, providing land to landless squatters, and protecting the Sirsiya River.

On 9 January 2024, the Chure (hill range) and Forest Conservation Campaign staged a protest march in the Malangwa area (Sarlahi district). The protesters were making demands, such as ending the destruction of the Chure Hills, providing land to landless squatters, and protecting the Sirsiya River.

On 8 January 2024, members of the CPI staged a protest in front of the Head Post Office in Ramanathapuram city (Tamil Nadu) against the BJP-led Union government for not providing relief funds to the state of Tamil Nadu in the aftermath of Cyclone Michaung and the subsequent floods. The protesters said that people had suffered huge damage and the loss of properties and need the government's assistance to recover from their losses.

On 8 January 2024, members of the CPI(M) including a Councillor and a former MLA, along with fishers and the family of the fishing community staged a protest at the Coromandel International plant in the Ennore locality in Chennai city (Tamil Nadu) demanding the closure of the private fertilizer-manufacturing plant after ammonia gas leaked from a pipeline at the plant. The protesters demanded the relocation of the plant from their locality.

On 9 January 2024, members of the CPI(M) and the SFI took out a protest march and staged black flag protests in Thodupuzha town (Idukki, Kerala) against the Kerala Governor for delaying his assent to the Kerala Land Assignment (Amendment) Bill which was passed by the state assembly last year. The Kerala Governor was visiting the district to inaugurate the Karunavam Family Security Scheme of the Vyapyari Vyavasayi Ekopana Samithi (organisation of traders). The protesters raised slogans like 'Rogue Khan' go back to mark their protest.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), members of CPN(M)-NBC staged a protest in the Kathmandu area (Kathmandu district). The protesters were calling for an end to irregularities within the Securities Board of Nepal, highlighting concerns about political corruption, and urging transparent appointments for the Board Chairman.

On 8 January 2024, a group of people's representatives, including ward chairpersons elected from CPN(US), CPN(UML), and NC, led by the deputy mayor elected from NC, protested by padlocking the municipal office in Katahariya municipality (Rautahat district). The protesters made various demands, such as payment of salaries for all employees, including doormen, contractors, and cleaners working in the municipality, and expressed dissatisfaction with financial irregularities and corruption by the mayor.
On 7 January 2024, scores of local people staged a protest in Nihura village (Kanpur Nagar, Uttar Pradesh) against the vandalization of a statue of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar (a Dalit champion).

On 9 January 2024, leaders of the Bharatiya Parivartana Sangha (Indian Transformation Society) staged a protest in front of the taluk office in Tarikere town (Chikmagalur, Karnataka) against the practice of untouchability in the village of Gollarahatti after a Dalit youth was assaulted for entering a locality in the village (coded separately). The protesters demanded the administration to ensure that Dalits could enter and pray at the Kambada Ranganatha Swamy temple in the village.

On 3 January 2024, the members of 28 families, mostly Dalits, along with a member of the YSRC and former Mandal Parishad Territorial Constituency (MPTC) member launched a 10-day protest in Bhimavaram village (West Godavari, Andhra Pradesh) against the proposal to constrict health centres on the land they had cultivated on for many years.

On 12 January 2024, the members of 28 families, mostly Dalits, along with a member of the YSRC and former Mandal Parishad Territorial Constituency (MPTC) member staged a protest for the 10-day in Bhimavaram village (West Godavari, Andhra Pradesh) against the proposal to constrict health centres on the land they had cultivated on for many years.

On 8 January 2024, farmers under the aegis of the Backward Classes Welfare Union staged a protest in Srikakulam city (Andhra Pradesh) demanding the YSRCP-led state government to complete the temporary repair work that was being carried out on the Pydigam irrigation project. The protesters said that they have not been able to get water from the water body to irrigate their crops.

On 7 January 2024, hundreds of victims of meter-interest business, led by the Farmers' Labor Struggle Committee, staged a protest in Janakpur city (Dhanusha district). The demonstrators put forth several demands, such as the dismissal of cases against victims of usury, investigations into acts of violence committed by usurers, compensation for victims of meter-interest businesses, and legal actions against the usurers.

On 7 January 2024, industrialists, including members of FNCCI, staged a sit-in protest in front of the distribution center of the Nepal Electricity Authority in Bhairahawa town (Rupandehi district). The protesters were demanding the reconnection of power lines to industries whose connections had been severed while accusing the authority of attempting to impose unnecessary billing amounts.

On 7 January 2024, Anganwadi workers staged a protest in front of the Collectorate in Chittoor city (Andhra Pradesh) against the YSRCP-led state government for invoking the Essential Services Maintenance Act (ESMA) against Anganwadi workers who were protesting over a list of demands including an increase in wages.

On 9 January 2024, Anganwadi workers and the members of various trade unions including the CITU, the AITUC, and the AIFTU took out a protest march from the Ambedkar Statue to the Collectorate in Tirupati city (Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh) as part of the 'jail bharo' (fill the jails) protest programme and to demand the YSRCP-led state government to withdraw the Essential Services Maintenance Act (ESMA) invoked against the striking Anganwadi workers.

On 11 January 2024, a group of job seekers for teaching posts held a protest under the aegis of DEd BEd Teachers' Association and surrounded the state higher and technical education minister at the education commissioner's office in Pune city (Maharashtra) against the decision to prioritize English medium teachers over Marathi language DEd teachers.

source: Press Trust of India; Times of India
notes: On 10 January 2024, members of INC staged a state-wide protest in the Uttar Pradesh state (coded as Lucknow city) against the suspected involvement of local BJP leaders in the gangrape of a female student from Banaras Hindu University (BHU) on 1 November 2023 and claimed that the BJP central government was shielding the accused.

On 9 January 2024, members of the INC including a minister and a former MLA staged a protest in front of the Deputy Commissioner's office in Dharwad city (Karnataka) against a statement made by a Union Minister comparing the INC-led state government to the Islamic State (IS) and the Taliban. The protesters demanded the minister to withdraw his comments and apologise.

Around 3 January 2024 (week of), residents of Golf Links 1 staged a protest at Sector Omega 1 in Greater Noida city (Gautam Buddha Nagar, Uttar Pradesh) against the poor condition of the roads and sewerage system in the area.

On 5 January 2024, family members of the deceased staged a protest 'day 1' and refused to cremate the deceased's body at the General Hospital in Hisar city (Haryana) demanding action against the accused involved in the suicide of a man. The police registered charges against 6 accused - including a Deputy superintendent of police.

On 7 January 2024, family members of the deceased staged a protest and placed the deceased's body outside the office of the Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP) in Patti city (Tarn Taran, Punjab) demanding action against the accused involved in an indiscriminate shooting incident in the area, likely over a personal dispute, earlier in the day - which resulted in the death of a woman.

On 8 January 2024, social activists, led by the District chief of Lok-Raj Punjab (social outfit) staged a protest at Mahavir Chowk in Sangrur city (Punjab) demanding a ban on electronic voting machines (EVMs) which the protesters claimed posed a threat to free and fair elections.

On 9 January 2024, family members of the deceased and local people staged a protest, and placed the deceased's body, outside the Deputy Superintendent of Police's (DSP) office in Patti city (Tarn Taran, Punjab) demanding action against the accused who assaulted a man - over suspicions of stealing - on 29 November 2023 (coded separately). The man succumbed to his injuries on 7 January 2024.

On 10 January 2024, locals blocked the Patna-Ara stretch of the national highway for more than six hours in Phulwari town (Patna, Bihar) demanding action against the accused in the rape cum murder of a minor girl from Mahadalit (a subset of the Dalit caste group in Bihar) girl (coded separately).

On 11 January 2024, students (likely from Dalit and Maratha communities) held a protest in front of Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Research and Training Institute (BARTI) and Chhatrapati Shahu Maharaj Research, Training and Human Development Institute (SARTHI) in Pune city (Maharashtra) demanding the cancelation of the fellowship for SC and Maratha community screening exam over the paper leak.

On 7 January 2024, hundreds of Bajrang Dal members gathered in the Rajwada area in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) demanding strict action against the accused arrested for killing a Bajrang Dal member during prabhat pheri of Ranjeet Hanuman, action against drug suppliers and a review of night culture in the city.

On 12 January 2024, members of the BJP raised slogans and held a black flag protest in Bhawanipatna town (Kalahandi, Odisha) against the visit of newly inducted BJD leader and former 5T secretary for the 'Nabin Odisha' programme.

On 8 January 2024, as many as 42 sarpanches (head of the panchayat, village level local government body) and 56 samiti members staged a sit-in protest at the Block office of Baliapal town (Baleshwar district, Odisha) demanding the transfer of the BDO.

On 8 January 2024, members of the Hindu community held a protest outside the police in Shajapur city (Madhya Pradesh) demanding action against culprits involved in violence during a religious procession organized ahead of the consecration ceremony of the Ram Temple (coded separately).

On 9 January 2024, members of the INC city unit led by former corporator and Shiv Sena (UBT) staged a protest in front of the Municipal Corporation headquarters in Pune city (Maharashtra) against the civic body's decision to change of land reservation at Katraj Dairy.

On 5 January 2024, 10 people's representatives, including executive members and ward chairpersons elected from CPN(UML) and JSPN, led by the deputy mayor elected from NC, staged a sit-in protest at the municipal office in Garuda municipality (Rautahat district). The protesters were accusing the mayor of corruption, irregularities, and mismanagement of municipal funds.

On 7 January 2024, family members of the two murdered youth held a protest and blocked the intersection in Datia district (Madhya Pradesh) demanding immediate arrest of all the five accused involved in the murder. Police reached the spot and assured the family members of appropriate actions.

On 10 January 2024, family members of the deceased staged a chakka jam (road blockade) protest for 2.5 hours on Manikbagh Bridge in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) demanding action against the school management, compensation, job for one of the family members of the deceased, and education for daughters after a restaurant owner was fatally hit by a school bus.

On 7 January 2024, locals staged a protest and blocked a stretch of the Bariatu Road in Ranchi city (Jharkhand) demanding the immediate arrest of the accused involved in damaging idols at a city temple (coded separately).

On 11 January 2024, more than 500 villagers gathered and held a protest outside the Asind primary health center (Bhilwara, Rajasthan) demanding the arrest of the culprits involved in the murder of an 18-year-old girl, Rs 50 lakh compensation to the family, and a government job for the victim's family.

Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), Rajasthan civil services examination aspirants staged a protest at the Rajasthan University (RU) main gate in Jaipur city (Rajasthan), demanding that the main examination be postponed.

On 9 January 2024, protestors under the aegis of the drivers' union stopped the truck drivers at Atraliya Mandi intersection in Nasrullaganj town (Sehore district, Madhya Pradesh) as they relaunched their protest against proposed new penal provisions for hit-and-run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (seeks to repeal and replace the Indian Penal Code, 1870). The police reached the spot the protesters escaped from the spot.

On 6 January 2024, members of the Nationalist Congress Party (NCP--SP) led by a former mayor held a protest in Pune city (Maharashtra) demanding the resignation of a Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) MLA for slapping an on-duty police constable.
On 6 January 2024, service selection board job candidates held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) to demand restoration of the provision of preparing waiting lists for recruitment in government services.
On 10 January 2024, voluntary retired employees held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) over delay in payment of pending dues.

On 10 January 2024, LIC employees held a massive protest under the banner of Northern Zone Insurance Employees' Association amidst a 1 hour strike at Jammu city (J&K) to demand an increase in national pension scheme contribution by the corporation, increase in wages and recruitment in all cadres.
On 8 January 2024, service selection board job candidates held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) to demand restoration of the provision of preparing waiting lists for recruitment in government services.

On 10 January 2024, railway workers held a sit-in protest amidst a 4-day hunger strike at Jammu city (J&K) to demand restoration of the old pension scheme, resumption of dearness allowance and constitution of wage revision board as per schedule among other demands.

On 5 January 2024, members of the Joint Front of Engineers and Employees staged a brief protest 'day 2' (during lunch hours) outside most of the State Electricity Board offices in the Himachal Pradesh state (coded as Shimla city) demanding the payment of pending salaries and pensions, and the restoration of the old pension scheme.

On 5 January 2024, a large number of laborers under the banner of Mazdoor Mukti Morcha (MMM) staged a protest, raising slogans, along the Sangrur-Patiala Road near the Punjab Chief Minister's residence in Sangrur city (Punjab) over various demands including 700 rupees daily wage under MGNREGA, 200 days' work, disbursal of scholarship to Dalit students, and loan waiver to laborers, among others.

On 6 January 2024, members of the Joint Front of Engineers and Employees staged a brief protest 'day 3' (during lunch hours) outside most of the State Electricity Board offices in the Himachal Pradesh state (coded as Shimla city) demanding the payment of pending salaries and pensions, and the restoration of the old pension scheme.

On 7 January 2024, a large number of shopkeepers staged a protest outside the police station in Muktsar city (Sri Muktsar Sahib, Punjab) demanding the arrest of the accused who robbed a shopkeeper in the city.

On 8 January 2024, members of the Joint Front of Engineers and Employees staged a protest, raising slogans, outside the State Electricity Board headquarters in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) demanding the payment of pending salaries and pensions, and the restoration of the old pension scheme. The protesters also demanded the resignation of the Electricity Department's Managing Director for failing to address their issues.

On 10 January 2024, more than 2000 Smarak Samiti (Memorial committee) workers staged a protest outside the Gomti Nagar police station in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) demanding the release of workers, including a union leader, who were arrested during a clash earlier in the day at Ambedkar Park (coded separately).

source: Press Trust of India; Times of India
notes: On 11 January 2024, hundreds of employees, pensioners, and engineers gathered under the call of the Joint Front of Engineers and Employees and staged a protest 'day 1' (during lunch hours) outside the State Electricity Board headquarters in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) demanding the payment of pending salaries and pensions, and the restoration of the old pension scheme. The protesters also demanded the resignation of the Electricity Department's Managing Director for failing to address their issues.

On 9 January 2024, transport workers affiliated to various trade unions of the Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) launched an indefinite protest as part of a state-wide strike in front of the bus depots in Sathyamangalam town (Erode, Tamil Nadu) with a list of demands including the revival of the old pension scheme, the filling up of vacancies and the allocation of a fund to meet the losses incurred by the transport corporations.

On 8 January 2024, agents of PACL, a real-estate development company, staged a protest at the District Collectorate in Ramanathapuram city (Tamil Nadu) demanding an early refund of the investment they had made to company. The protesters said that the refund process was not initiated despite a Supreme Court order in 2016 and also said that many investors had died while waiting to get the refund to treat their health issues.

On 9 January 2024, vehicle drivers affiliated to the Clean Andhra Pradesh (CLAP) union along with members of the CITU initiative staged a protest in front of the municipal commissioner's office in Kakinada city (Andhra Pradesh) with a list of demands including increased wages, holidays, casual leaves, Provident Fund (PF) and Employees State Insurance Corporation (ESIC) benefits. The protesters raised slogans to mark their protest.
On 6 January 2024, police pensioners held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) over pay anomalies and claimed discrimination with retired personnel.

On 9 January 2024, railway employees and members of the Northern Railway Men Union (NRMU) staged a hunger strike and a sit-in protest at the railway station in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) demanding the cancellation of the New Pension Scheme and the reinstatement of the Old Pension Scheme.

On 10 January 2024, members of the NRMU and the Uttariya Railway Mazdoor Union (URMU) staged protests, raising slogans, at the railway station and outside the railway workshop in Amritsar city (Punjab) demanding the cancellation of the New Pension Scheme and the reinstatement of the Old Pension Scheme.

On 10 January 2024, electric rickshaw drivers staged a protest in Nepalgunj city (Banke district) in response to the sub-metropolis taking action against unregistered electric rickshaws by using a dozer to destroy them.

Around 9 January 2024 (as reported), municipal and ward employees staged a protest in Kanchanrup municipality (Saptari district), demanding action against the mayor for assaulting the chief administrative officer.
Around 12 January 2024 (as reported), workers staged a sit-in protest in front of the Samrat Cement Industry in Lamahi municipality (Dang district), against the non-disbursement of their salaries.

On 8 January 2024, MSJK members held a protest by burning a flag at Jammu city (J&K) against Pakistan and demanded a military response from India to take over Pakistan administered Kashmir and China administered Ladakh areas, deportation of Rohingya refugees from J&K and to demand restoration of statehood for J&K.
On 12 January 2024, a large number of transporters led by MSJK members held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) to demand the proposed revocation of hit and run law by the central government.

Around 12 January 2024 (as reported), ward chairpersons elected from NC protested by padlocking the municipal office in Bijaypur municipality (Rautahat district), expressing their dissatisfaction with the deputy mayor.

On 6 January 2024, thousands of tribals armed with traditional weapons gathered and held aam sabha/gram sabha (traditional rural self-rule body) at Hulasdangal village under Shikaripara block (Dumka, Jharkhand) against the administrative move of removing chudka (a traditional method of imposing restrictions on any kind of activities on a particular area proclaimed by the gram sabha) proposed coal rake point at Pagdaha.

On 11 January 2024, NSU protested by staging a candle rally in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district), demanding a transparent and merit-based selection process for the position of vice chancellor at Tribhuvan University.

On 4 January 2024, a group of people from the tribal community raised slogans and staged a protest at the collectorate office in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh), opposing the cutting down of trees in the Hasdeo forest located in Chhattisgarh. The protestors also submitted a memorandum addressed to the President of India via the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM).

On 6 January 2024, farmers gathered under the banner of SKM and 18 other farmer unions, including BKU, and staged a 'Mahapanchayat' (large meeting) and a protest, raising slogans, in Barnala city (Punjab) over various demands including the implementation of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for crops and the Swaminathan Commission report, and justice for the victims of the violence at Lakhimpur Kheri on 3 October 2021 (coded separately).

On 10 January 2024, many students staged a protest, raising slogans, at Babasaheb Bhimrao Ambedkar University (BBAU) in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) against the university authorities for failing to take action against the objectionable remarks made against Dr. B. R. Ambedkar (a Dalit champion) by some students over an instant messaging app. The protesters prevented university officials from garlanding a statue of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar during an event.

On 8 January 2024, hundreds of tribals staged a protest at the collectorate in Ratlam town (Madhya Pradesh) over the externment of two members of their community demanding an immediate revocation of the externment orders.

On 10 January 2024, the Independent Students' Union protested by padlocking the Mahendra Vindeshwari Multipurpose Campus in Rajbiraj Municipality (Saptari District). The protesters raised concerns about financial irregularities in the recruitment of teachers and called for transparency in financial matters, campus maintenance, and efficient management of facilities.

On 8 January 2024, more than 300 members of the SWRMU launched a protest by organising a relay hunger strike near the RMS office at the KSR Bengaluru train station in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) as part of a nationwide strike call given by the Joint Forum For Restoration Of Old Pension Scheme (JFROPS) and the All India Railwaymen's Federation (AIRF) demanding BJP-led Union government to restore the old pension scheme and the abolish the new pension scheme.

On 9 January 2024, more than 300 members of the SWRMU launched a protest for the second day by organising a relay hunger strike near the RMS office at the KSR Bengaluru train station in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) as part of a nationwide strike call given by the Joint Forum For Restoration Of Old Pension Scheme (JFROPS) and the All India Railwaymen's Federation (AIRF) demanding BJP-led Union government to restore the old pension scheme and the abolish the new pension scheme.

On 7 January 2024, scores of TDP members staged protests in the Eguva Palakuru locality in Puthalapattu town (Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh) condemning the vandalism of a statue of the TDP founder and president by unidentified assailants for unknown reasons (coded separately). The protesters demanded the police to take action against the accused.

On 9 January 2024, members of the TDP along with a former MLA staged a protest at the Municipal Corporation Office in Tirupati city (Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh) against the election officials for not removing the names of ineligible voters from the voters' list.

On 8 January 2024, over 100 teachers staged a protest in front of the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) office in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) demanding the INC-led state government to retain their services even after the government hands over the BBMP's educational institutions to the education department. The protesters feared that they would lose their jobs and demanded an assurance from the government.

On 1 January 2024, members of the VHP staged a protest in front of the Town Police Station in Hubballi city (Dharwad, Karnataka) against the arrest of a Hindutva member in connection with a 1992 criminal case. The protesters accused the INC-led state government of targeting members of Hindutva groups before the inauguration of Ram Temple in Ayodhya.

On 8 January 2024, villagers of Hebbagodi staged a protest in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) against the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited's (BMRCL) decision to name the Hebbagodi Metro station on Hosur Road after a biopharmaceutical company called Biocon. The protesters demanded the BMRCL to revoke its decision and also demanded the construction of a skywalk in the area for pedestrians.

On 7 January 2024, locals, including relatives of the deceased individuals, mother's groups, and various clubs, staged a protest at the court in Pokhara city (Kaski district). The protesters were demanding action against the culprit responsible for the women's deaths and justice for the women who lost their lives in a scooter-car collision.

On 8 January 2024, locals staged a protest on the Uttarahalli main road in the Kengeri locality in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) after an engineering graduate travelling on a two-wheeler died in an accident when he was run over by a bus. The protesters demanded the administration to implement road safety measures.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), the villagers of Tharuvaikulam staged a road roko (block) protest in Tharuvaikulam village (Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu) demanding the government to grant them a flood relief since their houses were destroyed and their cattle were killed during the floods in December 2023.

On 6 January 2024, police intervened by arresting a doctor and seven activists, including a medical reform activist, who were staging a protest in the restricted area of Maitighar Mandala in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district). The protesters were calling for health and medical education reforms, universal access to healthcare, affordable medical education, and justice for conflict victims, among other demands.

On 8 January 2024, police intervened by arresting three protesters, including relatives of a deceased woman, who were staging a demonstration in front of the National Human Rights Commission in Lalitpur city (Lalitpur district). The protesters were arrested for staging protests in a restricted area. The protesters were demanding justice for the woman, claiming that she was murdered, and were accusing the police of compromising the case through monetary transactions.

On 9 January 2024, police intervened by arresting three protesters, including a medical reform activist, who were staging a protest in the restricted area of Maitighar Mandala in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district). The protesters were demanding the implementation of the agreements reached with the government on health and medical education reforms.

On 11 January 2024, police intervened in the protest staged by the CPN(RM), who were burning an effigy of the Prime Minister at Ratnapark locality in Kathmandu city (Kathmandu district). The protesters were against the current government's inability to control inflation and address corruption.

On 5 January 2024, members of the BJP including a former MLA staged a protest in Hubballi city (Dharwad, Karnataka) against the arrest of a Hindutva member in connection with a 1992 criminal case. The protesters accused the INC-led state government of targeting members of Hindutva groups before the inauguration of Ram Temple in Ayodhya. The police detained the protesters.

On 6 January 2024, family members of the deceased, joined by a BSP leader, staged a protest 'day 2' and refused to cremate the deceased's body at the General Hospital in Hisar city (Haryana) demanding action against the accused involved in the suicide of a man. The police registered charges against 6 accused - including a Deputy superintendent of police. Protesters were detained.

On 10 January 2024, members of the CITU staged a protest for the second day in front of the Pallavan Illam, the head office of the Metropolitan Transport Corporation (MTC) in Chennai city (Tamil Nadu) as part of a state-wide strike by the Tamil Nadu State Transport Workers' Unions demanding the revival of the old pension scheme, the filling up of vacancies and the allocation of a fund to meet the losses incurred by the transport corporation. The protesters shouted slogans to mark their protest and the police detained the protesters.

On 9 January 2024, Anganwadi teachers and workers, municipal workers and Samagra Siksha employees along with members of the CITU took out a protest march and staged a rasta roko (road block) at the RTC Bus Stand in Ongole city (Prakasam, Andhra Pradesh) following a 'Jail Bharo' (fill the jails) protest call by trade unions. The protesters demanded the revocation of the Essential Services Maintenance Act (ESMA) invoked against the striking Anganwadi workers, an increase in their wages and the implementation of welfare schemes. The police arrested the protesters.

On 12 January 2024, members of the IYC took out a protest march to the District Collector's office in Kottayam city (Kerala) accusing the LDF-led state government of attempting to 'stifle democratic dissent' after the IYC state president was arrested in connection with a protest at the Secretariat on 20 December 2023 against the CPI(M)-led state government's Nava Kerala Sadas (public outreach program) (coded separately). The police discharged water cannons to disperse the protesters.

On 12 January 2024, members of the IYC staged a demonstration at the Collectorate in Kannur city (Kerala) against the arrest of the IYC state president in connection with a protest at the Secretariat on 20 December against the CPI(M)-led state government's Nava Kerala Sadas (public outreach program) (coded separately). The demonstrators attempted to enter the Collectorate but were stopped by the police leading to a clash. The police fired water cannons to disperse the demonstrators. There were also reports of the police assaulting women demonstrators.

On 10 January 2024, transport workers affiliated to various trade unions of the Tamil Nadu State Transport Corporation (TNSTC) launched an indefinite sit-in protest for the second day as part of a state-wide strike in in front of the bus depots in Sathyamangalam town (Erode, Tamil Nadu) with a list of demands including the revival of the old pension scheme, the filling up of vacancies and the allocation of a fund to meet the losses incurred by the transport corporations. Police detained over 150 protesters across Gobichettipalayam, Anthiyur, and Sathyamangalam.

On 10 January 2024, eight members of a family staged a protest and tried to set themselves on fire outside the Vidhan Soudha in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) over the auctioning of their house by a bank to recover the loan amount that they were unable to pay. The police stopped and detained the protesters.

On 9 January 2024, pramila vahini (likely a womens outfit based on name) held a demonstration and vandalised a liquor store at Gokulbhita village in Rajganj tehsil (Jalpaiguri district, WB) to demand its shutdown claiming it would lead to a 'spike' in crimes in the area.

On 6 January 2024, locals staged a demonstration by burning tires at the Malangwa-Nawalpur road section in Malangwa municipality (Sarlahi district). The incident occurred following the death of a youth in a police firing during a demonstration (coded as a separate event) in Barahathawa municipality.

On 7 January 2024, a clash ensued between the demonstrating locals and the police in Barahathawa municipality (Sarlahi district) in response to the death of a youth due to police firing during a demonstration (coded as a separate event) that had occurred on January 5, 2024. The clash occurred after the demonstrators set fire to the thatched-roofed roundhouse at the premises of the police office, resulting in the police firing dozens of teargas and shots in the air to take the situation under control. The demonstrators were calling for action against the guilty police officers and the resignation of the mayor, among other demands.

On 10 January 2024, tribal residents blocked the Bonai-Jangla road and detained four forest department officials at Jakeikala village under Bonaigarh police limits (Sundargarh district, Odisha) demonstrating against the elephant menace in their area and demanded appropriate compensation for their property and crop loss. The villagers lifted the blockade and released the officials after an assurance that officials of the Bonai and Kuliposh forest ranges would jointly conduct operations from the evening to drive away the two tuskers to the forest.

source: Afghan Islamic Press News Agency; Etilaat e Roz; Hasht-e Subh
notes: Explosive remnants of war: On 6 January 2024, a young shepherd was killed and a child was injured in a blast caused by an unexploded grenade, which was left from past wars in Yakah Pastah area of Qaisar district, Faryab.

Around 6 January 2024 (as reported), a group of women from the 'Purple Saturdays Movement' held an indoor protest in Ghazni province, condemning the Taliban's arbitrary arrest of women over improper Hijab charges. The demonstration criticized the Taliban's policy for systematically excluding women from society.

Other: On 9 January 2024, unknown armed men burned more than 150 copies of Qur'an inside Habibullah mosque in Lamtak [coded to Badel] village of Narang Wa Badil district, Kunar. Taliban confirmed the incident but the motive and perpetrators of the incident remained unrevealed.

Around 6 January 2024 (as reported), unknown armed men shot dead three sons of a former local police commander in Loway Char Rahi - 11th Block area of Nad Ali (Marja) district, Helmand. While the exact motive for the attack remained unclear, there are claims that the former commander had a personal enmity in the area but it remained unconfirmed.

Weapons seizure: Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), the Taliban police forces discovered and seized a large amount of weapons and ammunition including many boxes of different type bullets, an American machinegun, a Kalashnikov and other light and heavy ammunition, belonging to an unknown armed group, in Paktia [coded to Gardez] province.
On 10 January 2024, three construction workers held banners and placards on the edge of a balcony of a building in Hong Kong - Wan Chai to demand wage arrears.

On 6 January 2024, parents held placards with their children's faces and gathered to demonstrate in a plaza in Fujian province (coded to Gulou, Fuzhou). They demanded that their children, who had been lost, be found. Media reports had attributed the missing incidents to illegal organ trading.

On 5 January 2024, a group gathered to sit with placards and banners outside the government headquarters in Hong Kong - Central and Western. This is part of a 25-day sit-in demonstration, starting from 5 January. The demonstrators demanded that the government grant the right of abode to mainland-born children belonging to Hong Kong-based parents.

On 6 January 2024, about 20 supporters of the chairman of the main opposition Democratic Party of Korea (DPK), Lee Jae-myung, held a rally in front of the Seoul National University Hospital Back Gate (Seoul City - Jongno), urging authorities to investigate the attempted murder of the chairman thoroughly. On the same day, the chairman was discharged from the hospital after his stabbing in the neck on 2 January.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including supporters of the chairman of the main opposition Democratic Party of Korea (DPK), held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Chungcheongbuk-do Provincial Office (Cheongju). They oppose the former Chief of Staff to the President Moon Jae-in to run for general election, citing it as improper. At the same time, Cheongju Liberty Citizen's Alliance (a conservative group) also held a press conference to oppose his candidacy.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including supporters of the chairman of the main opposition Democratic Party of Korea (DPK), held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Busan Metropolitan Police Agency (Busan - Yeonje). They criticized the recent attack against the DPK leader and urged the authorities for a strict investigation of the incident.

On 9 January 2024, university students affiliated with the Democratic Party of Korea (DPK) held a press conference with a banner in front of a lawmaker's district office (Daejeon - Yuseong), denouncing the lawmaker for leaving the party to join the ruling People Power Party (PPP).

On 8 January 2024, about 30 members of the National Federation of Flower Producers in Busan and South Gyeongsang held a press conference with a banner at the Gimhae City Hall (Gimhae). They opposed signing the Strategic Economic Cooperation Agreement (SECA) between Korea and Ecuador, saying that the increase in imported flowers would impact the domestic producers.

On 11 January 2024, about 100 farmers staged a demonstration at a floriculture farm (Gimhae). They protested against the Strategic Economic Cooperation Agreement between South Korea and Ecuador as it would allow the massive import of cheap flowers from Ecuador, threatening the local floriculture industry.

On 11 January 2024, a group of flower farmers staged a protest in front of a greenhouse (Chojeong-ri, Gimhae). They condemned the authorities for making the Strategic Economic Complementation Agreement (SECA) with Ecuador, arguing due to the agreement flowers are being imported recklessly and threatening their livelihoods.

On 6 January 2024, animal rights activists affiliated with 19 groups, including Korea Animal Rights Advocates (KARA), staged a protest in front of the Seogwipo Student's Cultural Center (Seogwipo), demanding the enactment of 'Lucy's Law' in Korea to regulate and limit the sale of puppies and kittens as pets. At the same time, a group of auction house owners and pet industry workers counter-protested nearby to protest the legislation.

On 9 January 2024, members of the KCTU Korean Health and Medical Workers' Union (KHMU) Gwangju and South Jeolla branch held a press conference holding pickets in front of the Gwangju Metropolitan City Hall (Gwangju - Seo). They condemned the local government for closing down the Gwangju 2nd Geriatric Hospital, citing the collapse of public health care.

On 12 January 2024, members of the KCTU National Union Of Mediaworkers (NUM) held a press conference with banners at the Korea Communications Standard Commission (Seoul City - Yangcheon). They urged authorities to investigate the head of the Korea Communications Standard Commission (KCSC) over the allegations of media control.

On 8 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Construction Workers' Union (KCWU) Seoul and Northern Gyeonggi branch held a press conference with a banner in front of a construction site in Yongdap-dong (Seoul City - Seongdong). They urged the debt-ridden Taeyoung Engineering & Construction Corporation to pay overdue wages to subcontractor construction workers amid the company's liquidity shortage over real estate project financing (PF) loans.

On 9 January 2024, labor activists in Busan affiliated with the Korea Institute of Labor Safety and Health (KILSH) and KCTU Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union (KPTU) held a press conference and picketed in front of the Busan Metropolitan City Hall (Busan - Yeonje). They urged the National Health Insurance Service (NHIS) to hire call center employees from affiliated organizations directly and make them permanent positions.

On 10 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean General League of Unions and 5 apartment security workers staged a protest in front of an apartment complex (Seoul City - Gangnam). They urged the apartment management to retract the dismissal of security workers and guarantee their job security.

On 10 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union and Daejeon Citizens' Solidarity for Participation and Autonomy held a press conference with a banner in front of the Daejeon Metropolitan City Hall (Daejeon - Seo). They urged the authorities to improve the labor environment of local distribution center workers.

On 11 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union and the bereaved families of a taxi driver who burned himself to death, denouncing overdue wages and demanding the implementation of a complete monthly wage system for taxi drivers, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Seoul Southern District Court (Seoul City - Yangcheon). They urged for strict punishment of the taxi company, acknowledging that the cause of the deceased's death was workplace bullying.

On 11 January 2024, KCTU-affiliated Parcel Delivery Workers' Solidarity Union members held a press conference and picketed in front of Coupang headquarters (Seoul City - Gangnam). They condemned Coupang Logistics Services (CLS) for allegedly refusing contract renewal with a subcontractor with a labor union.

On 11 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Metal Workers' Union (KMWU) Seoul branch held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of Deutsch Motors (Seoul City - Seongdong). They urged Deutsch Motors to agree on the collective agreement.

On 10 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Construction Workers' Union (KCWU) Gangwon branch staged a protest in front of an apartment complex construction site (Wonju). They urged the construction companies and authorities to provide better treatment for construction workers and actively participate in the collective wage negotiations.

On 11 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean General League of Unions and apartment security workers held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of an apartment complex (Seoul City - Gangnam). They urged the apartment management to retract the dismissal of security workers and guarantee their job security.

On 9 January 2024, members of three civic groups in Pohang, including the Korea Federation for Environmental Movement (KFEM) Pohang, held a press conference with banners at Pohang City Hall (Pohang). They urged POSCO Holdings to elect a CEO who can actively practice ESG (Environmental, Social, and Corporate Governance) management.

On 2 January 2024, a Catholic bishop became incommunicado, after police took him away, likely, in Wenzhou city, Zhejiang (coded to Lucheng), where he was based. He had been a key figure in underground churches, which are not part of the state-administered Three-Self Church, and had been known for his close ties with the Vatican City.

On 11 January 2024, KFEM Seoul branch members held a press conference and picketed in front of Namsan Tunnel 3 (Seoul City - Jung). They condemned the city government's plan to reduce congestion charge for Namsan Tunnel, arguing it will exacerbate air pollution.

On 9 January 2024, about 30 members of the Korean Progressive University Student Union (KPUSU) held a press conference with banners in front of the Seoul Western District Court (Seoul City - Mapo), opposing the issuance of an arrest warrant for KPUSU student activists over their attempt to break into the presidential office on 6 January. They demanded a special probe into the first lady.

On 7 January 2024, members of the Korean Progressive University Student Union (KPUSU) held a press conference with banners in front of the Yongsan Police Station (Seoul City - Yongsan), urging authorities to release the KPUSU student activists who were booked during their attempt to break into the presidential office on 6 January. They demanded a special probe into the first lady.

On 8 January 2024, Wonju City Hall Public Officials Union members held a press conference with banners at the Wonju City Hall (Wonju). They condemned the Wonju mayor for transferring a full-time labor union worker outside the main office, citing the suppression of the labor union activities.

On 9 January 2024, about 50 senior painters in Gwangju held a press conference with a banner at the Gallery Gwansungea (Gwangju - Dong). They urged authorities to re-recruit Gwangju Biennale Exhibition Hall designers through various opinion-gathering processes.

On 9 January 2024, the Incheon Casino Countermeasures Committee, composed of residents and business owners in Yeongjong and Yongyu, held a press conference with banners at the Incheon Metropolitan City Hall (Incheon - Namdong). They opposed licensing a casino resort in Yeongjongdo Island without a confirmed plan for residents and the community.

On 9 January 2024, Busan Taxi Transportation Association members held a press conference with banners in front of Busan District Court (Busan - Yeonje). They demanded a fair ruling by the court over the taxi drivers' trial against the companies, claiming that reducing the prescribed working hours in the taxi industry was illegal.

On 8 January 2024, security and clean-up workers for apartments in South Chungcheong province held a press conference with banners at the Chungcheongnam-do Provincial Office (Singyeong-ri, Hongbuk-myeon, Hongseong-gun). They called for effective measures by the local government to eradicate short-term labor contracts of apartment workers.

On 9 January 2024, about 60 business owners at a site for Changwon Administrative Complex Town staged a protest in front of Changwon City Hall (Changwon), urging the city government and the builder Taeyoung Engineering & Construction Corporation to prepare appropriate relocation measures for the current residents and business owners.

On 12 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Culture and Artists Solidarity, the Association of Korean Independent Film and Video, the Federation of Korean Film Artists, the Producers Guild of Korea, and the Korea Broadcasting Actors Union (KBAU), held a press conference with a banner at the Press Center (Seoul City - Jung). They urged the authorities to thoroughly investigate and reveal the truth of the death amid the allegations that his suicide was caused by excessive police investigation and media coverage.

On 3 January 2024, the State Administrative Council, comprised of military leaders that govern Myanmar, fired heavy artillery shells from Luakkaing town in Myanmar, which landed in Zhenkang town (Lincang, Yunnan), and injured five Chinese local civilians. The shells were meant to target the Kachin Independence Organization based in neighboring Laiza, Myanmar. Attacks against the organization had increased since it seized land from the State Administrative Council in Laiza in June.

On 11 January 2024, merchants affiliated with the Daejeon Jungangno Underground Shopping Center held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the Daejeon Metropolitan Council (Daejeon - Seo). They condemned the city government, citing they had unilaterally decided on public bidding for shopping center management without consultation.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including members of Disability Discrimination Act of Solidarity in Korea (DDASK) and Korean Lawyers for Public Interest and Human Rights, held a press conference with a banner in front of the Seoul Administrative Court (Seoul City - Seocho). They welcomed the court's decision to withdraw the disqualification of a person with language disorder at the National Court Administration staff recruitment, citing it as a violation of the Act On The Prohibition Of Discrimination Against Persons With Disabilities And Remedy Against Infringement Of Their Rights.

On 9 January 2024, members of the Action for Youth Rights of Korea (ASUNARO) Busan branch held a press conference with banners in front of the Busan Metropolitan City Office of Education (Busan - Busanjin), urging authorities to improve the rights of sexual minorities in schools.

On 8 January 2024, prospective general election candidates of the Progressive Party from South Gyeongsang province held a press conference with a banner in front of the Gyeongsangnam-do Provincial Government Office (Changwon). They condemned the president for vetoing the bill allowing the National Assembly to appoint a special counsel to investigate the First Lady's alleged involvement in stock manipulation.

On 8 January 2024, members of disability rights groups, including the Solidarity Against Disability Discrimination (SADD), held a press conference holding banners in front of the National Human Rights Commission of Korea (Seoul City - Jung). They urged the commission to confirm discrimination against the chairman of the Korea Association of Persons with Physical Disabilities (KAPPD), who referred to people with disabilities as deformed children.

On 11 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Teachers and Education Workers Union (KTU) held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Seoul Metropolitan Office of Education (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the authorities to reform the system that automatically dismisses temporary teachers when faculty on leave returns.

On 10 January 2024, protesters staged the 1630th 'Wednesday demonstration' organized by the Korean Council near the former Japanese embassy in Korea (Seoul City - Jongno). They called for an official apology and compensation from the Japanese government for Japanese sex slavery and comfort women.

On 6 January 2024, members of a civic group in Busan held a protest march on the streets of Busanjin-gu (Busan - Busanjin), condemning the president for vetoing the bill allowing the National Assembly to appoint a special counsel to investigate the First Lady's alleged involvement in stock manipulation.

On 8 January 2024, about 130 protesters, including about 40 members of the Bereaved Family Council for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster and 90 members of the People's Task Force for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster, marched from the memorial altar at Seoul Plaza (Seoul City - Jung) to National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged authorities to enact a special law to further probe into the cause of the disaster within this year and prepare measures to prevent the recurrence of such tragedy.

On 8 January 2024, victims of the toxic humidifier sterilizer and environmental activists, including members of the Asian Citizen's Center for Environment and Health, held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the Gwanghwamun Plaza (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the authorities to convict the manufacturers of toxic humidifier sterilizers and compensate the victims.

On 9 January 2024, members of the Bereaved Family Council for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster and the People's Task Force for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster held a press conference with banners in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the president not to veto but immediately promulgate the special bill on the launch of a committee to reopen an investigation into a deadly 10.29 Itaewon Disaster.

On 9 January 2024, about 40 residents of Yeongseon 2-dong held a press conference with banners in front of the Busan Yeongdo-gu Office (Busan - Yeongdo), condemning the local government's budget cuts against the needs of residents.

On 9 January 2024, animal rights activists affiliated with the National Action to End Dog Eating held a press conference with banners in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo), advocating for a special bill banning dog meat consumption. They urged authorities to protect dogs in dog farms during a three-year grace period before the law comes into effect in 2027.

On 9 January 2024, residents of an apartment in Changwon held a press conference with a banner at the apartment complex (Changwon), condemning the builder LH for the poor design of the apartment complex, which lacked life and safety.

On 9 January 2024, members of the Gadeokdo New Airport Compensation Committee (a civic group) held a press conference with pickets at the Busan Metropolitan Council (Busan - Yeonje). They urged authorities to install Gadeok Station to access Gadeokdo New Airport.

On 6 January 2024, about 500 (police estimate) to 1,000 (organizer estimate) protesters, including residents of Jung-gu, Incheon, and members of parent organizations, staged a rally in front of the Incheon Metropolitan City Hall (Incheon - Namdong). They urged the city government to withdraw the approval of a building permit for religious cult Shincheonji, altering the usage purpose of the land.

On 8 January 2024, members of the Jinju People's Action for President Yoon Suk-yeol's Resignation held a press conference holding banners in front of the Jinju City Hall (Jinju). They condemned the president for vetoing two special investigation bills, which include investigations of the first lady over her alleged involvement in stock manipulation and people accused of receiving bribes of at least 5 billion won in relation to a land development scandal in Daejangdong.
