On 11 January 2024, members of the Citizens Council on Strengthening Public Health Care and Establishing Public Medical School at Incheon National University held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the Incheon Metropolitan City Hall (Incheon - Namdong). They urged authorities to establish public medical schools in Incheon to improve public health care.

On 12 January 2024, members of Incheon Regional Solidarity and Incheon Network for Peace and Welfare held a press conference and picketed in front of the Incheon Metropolitan Police Agency (Incheon - Namdong). They accused the chairman of the city council, condemning his distribution of a newspaper distorting facts on the May 18 Democratization Movement.

On 11 January 2024, members of the Bereaved Family Council for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster and People's Task Force for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster held a press conference and picketed in front of the memorial altar at Poongnammun Square (Jeonju). They urged authorities to enact a special law to further probe into the cause of the disaster within this year and prepare measures to prevent the recurrence of such tragedy.

On 11 January 2024, members of the Jukgok Migration Countermeasure Committee staged a demonstration on the sea near the Oriental Marine Tech Corporation (Changwon), condemning the foul odor and iron powder generated by shipbuilders. They called for the protection of residential and environmental rights.

On 12 January 2024, members of the Korea Vegetarian Union (KVU) and the Korea Association for Animal Protection (KAAP) held a demonstration at Gwanghwamun Plaza (Seoul City - Jongno). They demanded the abolishment of the Hwacheon Mountain Trout Ice Festival, South Korea's famous fishing festival, urging for animal rights.

On 10 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Korea Association for Animal Protection (KAAP) and 15 Million Animal Companions, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They welcomed the legislative ban on the trade and consumption of dog meat.

On 10 January 2024, the 10th Anniversary of Sewol Ferry Disaster Commemoration Committee members held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Sewol Ferry Memorial Hall near the Seoul Metropolitan Council (Seoul City - Jung). They urged the authorities to keep investigating the truth about the Sewol Ferry Disaster and come up with countermeasures to build a safer society.

On 10 January 2024, members of the Changwon Parkgolf Association held a press conference with a banner near Changwon Convention Center (Changwon). They urged the Changwon city government to discuss the management of Daesan Park Golf Course to normalize its operation.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Coalition of Humidifier Sterilizer Environmental Exposure Victims, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Seoul Central District Court (Seoul City - Seocho). They urged the authorities to convict the manufacturer of toxic humidifier sterilizers and compensate the victims.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including members of the National Task Force of Victims of Jeonse Rental Fraud, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the PPP headquarters (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the interim leader of the PPP to directly consult with victims of housing rental scams ('jeonse scams') and revise the special bill to provide them with adequate support.

On 11 January 2024, Jeonbuk Education Solidarity members held a press conference with a banner at the Jeollabukdo Office of Education (Jeonju). They urged the superintendent to apologize as the Jeollabukdo Office of Education ranked the lowest in the comprehensive integrity index two years in a row.

On 11 January 2024, about 500 protesters, including members of the Pan-Citizens Countermeasures Committee for the relocation of POSCO Holdings to Pohang, staged a protest in front of POSCO Center (Seoul City - Gangnam). They urged POSCO to withdraw its purchase of a site at Seongnam to relocate a future technology research institute, saying the company should implement the agreement with the Pohang city government to establish the institute in Pohang.

On 11 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Busan Task Force of Victims of Jeonse Rental Fraud, held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of a PPP-affiliated lawmaker's office (Busan - Gangseo). They urged the authorities to revise the special bill to help victims of housing rental scams ('jeonse scams') with adequate support.

On 10 January 2024, members of the Geoje Committee on the Establishment of the Statue of the Forced Workers held a press conference with a banner and picketed at Geoje City Hall (Geoje). They urged the authorities to approve the establishment of a forced labor statue to recognize the victims of Japan's wartime forced labor.

On 11 January 2024, residents from Hampyeong held a press conference with a banner in front of the Jeollanam-do Provincial Government (Namak-ri, Samhyang-eup, Muan-gun). They opposed the installation of the transmission line in their neighborhood without people's consent.

On 10 January 2024, protesters, including members of the National Association of Yeosu-Suncheon 10.19 Incident Bereaved Family, held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the National Assembly (Seoul City - Yeongdeungpo). They urged the authorities to reorganize the team to prepare the Yeosu-Suncheon 10.19 Incident Report with unbiased experts.

On 10 January 2024, protesters, including residents of Okgye-myeon, held a press conference with a banner in front of the Office of the President (Seoul City - Yongsan), opposing the construction of the transmission line in their neighborhood without people's consent.

On 9 January 2024, members of We, the People held a stand-still protest at Tha Pae Gate in tambon Chang Khlan, Mueang Chiang Mai (Chiang Mai), demanding the release on bail of the anti-government protesters, the removal of the lese majeste law, and prison reform. The protesters gathered to support Anon Nampa, one of the political prisoners who came to the Chiang Mai Provincial Court. The protesters stood still for one hour and 12 minutes, symbolizing the lese majeste law, Article 112.

On 10 January 2024, members of We, the People held a stand-still protest at Tha Pae Gate in tambon Chang Khlan, Mueang Chiang Mai (Chiang Mai), demanding the release on bail of the anti-government protesters, the removal of the lese majeste law, and prison reform. The protesters gathered for the second consecutive day to support Anon Nampa, one of the political prisoners who came to the Chiang Mai Provincial Court. The protesters stood still for one hour and 12 minutes, symbolizing the lese majeste law, Article 112.

source: Daily of the University of Washington
notes: On 7 January 2024, students held a rally at Lakeview Park in Seattle (Washington) to oppose the Starbucks Corporation's labor practices and to call on the University of Washington to end its contract with the company. Starbucks Workers United (affiliated with SEIU), Student Socialist League, United Students Against Sweatshops at UW, the Harry Bridges Center for Labor Studies and Students Against Starbucks were also present.

On 6 January 2024, people held a pro-Palestine blockade of Interstate 5 with about dozen vehicles between Pine Street and south of Denny Way in Seattle (Washington) to support Palestinian civilians, condemn Israel, and to call for a ceasefire and end of U.S. aid to Israel. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Seattle police and the Washington State Patrol arrived at the location but no arrests were made. The Green Party, Palestinian youth organization Falastiniyat and Jewish Voice for Peace were present.

On 6 January 2024, more than 100 people, including Palestinian-Americans, held a pro-Palestine rally in the Treasure Valley in Garden City (Idaho) to support Palestinian civilians, condemn Israel, and to call for a ceasefire and end of U.S. aid to Israel. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Boise to Palestine was also present.

On 11 January 2024, over 150 Graduate Teaching Fellows Foundation members and their supporters held a 'UO's Last Chance' rally at the Erb Memorial Union green at the University of Oregon in Eugene (Oregon) to demand an increase in wages and benefits for graduate student employees.

source: Capitol Hill Seattle Blog; Press TV
notes: On 12 January 2024, pro-Palestine demonstrators waved Palestinian flags and held a 'Stop US/UK bombing of Yemen' rally at Central Plaza near Broadway and Pine in Seattle (Washington) to oppose the recent American and United Kingdom airstrikes against Yemen. Demonstrators also chanted 'long live Palestine.' The Palestinian youth organization Falastiniyat and the Party for Socialism and Liberation were also present.
On 6 January 2024, around 30 demonstrators marched and rallied in Harrisville (Michigan) to protest against the US Capitol Riots on their third anniversary and in favor of democracy.

On 8 January 2024, nearly 150 student demonstrators with March For Our Lives marched to and rallied at the Old Capitol Building in Iowa City (Iowa) to protest gun violence and demand stronger gun control legislation following a recent school mass shooting. Those present staged walkouts and marches from various high schools throughout the city, including Iowa City High and Iowa City West High School

On 8 January 2024, dozens of demonstrators rallied outside the Lew Sterrett Jail in Dallas (Texas) to demand the release of 13 people who had been arrested at an earlier pro-Palestinian demonstration that evening and to continue protesting in favor of Palestine and against Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 10 January 2024, a few dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied at a St. Louis county council meeting in Clayton (Missouri) to demand the council pass a resolution demanding a ceasefire in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023, calling the events in the region a genocide. Those present also included Jewish community members.

On 8 January 2024, students and others demonstrated by holding signs and delivering a petition at a school board meeting in Springfield (Missouri) to protest the proposed closure of Pershing Elementary School.

On 7 January 2024, demonstrators with Native Roots ATX staged a pro-Palestinian march in Austin (Texas) to support the people of Gaza and to oppose Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 11 January 2024, about two dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied at the San Antonio City Council meeting in San Antonio (Texas) to demand the council call for a ceasefire in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. The meeting was disrupted by the protest and put into recess. After the protest ended, police barred entry to City Hall in order to prevent the protesters from returning so that the meeting could resume without disruptions. Those present included members of PSL.

On 10 January 2024, demonstrators rallied outside the Corpus Christi Regional Transportation Authority (CCRTA) in Corpus Christi (Texas) to protest 'hostile architecture' in the form of bars on public benches, which they argue is harmful to the homeless community. Those present included members of the Vulnerable Communities' Defense League.
On 6 January 2024, demonstrators with the Madison County Tenants Union rallied in Berea (Kentucky) to protest a record-high number of home evictions in Richmond, Kentucky.

On 7 January 2024, two dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators with Green Bay Anti-War Committee (GBAC) rallied in Green Bay (Wisconsin) and displayed signs over a highway bridge to support a free Palestine and demand an end to US aid to Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 10 January 2024, about seven workers with Teamsters Local 705 picketed outside a US Foods Distribution Center in Indianapolis (Indiana) as part of an ongoing labor strike to demand improved contracts.

On 8 January 2024, dozens of demonstrators with Jewish Voice for Peace and Middle Eastern Students Association staged a pro-Palestinian march and rally outside the office of Sen. Todd Young in Indianapolis (Indiana) to demand a ceasefire in Gaza and an end to US aid to Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Those present included members of the Indiana Liberation Center, ANSWER Indiana, Indy Healthcare Workers for Justice, and students.

On 11 January 2024, demonstrators rallied at Federal Plaza in Chicago - Central (Illinois) to demand the Deferred Action for Labor Enforcement (DALE) program, which protects immigrant workers from unfair labor practices, be renewed. Those present included members of Arise Chicago and Raise the Floor Alliance.

On 9 January 2024, teachers picketed outside the School City of Hammond Administration Building ahead of a school board meeting in Hammond (Indiana) to protest the possible closure of several schools in order to avoid a large budget deficit.

On 7 January 2024, around 50 workers with the National Association of Letter Carriers (NALC) rallied in Minneapolis (Minnesota) to demand protective measures be passed to protect mail carriers, such as stronger punishments for those convicted of crimes against postal workers, following recent attacks and robberies.

On 8 January 2024, nearly 1,000 workers with the Anoka-Hennepin Education Association (associated with the National Education Association) rallied outside a school board meeting in Anoka (Minnesota) to demand an improved contract amid negotiations.

On 7 January 2024, around 200 demonstrators staged a pro-Palestinian rally and march in Grand Rapids (Michigan) to support a free Palestine and condemn US support of Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Those present included members of FRSO, Palestine Solidarity Grand Rapids, Grand Rapids Alliance Against Racist and Political Repression, Students for Justice in Palestine, SDS, US Palestine Community Network, New Students for a Democratic Society, Iota Epsilon Alphas, and B.L.A.C.K. Beta Chapter (an African American fraternity).

On 8 January 2024, dozens of student demonstrators gathered outside the Hannah Administration building on the campus of Michigan State University (MSU) in East Lansing (Michigan) to protest the re-opening of Berkey Hall, the building where a mass shooting occurred in February 2023.

On 11 January 2024, around 70 pro-Palestinian demonstrators marched and rallied on the campus of Ohio State University (OSU) in Columbus (Ohio) to support a free Palestine and demand the university divest from Israeli-based companies amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Those present included members of Students for Justice in Palestine, PSL, and Ohio Youth for Climate Justice.
On 10 January 2024, as many as 100 students and teachers rallied on the campus of Youngstown State University (YSU) in Youngstown (Ohio) to protest the proposed program closures and faculty layoffs.

On 8 January 2024, workers with Stewartville United Educators and Education Minnesota held signs in protest at a school board meeting in Stewartville (Minnesota) to demand higher wages amid contract negotiations.
On 9 January 2024, workers with UAW Local 1391 rallied in East Dubuque (Illinois) as part of an ongoing labor strike to demand increased pay and an improved contract.

On 6 January 2024, demonstrators staged a silent protest during the city council inaugurations in East Chicago (Indiana) by holding signs to demand the Council pass legislation to honor a local Medal of Honor awardee.
On 9 January 2024, demonstrators with Citizen's Climate Lobby created a display of over 100 snow people in Duluth (Minnesota) to demand carbon pricing legislation to prevent global warming.

On 8 January 2024, about 50 people including family members protested in Palmetto (Florida) demanding justice for a 36-year-old Black man who died on Nov. 1, 2023. According to the family, the man was involved in a mental health episode when Palmetto police arrived and tased him multiple times. The family also says that the police department has not provided any details or the official police report on the death. The protest was organized in support of the Black Lives Matter Movement.

On 6 January 2024, around 100 people, including Palestinian families, gathered at a pro-Palestinian demonstration in Tallahassee (Florida) in support of Palestine amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.. Demonstrators demanded a ceasefire in the conflict, and the US government to stop supporting Israel. The rally was organized by the Tallahassee Community Action Committee (TCAC). Florida Palestine Network was present at the event.

On 8 January 2024, people protested outside Duval County Public Schools headquarters ahead of the board's first meeting of 2024 in Jacksonville (Florida) demanding an end to book bans across the state. Public School Defenders Duval, Florida Freedom to Read, Families for Strong Public Schools, Rebel Readers, 904WARD, Equality Florida, and Moms Rising were present.

On 7 January 2024, more than 200 people, including Palestinian families, gathered at a pro-Palestinian demonstration in Birmingham (Alabama) in support of Palestine amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Demonstrators demanded a ceasefire in the conflict. Members of Birmingham chapters of the Democratic Socialists of America and Party for Socialism and Liberation organized the demonstration.
On 7 January 2024, several dozen cars caravanned around Sherbrooke (Quebec) in support of Palestine and to condemn 'Israel's genocide' amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

source: CTV News; National Post (Canada); Ottawa Citizen
notes: On 7 January 2024, a few dozen people held a pro-Palestinian rally on Parliament Hill in Ottawa (Ontario) to call for a permanent ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war. Demonstrators also called for a weapons embargo on Israel and a 'free Palestine.' The Palestinian Youth Movement organized the rally. There were a handful of fines from bylaw officers at this protest for using a microphone or megaphone in a public space. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 10 January 2024, about two dozen people gathered outside the Edmonton Police Service headquarters in Edmonton (Alberta) to protest against the homeless encampment closures. The rally was organized after a demonstration that occurred on the previous day where a homeless encampment was cleared. The Bear Claw Beaver Hills House (an indigenous group) organized the rally.

On 5 January 2024, local residents, including South Okanagan Indigenous Chiefs, MLA Joan Philip (NDP), and the Mayor of Penticton Julius Bloomfield, held a rally on the steps of the Penticton Courthouse in Penticton (British Columbia) to demand justice after the murderer of an Indigenous woman was granted day parole seven years into a life sentence.
On 9 January 2024, Transco bus drivers and workers picketed in front of the Montreal School Service Center (CSSDM) in Montreal - East (Quebec) to demand higher wages. Members of CSN were present.
On 11 January 2024, locked-out municipal workers, including CUPE Local 1490 members, staged a rally in Matheson (Ontario) to demand a new contract with higher wages.

On 9 January 2024, demonstrators and residents of a homeless encampment on Rowland Road and 95 Street rallied in Edmonton (Alberta) to protest the removal and closure of the encampment. Police arrested 3 people, including one journalist, for obstructing police, assaulting a peace officer and resisting a peace officer. The journalist was arrested when she stayed in an exclusion zone and refused to leave the area. According to demonstrators, a police officer shoved a demonstrator to the ground and punched another demonstrator when he shoved the police officer in response. The police tackled the demonstrator to the ground. The two demonstrators were treated for minor injuries. Police said they seized a pellet and BB guns, 34 knives, 11 machetes, 10 samurai swords, two axes, brass knuckles and a collapsible baton from the camp.

On 6 January 2024, people held a pro-Palestinian rally near the Avenue Rd. Overpass of Hwy. 401 in Toronto - North York (Ontario) to call for a permanent ceasefire and a 'free Palestine' in the Israel-Hamas war. Palestine House organized the rally. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.
On 6 January 2024, the Sikh family of a couple who were killed in a shooting in Caledon on November 2023 held a vigil and rallied in Brampton (Ontario) to demand justice for them.

On 8 January 2024, people from the Sikh community gathered outside the Dashmesh Culture Centre in Calgary (Alberta) to protest against the temple's elected leadership committee. The protesters have been continuously gathering since December 23, 2023.

On 6 January 2024, dozens of people, including the Ukrainian community, held a rally in front of the B.C. legislature in Victoria (British Columbia) to call on support for Ukraine and to protest the 29 December Russian missile attack on Ukraine.

On 5 January 2024, dozens of local residents held a rally in the parking lot of North Cowichan's Municipal Hall in Shawnigan Lake (British Columbia) to protest against delays at the Shawnigan Village Rail Trail project and its impact on the environment and property rights.

On 6 January 2024, people held a pro-Palestinian rally on the riverfront in Windsor (Ontario) to support Palestinian civilians and call for a permanent ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war. The Windsor4Palestine organized the demonstration. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 6 January 2024, 17 people held a pro-Palestinian rally at the Four Corners intersection and marched to the Nunavut legislature in Iqaluit (Nunavut) to support Palestinian civilians and call for a ceasefire in the Israel and Hamas war. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 9 January 2024, local residents held a rally in the parking lot of North Cowichan's Municipal Hall in Duncan (British Columbia) to demand changes to the way drug abuse and crime are controlled across the country. The Canadian Citizens Against Crime and Public Drug Abuse organized the rally.

On 7 January 2024, between 50 and 100 people from the Sikh community gathered in and outside the Dashmesh Culture Centre in the northeast of Calgary (Alberta) to express opposition to the temple's elected leadership committee. There were also counter-demonstrators, including Sikhs, who gathered in support of the committee. The two groups fought each other during the demonstration. Police officers moved in to separate the groups and de-escalate the situation. According to police, 3 to 4 people suffered minor injuries during the fight. According to demonstrators, demonstrators suffered broken fingers and small wounds. No arrests were made in relation to the clash. Demonstrators in opposition to the leadership committee were demonstrating continuously since December 24 2023.

Property destruction: On 9 January 2024, two people damaged a car that was decorated with Israeli flags in Vaughan (Ontario). The two vandals and their driver arrived at a Jewish family's home after 3 a.m, where they shattered the windows of a car parked in front of the home and damaged its tires. After doing so, they left the scene. This event took place amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 6 January 2024, protesters and members of One NJ7 rallied in Flemington (New Jersey) to commemorate the police officers who died and were injured during the January 6 U.S. Capitol Riot and to support democracy. Protesters also called on people to vote and keep informed about the candidates. Representatives of Westfield 20/20, Summit Marches On, Summit Area Indivisible, LD23 Candidates, Indivisible Garden State Values, and Morris County Democrats joined the rally.

On 6 January 2024, protesters and members of One NJ7 rallied in Somerville (New Jersey) to commemorate the police officers who died and were injured during the January 6 U.S. Capitol Riot and to support democracy. Protesters also called on people to vote and keep informed about the candidates. Representatives of Westfield 20/20, Summit Marches On, Summit Area Indivisible, LD23 Candidates, Indivisible Garden State Values, and Morris County Democrats joined the rally.

On 9 January 2024, local residents and members of Communities of Color for Health Equity and the Black Men's Political Task Force rallied outside a Walgreens in Roxbury in Boston (Massachusetts) to call on the company to keep the store open as it is scheduled to close on 15 January.

On 6 January 2024, local residents, members of the Muslim community and of the Council on American-Islamic Relations (CAIR), and family members of the victim rallied at the NIA Masjid & Community Center ahead of the funeral in Newark (New Jersey) to call for justice and action after an imam who was fatally shot outside the mosque on 3 January.

On 11 January 2024, about 10 protesters rallied on the Greenfield Common in Greenfield (Massachusetts) to demand the closure of Guantanamo Bay detention camp due to poor living conditions, as well as what they claimed is 'torture methods and abuse.' No More Guantanamos, Witness Against Torture, CodePink, and Amherst Amnesty International Group 128 organized the rally.

Police say the body was thrown there, which indicates that the killing took place elsewhere. Authorship and motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 15 January 2024, in Recife (Pernambuco), a group of local residents protested to demand the demolition of an abandoned building in the Santo Amaro neighbourhood. The protesters claim that the building has been abandoned for 60 years and has a very high structural risk.

Around 14 January 2024 (as reported), in Teresina (Piaui), unknown individuals shot and killed a man in the Angelim neighbourhood. The victim's body was found with shot wounds and tied hands. The victim was abducted by the suspects before being killed. Authorship and motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

The suspects attacked the victim while he was working at a local burger shop. After shooting, the suspects returned to the shop and fired again against the victim, who was already on the floor, and then escaped (likely an execution). Authorship and motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

On 19 January 2024, in Teresina (Piaui), an unidentified armed individual shot and killed a man in a drive-by shooting in the Vila Mocambinho I. The victim was a delivery worker for a local burger shop, and the suspect chased him on a motorcycle while he was on his way to a delivery. The victim was also on a motorcycle, and the suspect fired against him over 20 times (likely an execution). Another worker at the same burger shop was also shot and killed by unidentified individuals two days before this attack (coded separately). The suspect escaped. Authorship and motivations for the attack are unknown. 1 fatality.

Protesters stated they had been told by police that the search could not commence until it had been requested by an attorney, but they were later informed it had actually begun.

Around 17 January 2024 (as reported), in Concepcion (Biobio), victims of human rights violations during anti-government mobilizations in October 2019 (estallido social) belonging to the Coordinating Group of Victims of Ocular Trauma and the United Biobio Corporation protested outside the Courts of Justice alongside their legal advisors to express frustration with the pace of trials in which they were involved and the lack of support for victims. Protesters demanded reparations and swifter justice.

On 15 January 2024, in Hualpen (Biobio), guardians of senior citizens residing at the San Jose Obrero care home occupied the building to protest over its planned closure at the end of February. Protesters stated they had nowhere for their relatives to go given limited space in other care homes and demanded a solution from authorities. Police arrested four protesters for their occupation.

Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), in San Bernardo (Metropolitana), residents of the Villa Ignacio Carrera Pinta neighborhood protested to demand increased security following a series of burglaries. Protesters banged pots and pans, blew whistles and waved signs.

Protesters, who waited for the former Minister outside a book launch at the Santiago headquarters of the Catholic University of Valparaiso (PUCV), shouted that he was an accomplice and that they would publicly denounce (funar) him wherever he went.

On 19 January 2024, in Santiago - Providencia (Metropolitana), workers at the National Service for Geology and Mining protested against a unilateral decision by the Services' National Directorate to change its mission, vision and strategic goals, as well as its organizational structure. On 12 January 2024, in San Antonio (Valparaiso), around 500 informal settlers belonging to 42 communities residing on 254 hectares of land between Cerro Centinela and the commune of Cartagena, including the leader of the Coordinating Group for Occupations and Camps of San Antonio and Cartagena, marched to the offices of the Presidential Delegation to protest against eviction orders they had recently received and to raise awareness about the level of uncertainty in their lives. Protesters delivered a letter to demand a meeting to discuss solutions to the housing problem.

The protesters also demanded the resignation of the hospital's manager.

On 17 January 2024, in Santa Marta (Magdalena), a group of people beat up two suspected thieves in the Bastidas neighborhood. One of the suspects ended up with a stabbing injury in her back. There were no fatalities.
Around 13 January 2024 (as reported), in Pupial (Meta), a group of residents blocked the Bogota - Villavicencio highway to protest the constant closures and difficulties in mobility in the area.

On 17 January 2024, in Ipiales (Narino), health contractors at the Ipiales Civil Hospital protested against low salaries and bad labor conditions. They claimed that they have low salaries compared to workers with employee contracts. CUT led the protest.

On 17 January 2024, in Espriella (Narino), unidentified armed individuals shot at a group of people gathered in the Nuevo Amanecer neighborhood. Five people were injured. There is no further information on the motives or perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of armed groups in the area. There were no fatalities.

actor1: FARC Dissident - 33rd Front: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia Dissident Faction (33rd Front)
notes: On 16 January 2024, in the rural area of Abrego municipality (Norte de Santander), 33rd Front FARC dissidents kidnapped two truck drivers of the Ara supermarket chain for a couple of hours in the Brisas del Tarra sector. They perpetrators also set the truck on fire. The victims were released hours later. The abduction was related to extortion threats against the supermarket.

Around 13 January 2024 (weekend of), in Cucuta - Cementerio (Norte de Santander), a group of residents in the San Rafael neighborhood beat up a homeless man who they say stabbed a man during a fight, causing his death. The police intervened and arrested the man. There were no fatalities.

On 16 January 2024, in Cucuta - Southwest (Norte de Santander), residents beat up and threw rocks at a man who they suspected of having stolen a motorcycle in the Carora neighborhood. The man was injured and taken to a hospital by the police. There were no fatalities.

On 16 January 2024, in Puerto Santander (Norte de Santander), unidentified armed individuals shot and killed a farmer in the 16 de Julio neighborhood. There is no further information on the motives or perpetrators of the attack but the area is known for the presence of armed groups. 1 fatality.

On 16 January 2024, in Orito (Putumayo), unidentified armed individual(s) shot and killed a man inside a commercial establishment. There is no further information on the motives or perpetrators of the attack but there is presence of armed groups in the area. 1 fatality.

On 19 January 2024, in Bucaramanga (Santander), dozens of teachers protested outside the Mayor's Office against delays in the appointment of 500 teachers who won a public contest to teach in public schools in the city. They demanded a meeting with authorities and a solution to the problem.

On 16 January 2024, in Bremen (Sucre), residents from Bremen, Las Flores, and El Recreo corregimientos blocked for three hours the Sincelejo - Corozal highway to protest against the lack of drinking water in the area and demanded the restoration of the service.

actor1: FARC Dissident - Jaime Martinez: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia Dissident Faction (Jaime Martinez)
notes: Property destruction: On 15 January 2024, in El Limonar vereda (Valle Del Cauca), members of the Jaime Martinez FARC dissident faction set on fire two trucks from the Smurfit Kappa Carton company in El Limonar vereda. It is suspected that the attack was a retaliation against the company for not paying extortion to the armed group.

On 19 January 2024, people blocked roads (means unspecified) in a demonstration in Mbadjini (location coded to Foumbouni, Mbadjini Est, Grande Comore), Makorani (coded separately), and Itsounzou (coded separately) against the re-election of President Assoumani, following accusations of electoral fraud.

On 19 January 2024, people blocked roads (means unspecified) in a demonstration in Makorani (Ngazidja ou Grande Comore), Mbadjini (coded separately), and Itsounzou (coded separately) against the re-election of President Assoumani, following accusations of electoral fraud.

On 19 January 2024, people blocked roads (means unspecified) in a demonstration in Itsounzou (Ngazidja ou Grande Comore), Mbadjini (coded separately), and Makorani (coded separately) against the re-election of President Assoumani, following accusations of electoral fraud.

On 14 January 2024, residents blocked roads with stones in Tsinimouapanga (Ngazidja ou Grande Comore) to express their dissatisfaction with the current president running for re-election. Demonstrators attempted to prevent police from entering the city with electoral materials of the Independent National Electoral Commission during presidential and gubernatorial elections.

On 11 January 2024, two unidentified armed men fired gunshots near the government palace in Praia (Nossa Senhora da Graca, Praia) for an unknown reason. Soldiers fired warning shots in reaction to dissuade any attempt to violate the security perimeter of the government palace. The two individuals escaped.

On 16 January 2024, in Ciego de Avila, Ciego de Avila, two police officers on a patrol arrested an activist and member of the Opposition Movement for a New Republic for unknown reasons. He was imprisoned and released hours later. The arrest was described as arbitrary and politically motivated.
Around 17 January 2024 (as reported), in Marianao, La Habana, a mob of residents grabbed and beat up a man who had committed a robbery moments before. The man was submitted to police forces.

actor1: Mbole Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
notes: On 11 January 2024, Mbole communal militia attacked IDPs affiliated to Lengola group in Babusoko (Ubundu, Tshopo) where the IDPs had come to look for food. At least 3 IDPs killed with machetes and arrow, and several injured.

actor1: Mbole Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)
notes: On 12 January 2024, Mbole communal militia attacked IDPs affiliated to Lengola group in Babusoko (Ubundu, Tshopo) and in Losilo (coded separately) where the IDPs had come to look for food. 4 IDPs killed with machetes and arrow, and one child seriously injured. Fatalities split across two locations, coded as 2.

actor1: Mbole Communal Militia (Democratic Republic of Congo)

Fatalities split across two locations, coded as 2.
Around 13 January 2024 (as reported), M23 shot and injured a farmer in Bikenge (Masisi, Nord-Kivu). The farmer was brought to hospital for care.
On 17 January 2024, Mobondo occupied the village of Mbusie, near Bagata (Kwango-Kasai, Bagata, Kwilu). No clash was reported. The villages were emptied of their residents.
On 17 January 2024, Mobondo occupied the village of Menkwo (Plateaux, Kwamouth, Mai-Ndombe). No clash was reported.
On 18 January 2024, 10 provincial ministers of Equateur province demonstrated in Mbandaka (Mbandaka, Equateur) to demand 17 months of salary arears and allowances.
On 18 January 2024, MLC party supporters demonstrated in Mbuji-Mayi (Mbuji-Mayi, Kasai-Central). They burnt tires to denounce the poor results of the national legislative elections

On 18 January 2024, supporters of the 'Cause Commune' party burned tires on the streets to demonstrate against the disqualification of their coalition REPOP in Kikwit (Kikwit). They are classified by the CENI among the coalitions that did not reach the eligibility threshold.
On 18 January 2024, more than 200 retired state employees demonstrated in Kindu (Kindu, Maniema) demanding payment of their final settlements from the Congolese government.

On 13 January 2024, Fano abducted six people in Ano Kere kebele, near Degem Hambiso, Degem woreda (North Shewa, Oromia). Three of the hostages were released after they paid a ransom of 300,000 ETB each to the armed group.

On 14 January 2024, overnight, Fano clashed with Kebele militias in Ano Kere kebele, near Degem Hambiso, Degem woreda (North Shewa, Oromia). Kebele communal militias managed to push Fano militias out of the area. Casualties unknown.

Looting: On 12 January 2024, overnight, Fano looted unspecified number of civilian properties in Ano Kere kebele, near Degem Hambiso, Degem woreda (North Shewa, Oromia), resulting in several residents displaced from their homes.
On 18 January 2024, Fano clashed with ENDF and took control of Ayna Bugna town (North Wello, Amhara). It is unknown for how long Fano exercised control over the town. Casualties unknown.
On 8 January 2024, families of a mass murder victims gathered at the commissariat of Franceville (Mpassa, Haut-Ogooue) calling for the capital punishment against the suspected murderer.
Around 13 January 2024 (as reported), kiosk dealers held a protest in front their company's headquarters in Libreville ( Libreville, Estuaire) to denounce their contracts and call for a salary raise.

On 15 January 2024, some residents blocked and set a truck on fire in Sabronum (Ahafo Ano South,) suspecting it to be transporting an excavator for illegal mining at a forest reserve. The rioters opposed illegal mining activities.

source: Agencia de Noticias da Guine; BBC News; Deutsche Welle; Jornal no Pintcha; O Democrata
notes: Other: On 15 January 2024, the Ministry of Interior announced the ban of all public demonstrations for an indefinite period in Bissau (Bissau, SAB) claiming that search and seizure operations for firearms were underway.

source: Agencia de Noticias da Guine; BBC News; Deutsche Welle; O Democrata
notes: Other: On 18 January 2024, police officers arrested four PAIGC leaders in Bissau (Bissau, SAB) when they tried to gather people to start a demonstration against the dissolution of the National Popular Assembly (ANP) on 4 December. Police officers set up cordons around the city, at intersections and access points to the center of the capital to prevent protesters to access gathering places. This is the second failed mobilization since 8 January (coded separately).
Around 10 January 2024 (as reported), 17 de Fevereiro Teacher Training School students took to the streets in Bissau (Bissau, SAB) against the change of director of the educational establishment.

Around 17 January 2024 (as reported), near La Florida Aceituno, Escuintla, a police officer was killed and his body was found bearing several gunshot wounds, inside a car. No more information is provided by the source. The motivation for the attack is unknown. 1 fatality.
On 4 January 2024, women demonstrated in Kagbelen (Madina Oula, Kindia) following the rape and murder of a young girl.
On 4 January 2024, suspected unidentified armed men raped and killed a girl in Kagbelen (Madina Oula, Kindia).
On 12 January 2024, a mob gathered to lynch a suspected rapist in Kagbelen (Madina Oula, Kindia) after he had been arrested by law enforcement (assumedly police and gendarmerie).

On 12 January 2024, a mob of mainly farmers destroyed houses, motos, cars and shops, property belonging mainly to pastoralists in Laine (Laine, Lola, Nzerekore) and clashed with law enforcement (presumed to be police or/and gendarmerie) who fired live ammunition.
On 13 January 2024, an unidentified armed group killed and burned a woman and her baby between Balato and Kintinian districts (Kintinian, Siguiri, Kankan).

source: Guinea News; Guinee Matin; Le Djely
notes: On 13 January 2024, a mob destroyed the house of an alleged rapist in Kagbelen (Madina Oula, Kindia) who had been arrested the day before.
On 17 January 2024, suspected farmers shot and killed a pastoralist in Kani (Kokota, Lola, Nzerekore). The conflict between farmers and pastoralists in the area is brewing.

Property destruction: On 17 January 2024, outside Trojes, El Paraiso, military forces seized 52,000 alleged marijuana plants in a mountainous area known as Sacanli. The plants were cultivated in an area of approximately 6.65 hectares. Additionally, two nurseries with 45,000 seedlings of the hallucinogen, two firearms, and the capture of two individuals who were responsible for caring for the plantations were secured.

On 15 January 2024, in San Pedro Sula, Cortes, a group of vendors located at the southern exit towards Tegucigalpa, at the former toll booth in the Chamelecon area, protested against the eviction of their businesses ordered by the Municipality of San Pedro Sula. At least 15 vendors would be affected by the decision of the city hall and they expressed their concerns about the lack of alternative proposed by the administration and the consequent impact on their livelihoods.

On 18 January 2024, in San Pedro Sula, Cortes, a group of bus drivers from El Ocotillo, El Carmen village, paralyzed their units in protest after a passenger was shot by assailants during a robbery on a bus on this same day. The protesters denounced that the frequency of violent assaults and demanded greater security to police forces.

On 16 January 2024, in San Pedro Sula, Cortes, a group of residents burned tires demanding the repair of roads in the Flor de Cuba neighborhood. The road were recently destroyed by sewage, which led residents to work on the construction of gutters on their own, using their own materials, tools, and labor.

Movement of forces: Around 15 January 2024 (as reported), RSF mobilized large forces in multiple directions in Tebon in the west, Samoaa direction in the southwest direction, and Muglad direction in the west around Babanussa (Babanusa, West Kordofan) in preparation for an attack on SAF 22nd Infantry Division. 80% of civilians in Babanussa were displaced due to fear of the clashes.
Change to armed group: On 15 January 2024, RSF announced that thousands of SAF soldiers defected to RSF in Babanussa (Babanusa, West Kordofan).

Movement of forces: Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), South Sudan army deployed troops and heavy equipment in Heglig 2 oilfield, bordering Heglig 1 in Heglig Oil Field (Al Idia, West Kordofan) in anticipation of an attack by RSF.

Recruitment: On 17 January 2024, White Nile governor announced the popular mobilization, including the arming with weaponry of residents (coded as Tendalti communal militia) in Tendalti locality (location coded to Tandalti, Tendalti, White Nile) for protection against RSF.
Displacement: On 12 January 2024, 174 civilians fled their houses in Abu Kershola (Abu Kershola, South Kordofan), due to concerns of potential escalations in the security situation.

On 15 January 2024, in Barcelona (Anzoategui), a group of demonstrators, including teachers, and members of the Teachers' Union Coalition, and the Anzoategui state SINVEMA-FVM teachers' union, gathered by the Casa del Maestro Office to demand better pay and working conditions, the restoration of certain benefits, and the renewal of their collective labor agreement. Elsewhere, another group of workers and unionists, summoned by the FETRANZOATEGUI workers' union, marched with similar demands, and in their support, from the Bombon Square to the Bolivar Square. These were two of the dozens of simultaneous nationwide demonstrations staged by teachers and other state workers that day, coinciding with the Venezuelan Teachers' Day.

source: OVCS: Observatorio Venezolano de Conflictividad Social
notes: On 19 January 2024, in Anzoategui state (coded to Barcelona), demonstrators protested with unspecified demands on issues related to justice, policy, labor, social security, civilian security, food, and housing.

Around 19 January 2024 (as reported), in Coro (Falcon), three unidentified crime group suspects opened fire on the house of a Coro prison guard (who was likely inside the house), and left a funeral flower wreath by it, in an attack in the San Agustin sector. The three perpetrators were arrested shortly after. It is suspected that the attack was ordered by an inmate at the prison in which the victim works, in retaliation for working with integrity, and not collaborating with their criminal activities within the prison. It is thought that the victim and his family came out unscathed from the attack. There were no fatalities.

actor1: Police Forces of Venezuela (1999-) CPNB: National Bolivarian Police
source: Diario 2001; El Carabobeno; El Pitazo; La Verdad de Vargas
notes: On 13 January 2024, in Ocumare del Tuy (Miranda), PNB police forces shot and killed four members of the El Danielito gang, including their leader and second-in-command, during an armed clash with six of them in the El Estadio de la Colonia Mendoza sector. The police officers had lured the suspects to the area by simulating being buyers in the Facebook Marketplace platform. When the police arrived at the scene, they found the six suspects, who hurled a grenade at them and a shootout ensued. Four were injured in the gunfire and pronounced dead in hospital. The other gang members are suspected to have escaped. Police collected four guns and a grenade, and seized three motorcycles. 4 fatalities.

The perpetrators escaped on a motorcycle. There is no further information about the motives of the attack. 1 fatality.

Around 5 January 2024 (as reported), family members gathered Mombasa (Mvita, Mombasa) against the alleged abduction of their kin. Joined by Haki Africa representative, the group demanded the police service and the government to help them find their missing kin.

On 9 January 2024, residents gathered in King'orani area of Mombasa (Mvita, Mombasa) against the move by the county government to use part of their land for road construction without compensation. Led by human rights defenders, the group demanded the governor to help address the matter.

On 12 January 2024, lawyers under the Law Society of Kenya (LSK) marched from the Imara Building to the Law Courts in Mombasa (Mvita, Mombasa) in support of the Judiciary, following the recent threats by the President to defy court orders, citing claims of corruption in the judiciary. The group demanded the President to stop undermining the Judiciary.

The group demanded the CJ not to honor the invitation.

On 12 January 2024, for the 14th consecutive week, at the call of the Moroccan Commission for the Support of the Islamic Nation Causes, worshippers demonstrated following the Friday prayer in Taroudant (Taroudannt, Souss-Massa) to denounce plans to expel Palestinians from Gaza, calling for the end of Israeli military operations in Gaza and Morocco's normalization with Israel.

On 14 January 2024, at the call of the Moroccan Front for Supporting Palestine and Against Normalization and Moroccan Commission for the Support of the Islamic Nation Causes, activists demonstrated in Taroudant (Taroudannt, Souss-Massa) in support of Palestine, calling for the end of Israeli military operations in Gaza and Morocco's normalization with Israel.

On 19 January 2024, for the 15th consecutive week, at the call of the Moroccan Commission for the Support of the Islamic Nation Causes, worshippers held a protest sit-in after the Friday prayer in Taroudant (Taroudannt, Souss-Massa in support of Palestine, calling for the end of Israeli military operations in Gaza and Morocco's normalization with Israel.

The victims were rescued by a local defense militia in collaboration with police forces and soldiers [coded separately].

On 16 January 2024, BSCVG in collaboration with soldiers and police forces engaged an unidentified armed group in an armed clash at the group's hideout and rescued abductees. The group had abducted commuters around Orokam (Ogbadibo, Benue) [coded separately], one BSCVG operative was wounded in the clash, one AK47 rifle machine gun among other item were recovered from the group.

Around 17 January 2024 (as reported), SAF air-raids targeted Al Farshaia (Dilling, South Kordofan). Casualties unknown.
On 6 January 2024, WCJF arrested a Houthi cell of 4 people that intended to plant explosive devices and set fire to military vehicles in the Western Coast (coded to Al Mukha district) (Al Hudaydah).

On 19 January 2024, thousands of demonstrators held a protest in Al Mukha city (Taizz) in solidarity with Palestinian people and to denounce the 'crimes of the Israeli occupation' in the context of the latest round of violence in Israel-Palestine.

Don't Break Article 9! Total Action Executive Committee, and NCCJ, Buddhists from Nipponzan Myohoji, and a company employee from Okinawa, protested in front of the Prime Minister's residence in Tokyo - Chiyoda against the ongoing land reclamation construction on the Oura Bay side of Henoko for the establishment of a U.S. military base. Banners with messages such as 'Don't pollute the sea' and 'No landfill' were displayed during the protest. A member of the House of Representatives from the JCP attended the event.

notes: On 12 January 2024, about 900 people, including representatives of the Shimagurumi Conference and a member of the House of Representatives from the JCP, protested in front of the gate of Camp Schwab in Nago, Okinawa, in response to the government's initiation of land reclamation on the Oura Bay side of Henoko to construct a U.S. military base. The protesters opposed the construction, asserting that the decision disregarded the local autonomy of the prefecture. The event was called 'Prefectural Citizens' Rally Against Delegated Landfill' and was organized by the All Okinawa Council. On the same day, Helicopter Base Opposition Group members protested on the sea on 4 boats and 12 canoes against the placement of stones into Oura Bay.

On 5 January 2024, SPM assailants on bike opened fire at a vehicle and killed ASWJ's leader, while injuring his driver in Ghori Town, Islamabad city (Islamabad, FCT). 3 SPM attackers were arrested in connection to the killing on 13 January 2024.

Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), Taliban arrested two girls on charges of violating the Taliban dress code, and also wearing white boots and socks near Mustafa Kamal School in Pul-i-Khumri city, Baghlan. While on of these girls reported to be still in the Taliban custody, the whereabout of the other girl remained unknown. No information about the identities of the girls.

On 15 January 2024, unknown armed men shot dead a senior leader of Islamic Movement Uzbekistan (IMU) and one of his companion in front of Abu Hanifa Mosque in Pul-i-Khumri city, Baghlan. The motive remained unknown and no group claimed responsibility.

actor1: Police Forces of Afghanistan (2021-) General Directorate of Intelligence
notes: On 16 January 2024, the Taliban intelligence forces apprehended a Taliban commander of Tajik ethnicity in the market of Chahab district, Takhar, and seized a Ranger and some ammunition from his house. The motive for his arrest remained unclear. He has served as a local police (Arbaki) commander during the former government, but later joined Taliban before the fall of the previous government.

On 15 January 2024, unknown armed men shot dead a religious scholar in Char Daryia area of Khulm district, Balkh (reported as Khulm district, Samangan). Taliban while confirming his killing, refused to provide more details on the incident.

actor1: Police Forces of Afghanistan (2021-) General Directorate of Intelligence
notes: Around 16 January 2024, one civilian was arrested by Taliban intelligence forces in Markaz-e-Behsud district, Wardak. He was tortured and died while being held. He was accused of collaborating with Alipour militia, a charge his family denied. He was arrested on 14 January and his body was released on 18 January.

Weapons seizure: Around 15 January 2024 (as reported), the Taliban police forces discovered and seized some weapons and military equipment belonging to an unknown armed group in Urozgan and Paktia [coded to Gardez], provinces. The size of the confiscated weapons remained unclear.

Weapons seizure: Around 15 January 2024 (as reported), the Taliban police forces discovered and seized some weapons and military equipment belonging to an unknown armed group in Paktia and Urozgan [coded to Tirinkot], provinces. The size of the confiscated weapons remained unclear.

Weapons seizure: Around 4 January 2024 (from 22 December - 17 January), the Taliban police forces discovered and seized a 14 different types of weapons and ammunition including 7 Kalashnikovs, 4 pistols, 340 rounds of Pika, 90 rounds of Kalashnikov, one ring mine, 35 kg of gunpowder, and some other different types of rounds and ammunition, belonging to an unknown armed group, in Hissa-e-awali Behsud, Daimir Dad, Qar, Jalrez and Maidan Shahr city of Wardak.

source: Etilaat e Roz; Khaama Press; Pajhwok Afghan News; Xinhua
notes: Explosive remnants of war: On 15 January 2024, 1-2 children were killed and seven-eight others were injured in a blast caused by an unexploded ordnance while the children were playing with it in Kohna Qala village of Baghlan-e-Jadeed district, Baghlan. The unexploded ordnance was left in the area from the past wars.

On 18 January 2024, residents of Lezhe protested in Tirana in front of the constitutional court against a deal struck between Albania and Italy to set up two refugee centers in the country for migrants coming in from the African continent.

On 17 January 2024, more than 300 Concord Hospital staff, including HSU members, marched in Sydney - Eastern, New South Wales, in response to the NSW premier's decision to eliminate free parking for healthcare workers.
On 13 January 2024, people marched in Sydney, New South Wales, to support Palestine.
On 14 January 2024, LGBTQ+ activists and their allies marched in Geelong, Greater Geelong, Victoria, to advocate for LGBTQ+ rights.

On 14 January 2024, thousands of people, including people from Palestinian, Indigenous, and South African communities, including an Aboriginal activist, a historian professor, and a South African artist and writer, marched in Melbourne, Victoria, to call for an immediate ceasefire in the armed conflict between Hamas and Israel and the freedom of Palestine. They also commended South Africa's legal action against Israel in the International Court of Justice.

On 15 January 2024, people protested outside the Israeli embassy in Canberra, Australian Capital Territory against the Israeli attack on Gaza. Palestinian and South African flags were displayed during the demonstration.
On 14 January 2024, Blue Mountains residents protested in Sydney - Western, Blue Mountains, New South Wales against the opening of the Western Sydney Airport due to noise concerns.

On 18 January 2024, more than 300 people, including members of the Unionists For Palestine and SA, marched from a Labor MP's office to the Preston City Hall in Preston, Melbourne - Northern, Whittlesea, Victoria, in response to her refusal to sign an open letter that urges a permanent ceasefire in the armed conflict between Hamas and Israel and an end to the Israeli occupation of Gaza. The protesters opposed the Israeli attack on Gaza and expressed their support for Palestine.

On 19 January 2024, people protested at Webb Dock in Melbourne - Central, Victoria, to prevent ZIM, an Israeli transportation company, from shipping. They also called for the freedom of Palestine. The Unionists For Palestine organized the event.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of people, including members of the Newcastle Australian Greens and CPA, marched around the Foreshore Park in Newcastle, New South Wales, against the Israeli attack on Gaza. They also expressed their support for Palestine. The Palestine Action Group Newcastle organized the event.
On 16 January 2024, 11 people protested outside Tasmanian Quality Meats' slaughterhouse in Cressy, Northern Midlands, Tasmania, highlighting allegations of animal cruelty in the facility.

On 11 January 2024, hundreds of people protested at Bondi Beach in Sydney - Eastern, New South Wales, to express support for Israel by displaying its flag and to demonstrate solidarity with those affected by the October 7 attacks carried out by Hamas.
On 13 January 2024, a small group of demonstrators protested at Mount Barker Road in Adelaide, South Australia, to show their support for Palestine.

On 14 January 2024, two factions of Chhatra League (BCL) engaged in a clash at Khulna University for Engineering and Technology in Khulna city (Khulna Sadar), over formation of new BCL committee. At least 4 people were injured in the clash.

On 13 January 2024, unidentified assailants (likely supporters of an Awami League candidate based on conflict on election day) shot and killed a polling agent of an independent candidate in Nateswar union, Sonaimuri upazila (Noakhali, Chittagong), in a targeted attack over electoral rivalry following elections.
On 15 January 2024, a supporter of Awami League hacked and killed a local leader of BNP in Goyespur Bazar area, Gaffargaon upazila (Mymensingh), in a targeted attack over unknown reasons.

On 17 January 2024, at least 10-12 persons (likely Muslim based on their names) vandalized houses and a Hindu temple at Chhatyani village, Dhunat upazila (Bogra, Rajshahi), over a land dispute. At least 2 people (likely Hindu based on their names) were injured in the assault.
On 13 January 2024, a group of villagers killed a farmer belonging to a rival group in Naodoba union (Shariatpur, Dhaka) over a long-standing land dispute.

On 15 January 2024, locals set fire to the house of an Awami League supporter, who was accused in the killing of a BNP leader (coded separately), and assaulted him, in Gaffargaon upazila (Mymensingh). Police fired blank shots to disperse the locals.

On 17 January 2024, residents of Plovdiv protested against the demolition of a Soviet-era monument referred to as the Alyosha. Authorities voted to remove the monument by the end of 2024. Ever since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine was launched the public has been split between wanting to remove all relics of the Soviet era and keeping them.
On 17 January 2024, residents of several villages in the Asenovgrad area protested in Plovdiv in front of the administrative court against the construction of a marble quarry in their region.
On 18 January 2024, transport workers in Sofia protested demanding higher salaries and better working conditions. The KNSB Union supporter the protesters.
On 12 January 2024, Belarusian police arrested the vice-head of the Directorate of Affairs of the President of the Republic of Belarus, in Minsk (Leninskiy, Minsk City) on charges of corruption.
On 16 January 2024, transit bus drivers, including Amalgamated Transit Union Local 616 members, gathered and picketed in Windsor (Ontario) to demand a new contract with higher wages.

On 17 January 2024, about 600 Ecole de Technologie Superieure (ETS) staff, including FTQ and CUPE members, held a rally and picketed in Montreal - Center East (Quebec) to demand a new collective agreement.

On 16 January 2024, non-public fund workers, including PSAC members, picketed in front of the Garrison Petawawa Military base in Petawawa (Ontario) to demand a fair contract agreement with wage increases.

On 15 January 2024, hundreds of people held a pro-Palestinian rally at the Canadian Tribute to Human Rights monument in Ottawa (Ontario) to mark the 100th day of the Israel-Hamas war, for supporting Palestinian civilians and calling for a ceasefire in the Israel and Hamas war. The Palestinian Youth Movement organized the rally. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 14 January 2024, about 80 international students and their supporters staged a rally at Algoma University's campus after they failed business courses amid new allegations of cheating in Brampton (Ontario) to call for an outside investigation into the administration's enrolment and to demand fair reassessment, transparency and accountability from school officials.

On 16 January 2024, teachers and supporters, including members of the Saskatchewan Teachers' Federation, picketed as a job action in front of Midtown Plaza, Lawson Heights and Centre Mall in Saskatoon (Saskatchewan) to demand progress at the bargaining tables for a new contract with higher wages.

On 16 January 2024, around 200 teachers and supporters, including members of the Saskatchewan Teachers' Federation, picketed as a job action in Yorkton (Saskatchewan) to demand progress at the bargaining tables for a new contract with higher wages.
On 12 January 2024, workers of the agency Swipe Jobs and Del Monte, including Unifor Local 222, staged a rally and picketed in Oshawa (Ontario) to demand a fair contract with a higher salary.

On 13 January 2024, the agency Swipe Jobs and Del Monte workers, including Unifor Local 222, staged a rally and picketed at 940 Thornton Rd. in Oshawa (Ontario) to demand a fair contract with a higher salary.
On 17 January 2024, workers of the agency Swipe Jobs and Del Monte, including Unifor Local 222, staged a rally and picketed in Oshawa (Ontario) to demand a fair contract with a higher salary.

On 14 January 2024, hundreds of people held a pro-Palestinian rally in front of the Consulate General of Israel in Toronto - Old Toronto (Ontario) to mark the 100th day of the Israel-Hamas war, for supporting Palestinian civilians and calling for a ceasefire in the Israel and Hamas war. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023. There was a separate pro-Israel demonstration on the same day, but the two demonstrations did not overlap.

On 15 January 2024, nine people held a pro-Palestinian rally outside Yukon MP Brendan Hanley's office in Whitehorse (Yukon) to support Palestinian civilians and criticize the Canadian position on an International Court of Justice case against Israel. Members of Yukoners for Solidarity and Action were present. This pro-Palestinian demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023. The protesters also opposed the federal government's support of military operations against targets in Yemen.
On 18 January 2024, 4 demonstrators rallied on Hunt Club Road in Ottawa (Ontario) to protest against removing trees by the Ottawa Airport Authority along Hunt Club Road.

notes: On 14 January 2024, hundreds of local residents held a rally in Riviere-Rouge (Quebec) to protest against the partial closure of the emergency room. The mayor Denis Lacasse was also present.

On 16 January 2024, dozens of local residents, including members of the Kainai Nation, held a rally at Central Memorial Park and marched to the Calgary Courts Centre in Calgary (Alberta) to demand a safe drug supply across the province.
