notes: On 16 January 2024, hundreds of teachers and supporters, including members of the Saskatchewan Teachers' Federation and Unifor, picketed as a job action on Albert Street Bridge near Saskatchewan's Legislative Building in Regina (Saskatchewan) to demand progress at the bargaining tables for a new contract with higher wages.
On 13 January 2024, people held a pro-Palestinian rally in Kitchener (Ontario) to support Palestinian civilians. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 13 January 2024, a few dozen people held a pro-Palestinian rally in front of Foreign Affairs Minister Melanie Joly's house in Montreal - Center East (Quebec) to support Palestinian civilians and to call for an end to Canada's military and diplomatic support for Israel. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 13 January 2024, about 200 people held a pro-Palestinian rally outside the Supreme Court in St. John's (Newfoundland and Labrador) to support Palestinian civilians and call for a ceasefire in the Israel and Hamas war. This demonstration took place amid the Israel-Hamas war, which began in October 2023.

On 15 January 2024, a handful of people staged a rally outside the provincial court in Charlottetown (Prince Edward Island) to demand justice for the death of a 17-year-old boy whose body was found last month.

On 16 January 2024, around 400 people held a rally at the Vancouver courthouse in Vancouver (British Columbia) to support the co-founders of the Drug User Liberation Front and to call for a safe drug policy and supply.

On 12 January 2024, representatives of the Democratic Alliance for the Betterment of Hong Kong, the Anti-Black Money, Anti-HK Independence Concern Group, and Protect Hong Kong Alliance held placards and defaced the Union Jack flag, outside the British Consulate General Hong Kong in Hong Kong - Central and Western. They were dissatisfied that Rishi Sunak, prime minister of the United Kingdom, voiced his support for Lai Chi Ying, founder of Apple Daily, a prominent dissident tabloid media outlet shut down by the government. They said the move marked an inference into Hong Kong's rule of law.

On 19 January 2024, representatives of the Hong Kong Federation of Trade Unions held banners and gathered to demonstrate outside the government headquarters in Hong Kong - Central and Western. They demanded that the government grant Shenzhen residents multi-entry visas to boost tourism.

On 13 January 2024, three representatives of the League of Social Democrats marched, chanting slogans and holding props and placards, in Hong Kong - Yau Tsim Wong. They demanded that the government build more boarding houses for adults with moderate intellectual disability.

Around 8 January 2024 (as reported), a political dissident writer became incommunicado, after police, suspectedly, took him away, likely from his home in Youyang Tujia and Miao Autonomous county (Youyang, Chongqing).

On 13 January 2024, police beat up a female (coded as attack), causing her to bleed, when detaining her and around 100 other people in a hotel in Xuchang city, Henan (coded to Weidu). They were bank depositors, who had travelled to Xuchang to demand their deposits be recovered after a freeze on bank withdrawals in 2022, as the government investigated the banks for involving financial crimes. Once arriving at a railway station in Xuchang, they were all intercepted and arrested for 'anti-terrorism' reasons, in the process injuring another bank depositor (separately coded).

On 13 January 2024, police lifted a bank depositor away from a railway platform, in the process causing back injuries and bone fracture to him, when intercepting him and around 100 other people in a railway station in Xuchang city, Henan (coded to Weidu). They were bank depositors, who had travelled to Xuchang to demand their deposits be recovered after a freeze on bank withdrawals in 2022, as the government investigated the banks for involving financial crimes. Once arriving at a railway station in Xuchang, police intervened and arrested them, subsequently detaining them in a local hotel for 'anti-terrorism' reasons, where another bank depositor was attacked (separately coded).

On 17 January 2024, staff members of a sub-district administrative office beat up a female petitioner (coded as attack), causing her mouth and nose to bleed, after intercepting her in Wuhan city, Hubei (coded to Jiang'an). The petitioner had planned to petition to the local government, which had sought to curb dissent as the local People's Congress would be held on 18 January.

On 12 January 2024, a Heilongjiang-based petitioner became incommunicado for six days, after government officials and the manager of a local bureau for letters and calls intercepted her outside the Supreme Court in Beijing - Dongcheng. She had travelled to Beijing to file for petitions.

On 13 January 2024, tens of Greek Cypriot and Turkish Cypriot activists and conscientious objectors rallied at Ledras crossing point in Nicosia, protesting for a demilitarized Cyprus and expressing their solidarity with a Turkish Cypriot conscientious objector, who was refusing to enlist in the army. The protesters also decried the hostile approach taken by the authorities on both sides of the island. Representatives of the platform Conscientious Objectors Cyprus, AKEL, Volt Cyprus, the movement AFOA (anti-capitalism, ecology, feminism, autonomy), and the multi-communal youth initiative Hade, participated in the protest.

On 18 January 2024, early in the morning, prisoners rioted after police officers of the Nicosia Criminal Investigation Department and the Cyprus Drug Squad (YKAN) raided Nicosia central prison, conducting searches in the cells of convicts suspected of having links to criminal activities in Pafos and the torching of a female state prosecutor's car on 16 January. Police believed the latest attack was linked either to a criminal case the prosecutor had handled or to her husband's position as the head of YKAN. The prisoners set fire to sheets and threw them in the corridor outside their cells and hurled uncapped bottles filled with urine at police officers.

On 19 January 2024, about 50 inmates at Cyprus' central prison in Nicosia protested in solidarity with the people of Palestine and engaged in a hunger strike. The prisoners from Palestine, Syria, Lebanon, Azerbaijan and other Arab countries, protested over the indifference of the Cypriot authorities and the international community to the deaths of women, children and elderly Palestinians, accusing Israel of genocide. They clarified that they do not identify with Hamas. They also protested over the long incarceration time awaiting for their trials and demanded the same rights as European prisoners.

On 15 January 2024, farmers with over 100 vehicles and machines gathered for a demonstrative ride from Chomutov to the border with Germany and back, declaring their solidarity with their protesting counterparts and highlighting chronic problems of the Czech agriculture.

On 15 January 2024, farmers protested and blocked the road L3080 near Bleicherode (Nordhausen, Thuringen). The event was part of a nationwide farmers' demonstration movement against planned agricultural subsidy cuts.
On 17 January 2024, people demonstrated outside of a tourism company's office in Vesterbro, Copenhagen, calling on the company representatives to discontinue offering travel tours to Israel.

On 13 January 2024, around 1000 people, including Palestinian community members, demonstrated in Helsinki to show support and solidarity with the people of Palestine and to call for a permanent ceasefire in the Hamas-Israel conflict.

On 13 January 2024, demonstrators, including Russian and Ukrainian community members, demonstrated outside of the Parliament in Helsinki in support of the Finnish government's decision to completely close the crossing points at the Finnish-Russian border amid a massive flow of undocumented refugees from the Russian side.

Property destruction: On 14 January 2024, overnight, an unidentified group of people set fire to cables in Marseille (Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes), disrupting the train line between Marseille and Toulon. The motives were unclear but the act was claimed by a far-left group on Attaque.

On 15 January 2024, at the call of CGT and FA/SPP-PATS - Autonomous Federation of Professional Firefighters and Specialized Administrative and Technical Personnel (Federation Autonome des Sapeurs Pompiers Professionnels et des Personnels Administratifs et Techniques Specialises), around 15 firefighters gathered outside the Departmental Council in Mont-de-Marsan (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to demand more staff.

On 17 January 2024, employees of the regional council of Nouvelle-Aquitaine, including CGT delegates, staged a protest action in Limoges (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to denounce the regrouping of personnel at a single work facility.

On 8 January 2024, at the call of CGT, FO, CFE-CGC, and CFDT, around 15 Cruas nuclear power plant employees gathered for a picket line outside their workplace in Cruas (Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes) to demand a pay rise.

On 16 January 2024, at the call of FNSEA and JA, farmers drove tractors at a slow pace to disrupt traffic on the A64 highway near Muret (Occitanie), to protest against rising production costs and bureaucratic constraints.

On 19 January 2024, almost 100 farmers, including FNSEA and JA affiliates and several dozen tractors, gathered in front of the offices of the ASP (Agence de Services et de Paiement) in Limoges (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to protest against the delayed disbursement of their Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) payments.

On 13 January 2024, at the call of the Lannion Hospital Defense Committee (Comite de Defense de l'Hopital de Lannion), around 2,000 people, including health workers, gathered in Lannion (Bretagne) to protest against the closure of the local hospital's emergency unit at night.

On 15 January 2024, taxi drivers gathered outside the Saint-Charles train station and subsequently drove at a slow pace to disrupt traffic in Marseille (Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur) to demand a limitation on the number of taxis in the city and better working conditions.

On 15 January 2024, at the call of the FM-VTC union, taxi drivers drove at a slow pace to disrupt traffic in Montpellier (Occitanie) to demand a limitation on the number of taxis in the city and better working conditions.

On 15 January 2024, at the call of the Union of Taxi Drivers (Union des VTC), around 100 taxi drivers drove at a slow pace to disrupt traffic on the A8 highway between Cannes and Nice (Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur) to demand a limitation on the number of taxis in the region and better working conditions.

On 13 January 2024, at the call of Stop Oil Arcachon Basin (Stop Petrole Bassin d'Arcachon), around 50 people, including regional councilor Vital Baude (LE), lay down on the Pilat Dune in La Teste-de-Buche (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to protest against planned oil drills in the local forest.

On 14 January 2024, at the call of LDH, around 50 people, including LFI, LE, and PCF affiliates, gathered in Bastia (Corse) to protest against the passing of the new immigration law, which they deemed xenophobic and unfair to immigrants.

On 18 January 2024, at the call of the APN, around 40 police officers, including UNSA delegates, gathered in front of the police headquarters in Nancy (Grand Est) to demand better working conditions to be able to cope with security during the 2024 Paris Olympics, including bonuses, better anticipation of their working hours, and help with childcare during the event.

On 13 January 2024, at the call of Blendecques : Aa, Never Again (Blendecques : Aa, plus jamais ca), around 600 people gathered in Blendecques (Hauts-de-France) to demand emergency reparations and financial support from the state after two floodings in the area.

On 14 January 2024, around 500 people, including Landes Aquitaine Environment (Landes Aquitaine Environnement) affiliates, gathered in Seignosse (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to protest against the construction of a high-voltage line between Bordeaux and Bilbao, which the protesters deemed damaging to local biodiversity.

On 19 January 2024, students staged a blockade at the Jules Verne High School in Nantes (Pays de la Loire) to oppose the newly approved immigration law championed by Minister of the Interior Gerald Darmanin, which the demonstrators deemed xenophobic. Some participants set fire to garbage cans.

On 13 January 2024, environmental campaigners from SAS gathered in Southwick (England) against the Southern Water company and the dumping of untreated wastewater on Kingston Beach, citing environmental concerns.
On 13 January 2024, far right groups organized a protest in Tbilisi and demanded stronger sanctions for offending religious items.
On 17 January 2024, the United National Movement organized a protest in Tbilisi against the prolonged reconstruction of a street.

On 13 January 2024, pro-Palestinian protesters marched in Athens - Central Athens in solidarity with the Palestinian people, following a call by the Palestinian Community of Greece and the Stop the War - Solidarity to Palestine alliance, demanding a ceasefire in Gaza and that Greece stop supporting Israel and the United States. The protesters shouted slogans and made stops at the embassies of the U.S. and Israel.

On 13 January 2024, members of the Thessaloniki Committee for International Disarmament and Peace (EDYETH) rallied at Thessaloniki port shouting slogans against the docking of U.S. warships in Thessaloniki and then marched in the city blocking traffic.

On 14 January 2024, a large crowd rallied at Thissio, Athens - Central Athens, for a protest concert over the neglect and closure of state mental health facilities and facilities treating addiction. The rally was organized by the labor unions of facilities such as KETHEA, Dafni, ESYN, and associations of relatives and friends of people with addictions.

actor2: Police Forces of Greece (2019-) Units for the Reinstatement of Order
notes: On 15 January 2024, about 40 anarchists raided the administration building of the National Technical University of Athens (NTUA) in Zografou, Athens - Central Athens, vandalizing the premises and attempting to occupy the rectorate. During the incident administrative staff were present in the building. The rioters were opposing the establishment of private universities in the country and other government reforms on education. MAT riot police forces chased them away using tear gas. Photographs of injured anarchists circulated on social media. There were 7 detentions, of which 4 turned into arrests.

On 15 January 2024, early in the morning, about 30 Roma stormed the hospital of Messolonghi and started smashing things, cursing and beating staff, nurses and doctors after a Roma woman, who had been transferred there with a stroke, started having an epileptic seizure and the persons accompanying her thought she was dying. A doctor received a head injury.

On 15 January 2024, late in the evening, students marched in Athens - Central Athens against police preventing the occupation of the National Technical University of Athens (NTUA) in Zografou earlier in the day and the head injury of a student; they also protested against the establishment of private universities in the country saying it contravened provisions in the Greek Constitution protecting free education. They were surrounded by strong police forces.

actor2: Police Forces of Greece (2019-) Units for the Reinstatement of Order
notes: On 16 January 2024, a group of hooded assailants (likely anarchists) stormed the administration building of the National Technical University of Athens (NTUA) in Zografou, Athens - Central Athens, frightening staff as they hurled abuse and smashed windows and furniture. The academic authorities called for police reinforcements. The assailants briefly clashed with police before running off. This was the second such incident in 2 days. The rioters were opposing the establishment of private universities in the country, saying it contravened provisions in the Greek Constitution protecting free education.

On 16 January 2024, archaeological site guards walked off the job and rallied at the Acropolis, Athens - Central Athens, for four hours to express their opposition to government plans to outsource ticketing due to swelling visitor numbers. The protesters, supported by their union PEYFA, demanded the state retain control of ticketing and admissions and hire additional staff. The new leader of SYRIZA also attended the protest.

Weapons seizure: On 17 January 2024, police arrested an Attica police officer, who was the manager of an Athens police department, for the misappropriation of service firearms, following a routine inspection by an armorer that revealed discrepancies, which the officer was unable to justify. The Internal Affairs division initiated an investigation, revealing the disappearance of six service pistols, four service revolvers and the officer's personal revolver. Authorities seized 2 hunting rifles, 41 cartridges of various calibers, a bulletproof vest and an unauthorized police baton during a search of the officer's office, vehicle and residence. Two days later he was placed on administrative leave.

On 17 January 2024, student associations of the anti-establishment sector, demonstrating against the establishment of private universities in Greece, unfurled a banner at the administration building of the Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, also supporting student occupations in the country. They wrote slogans in the lobby and damaged a turnstile.
On 18 January 2024, friends and relatives of a man presumed dead rallied outside the security police offices in Messolonghi, where a suspect was being held, demanding he reveals where the body is.

actor2: Police Forces of Greece (2019-) Units for the Reinstatement of Order
source: AMNA; Ekathimerini; I Avgi; To Pontiki; To Vima
notes: On 18 January 2024, thousands of university and school teachers and students, supported by their parents, as well as university staff and leftist organizations, marched in Athens - Central Athens against the establishment of private universities in the country saying it contravened provisions in the Greek Constitution protecting free education. The demonstrators responded to a call by more than 150 student associations and the coordinating committee of Athens school students, while OLME and DOE initiated a work stoppage to facilitate participation in the demonstration, while many schools and about 250 out of the total 450 university faculties and departments were under student occupation. Traffic in central Athens was severely disrupted. At the end of the demonstration, a group of rioters hurled petrol bombs at police, set fire to garbage bins and retreated in university grounds.

actor2: Police Forces of Greece (2019-) Units for the Reinstatement of Order
notes: On 18 January 2024, university and school teachers and students, supported by their associations and by OLME and DOE, and members of anti-establishment groups, marched in central Thessaloniki to the Ministry of Macedonia and Thrace shouting slogans against the establishment of private universities in the country, saying it contravened provisions in the Greek Constitution protecting free education. A group of hooded rioters split from the main demonstration and attacked police forces outside Aristotle University hurling various objects and makeshift petrol bombs. Police used tear gas and stun grenades chasing the rioters inside the campus.

On 19 January 2024, students refused to enter their classrooms in 2 high schools in Corfu protesting that the previous day police cleared the schools from students occupying the premises as part of a nationwide action against the establishment of private universities in the country. The previous day, after the schools' heads called the authorities, riot police entered the schools, following a prosecutor's orders, and detained 6 underage students.

source: 444.hu; Index.hu; Mandiner; Merce; Nepszava Online; Telex.hu
notes: On 13 January 2024, several hundreds of people, mostly students and teachers of the PDSZ union, as well as Fridays for Future activists, protested outside the Ministry of Interior in Budapest - V, demanding better funding for the education sector, better salaries, more teaching staff, and non-interference in the academic freedom. An MKKP-affiliated candidate mayor held a speech in favor of the education sector reform.
On 16 January 2024, around 60 people, mostly teachers, protested in Szentes against the dismissal of the principal of the reformed school.

On 18 January 2024, Momentum members, including one MP, protested outside the National Electoral Bureau building in Budapest - V, demanding that the Bureau examines the proposed questions for a referendum on assisted ending of life.

Property destruction: On 13 January 2024, unidentified perpetrators shot at a parked car belonging to a presidential candidate's campaign team member (Gerindra party) in Bissappu district (Bantaeng regency, South Sulawesi province). The car's back window was broken. The car owner suggested it might be related to his political activity. He was inside a mosque during the incident. There were no casualties.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of Muslims from the Humanity Care Archipelago Mass Organization Forum, including students from the Campus Teaching Agency Forum, members of the Palestine Care Women, the Palestine People's National Committee, the Indonesia Sharing Happiness, and the Indonesian People Care of Palestine civic movements, peacefully protested in Semarang city (Central Java province). They commemorated 100 days of the war in Gaza. They voiced support for Palestine and condemned the occupation of the Palestinian territory by Israel. They marched from the Baiturrahman Grand Mosque to the Pahlawan Road in the city.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of Muslims peacefully protested at the Digulis roundabout in Pontianak city (West Kalimantan province). They voiced support for Palestine and condemned the occupation of the Palestinian territory by Israel.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of Muslims, including students from the KAMMI organization, peacefully protested at the city square in Pangkal Pinang city (Bangka-Belitung province). They voiced support for Palestine and condemned the occupation of the Palestinian territory by Israel.

On 13 January 2024, dozens of Muslims peacefully protested at the Nunukan Square in Nunukan district (Nunukan regency, North Kalimantan province). They voiced support for Palestine and condemned the occupation of the Palestinian territory by Israel.

On 15 January 2024, a group of activists from the Indonesia Corruption Watch (ICW) peacefully protested in front of the Corruption Eradication Commission in South Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - South). They demanded the arrest of a corruption suspect from the PDI-P party.

On 16 January 2024, a group of students from the West Java Activist Alliance peacefully protested in Braga area, Bandung city (West Java province). They demanded a peaceful election in 2024 and opposed the spread of provocations and hoaxes during the campaign period.

They demanded an investigation of violence against a journalist by government staff in Ambon (coded separately).

On 17 January 2024, a group of students peacefully protested in front of the Digulist Monument on Ayani Road, Pontianak city (West Kalimantan province). They demanded a peaceful election in 2024 and opposed the spread of provocations and hoaxes during the campaign period.

On 17 January 2024, a group of people from the Belantu Community Struggle Forum (FPMB) peacefully protested in front of the Satam roundabout in Tanjung Pandan district (Belitung regency, Bangka-Belitung province). On 18 January 2024, hundreds of people from the Belantu Community Struggle Forum (FPMB) peacefully protested in front of the court building in Tanjung Pandan district (Belitung regency, Bangka-Belitung province). They demanded a land dispute resolution between them and PT Foresta Lestari Dwikarya palm oil company. On 17 January 2024, a group of people from the Kupang City Justice Hunter Alliance, accompanied by students, peacefully protested in front of the public prosecutor's office in Kupang city (East Nusa Tenggara province). On 18 January 2024, hundreds of teachers peacefully protested in front of the regent's office in Langkerembong district (Manggarai regency, West Nusa Tenggara province). They demanded permanent contracts.

They demanded a peaceful election in 2024 and opposed the spread of provocations and hoaxes during the campaign period. On 17 January 2024, dozens of parents peacefully protested in front of the Sate building in Bandung city (West Java province). They demanded the issuance of their children's high school diploma certificates.

On 16 January 2024, a group of students peacefully protested on Bogor Road, East Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - East). They demanded a peaceful election in 2024 and opposed the spread of provocations and hoaxes during the campaign period.

On 19 January 2024, around 100 market sellers peacefully protested in front of the council building on Veteran Road, Kabanjahe district (Karo regency, North Sumatra province). They opposed the increase in market retribution fees, claiming it created a financial burden for them.

On 19 January 2024, hundreds of PP mass organization members peacefully protested in front of the General Election Commission on Imam Bonjol Road, Menteng district, Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). On 17 January 2024, a group of people from the Suing People's Alliance civic movement peacefully protested on Imam Bonjol Road, Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). On 13 January 2024, thousands of Muslims peacefully protested in front of the US Embassy in Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). They voiced support for Palestine and condemned the occupation of the Palestinian territory by Israel.

On 18 January 2024, a group of students from the Indonesia Student Line (BIMA Indonesia) peacefully protested in front of the Financial Transaction Reports and Analysis Center in Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). They demanded the agency investigate all suspicious transactions during the 2024 election campaign period.

On 19 January 2024, a group of farmers from the SPI, accompanied by the Labour Party, peacefully protested in front of the Ministry of Agriculture and the National Food Agency in South Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - South). They opposed the import of rice, claiming it negatively affected local farmers' incomes.

On 17 January 2024, a group of market sellers peacefully protested on Jati Baru Road, Tanah Abang district, Central Jakarta city (Jakarta province) (coded as Jakarta - Central). They opposed the increase in market retribution fees, claiming it created a financial burden for them.

On 16 January 2024, a group of Muslim students from the HMI organization peacefully protested in front of the public prosecutor's office in East Banggae district (Majene regency, West Sulawesi province). They demanded a corruption investigation in Majene. They confiscated a truck and used it as a stage. The protest remained peaceful.

On 17 January 2024, a group of students protested in front of the Bima Muhammadiyah University (UMB) in Bima city (West Nusa Tenggara province). They opposed the increase in the tuition. A group of lecturers, accompanied by the university's rector, dragged a student to the office and beat him. There was no detail regarding the victim's injury.

On 4 January 2024, tea workers held a demonstration by detaining a ration dealer at Raipur estate on the outskirts of Jalpaiguri town (WB) against a mobile ration distribution center over not getting equal and proper quantities of ration.

On 11 January 2024, locals assaulted three monks (likely Hindu monks based on BJP statement of support) and damaged their vehicle over claimed suspicion of them engaging in kidnapping at Gaurangdi village in Gangasagar area in Kashipur tehsil (Purulia district, WB). Police later rescued them and detained 12 persons.
On 6 January 2024, BJP members held a protest march and blocked road for an hour at Jalpaiguri city (WB) against claimed corruption by TMC.

On 18 January 2024, members of the AIDYO staged a protest march at the Freedom Park in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) demanding the INC-led state government and the BJP-led Union government to recruit and fill up vacant posts in the various government departments.

On 18 January 2024, members of the All India Loco Running Staff Association affiliated to the Dakshin Railway Employees Union, staged a protest in Chennai city (Tamil Nadu) demanding the government to classify the job of loco pilots as intensive. The protesters also demanded for the restoration of the old pension scheme and also said that their working time be reduced to six hours.

On 17 January 2024, hundreds of people including members of the BJP under the aegis of the Heritage Kunigal Stud Farm Protection Committee staged a protest in Kunigal town (Tumakuru, Karnataka) against the INC-led government's decision to convert the farm into a township. The protesters demanded the government to conserve the heritage farm and not allot the land for any commercial purposes.

On 19 January 2024, members of the BJP took out a protest march to the Memorial at Killikurissimangalam near Ottappalam town (Palakkad, Kerala) against the LDF-led state government for not paying the staff and students of the Kalakkath Kunchan Nambiar Memorial which housed many artefacts and documents about the history and literature of Kerala.

On 16 January 2024, members of the CPI(M) including a former minister under the aegis of the Kerala Private School Management Association staged a protest in Alappuzha city (Kerala) against a literary writer for criticising power politics and personality cults during a literature festival.

On 6 January 2024, farmers from Yadgir, Raichur, Bagalkot, Kalaburagi and surrounding areas staged a protest in front of the Krishna Bhagya Jala Nigam Ltd (KBJNL) office at Bhimarayanagudi town (Yadgir, Karnataka) demanding the INC-led state government to release water from two Krishna reservoirs to save the chilli crops in four North Karnataka districts. The protesters also dug up graves outside the office and sat inside them to mark their protest.

On 7 January 2024, farmers from Yadgir, Raichur, Bagalkot, Kalaburagi and surrounding areas staged a sit-in protest in front of the Krishna Bhagya Jala Nigam Ltd (KBJNL) office at Bhimarayanagudi town (Yadgir, Karnataka) demanding the INC-led state government to release water from two Krishna reservoirs to save the chilli crops in four North Karnataka districts. The protesters also dug up graves outside the office and sat inside them to mark their protest.

On 16 January 2024, more than 50 members of the Mysuru farmers' association staged a protest at the Karnataka Gramin Bank in Krishnarajanagara town (Mysuru, Karnataka) against the bank for auctioning of a farmer's house despite the farmer having repaid his loan. The protesters accused the bank of seizing the property in an illegal manner.

On 16 January 2024, sugarcane cultivators in Thirumandangudi and the surrounding areas in Papanasam taluk staged a protest at the Koonamchery burial ground in front of a Pudukottai-based distillery company in Koonancheri village (Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu) against the proposal to convert a private sugar mill into a distillery by a private company which had acquired the mill through the National Company Law Tribunal proceedings. The protesters completed 410 days of protest and raised slogans against the government. The protesters also said that the a distillery would affect the agricultural prospects of the area.

Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), members of the Tamil Nadu Viviasayigal Sangam (farmer group) affiliated to the Communist Party of India (Marxist) staged a protest in Thirumandangudi village (Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu) against the setting up of a brewery factory in the area. The protesters said that the area was declared as a Protected Agricultural Zone and the setting of a distillery would affect the agricultural prospects of the area which was declared as a Protected Agricultural Zone.

On 14 January 2024, members of the INC staged a protest in Shimoga city (Shivamogga, Karnataka) against a BJP MP for his provocative comments about mosques and derogatory comments about the Karnataka Chief Minister. The protesters demanded legal action against the MP and burned his effigy to mark their protest.

On 14 January 2024, members of the INC including an MLA staged a protest in Arasikere city (Hassan, Karnataka) against a BJP MP for his provocative comments about mosques and derogatory comments about the Karnataka Chief Minister. The protesters demanded legal action against the MP.

On 13 January 2024, members of the INC including MLAs staged a protest march from Kaduthuruthy town to the Thirunakkara temple ground in Kottayam city (Kerala) demanding action and assistance from the LDF-led state government and the BJP-led Union government to revive the rubber sector in the state. The protesters said that rubber farmers were facing financial difficulties and asked the government to intervene.

Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), members of the INC including an MLA staged a protest in Killiyoor town (Kanniyakumari, Tamil Nadu) against the DMK-led state government's proposal to merge a few village panchayats in the constituency with the nearby town panchayats.

On 17 January 2024, municipal workers of the Sakleshpur Town Municipal Council staged a protest in front of a house in Sakleshpur town (Hassan, Karnataka) against the house owner for throwing his waste outside when he saw municipal workers cleaning the area. A municipal worker told the house owner not to throw waste on the road following which he scolded the worker in a foul manner. The protesters also dumped waste in front of the house to mark their protest.

On 15 January 2024, members of the IYC staged a demonstration at the Collectorate in Alappuzha city (Kerala) against the arrest of the IYC state president for a prior demonstration against the LDF-led state government's Nava Kerala Sadas (public outreach program) on 20 December 2023. The demonstrators resorted to stone pelting and the police fired water cannons and lathi (baton) charge to disperse them. The police also beat up the demonstrators. Both IYC demonstrators including women and police personnel were injured.

On 15 January 2024, contractual employees of Visvesvaraya Iron and Steel Plant in Bhadravathi along with their families staged a protest in front of the residence an MP in Shimoga city (Shivamogga, Karnataka) demanding the reopening of the plant so that they could get their jobs back.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Federation of Karnataka Lorry Owners Association staged a protest march to the District Collector's office in Raichur city (Karnataka) demanding the BJP-led union government to withdraw the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870). The protesters demanded the withdrawal of the bill.

On 12 January 2024, street vendors affiliated to the Federation of Bengaluru District Street Vendors' Association staged a protest at the 27A Cross, Jayanagar 4th Block in Bengaluru city (Bengaluru Urban, Karnataka) when officials from the Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) arrived at the location and stopped the street vendors from continuing their business on the street by seizing their goods.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Valmiki Driver Association and the Sri Sathya Sai District Ekta Maha Manch staged a protest in Kadiri town (Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh) against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870). The protesters demanded that the government withdraw the law.

On 18 January 2024, scores of autorickshaw and truck drivers and owners affiliated to the North Karnataka Autorickshaw Drivers Association, Association of Karnataka Mini Goods Drivers, Hubballi, Transport and Lorry owners Association and the Dharwad District Goods Shed Owners Association took out a protest march from the Durgad Bail to the Mini Vidhana Soudha in Hubballi city (Dharwad, Karnataka) in response to a nationwide call by the Lorry Owners Association against the proposed new penal provisions for hit and run cases in the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (the recently passed replacement of the Indian Penal Code, 1870).

On 18 January 2024, members of the SFI staged a black flag protest in Cheppad village (Alappuzha, Kerala) against the Kerala Governor when he was on his way to Oachira in Kollam to attend a programme. The police arrested 15 protesters from three locations in connection with the protest.

Around 13 January 2024 (as reported), members of the Southern Railway Mazdoor Union (SRMU) launched a relay fast protest in Chennai city (Tamil Nadu) demanding the withdrawal of the New Pension Scheme and the implementation of the Old Pension Scheme. The protesters also condemned the attempts by the government to privatise the railway sector.

On 14 January 2024, members of the TDP staged a protest at the Gandhinagar circle in Dharmavaram town (Anantapur, Andhra Pradesh) against the YSRCP-led state government for the price increase of commodities, the high rate of unemployment and other issues in the state. The protesters also celebrated the Bhogi (harvest) festival, shouted slogans and burned copies of government orders and the First Information Report (FIR) against the TDP president to mark their protest.
On 17 January 2024, residents assaulted a man in the Thalakundah locality in Udhagamandalam town (The Nilgiris, Tamil Nadu) after he was caught sexually assaulting a minor.

On 16 January 2024, unidentified assailants attacked and assaulted a man associated with a Hindu organisation and his mother in Puttur city (Dakshina Kannada, Karnataka) when the man was distributing 'akshata' (uncooked rice grains offered to the deity during pujas and other such religious ceremonies). Both the man and his mother sustained injuries.

On 18 January 2024, the residents of Thalakundah staged a protest on the Ooty-Gudalur road and the Ooty-Masinagudi road in Udhagamandalam town (The Nilgiris, Tamil Nadu) demanding action against a man who was accused of sexually assaulting a minor. The protesters demanded that the accused be charged under the Goondas Act.

On 18 January 2024, residents of Sujatha Nagar staged a protest in Kavali town (Sri Potti Sriramulu Nellore, Andhra Pradesh) against the encroachment on a graveyard in the area. The protesters demanded the MLA and other government officials to intervene.

On 12 January 2024, two groups led by incumbent and former sarpanchs (village head) clashed and fired shots at each other in Anjaudha village under Mehgaon police station (Bhind, Madhya Pradesh) over political superiority. No one was injured in the clash. The administration canceled 71 gun licenses.

On 17 January 2024, 28 sanitation workers locked the main gate of the Amarpur Nagar Panchayat office in Amarpur town (Gomati, Tripura) and staged a protest to demand their pending salaries for 5 months.

On 10 January 2024, four persons strangled and killed three members of a family, including husband, his wife, and his eight-year old sister in Lalavadar village of Lathi taluka (Amreli district, Gujarat) over suspicion of practicing sorcery.

On 18 January 2024, unidentified persons shot and killed a 55-year-old farmer and injured another farmer at Mainma village under Amarpur police station (Banka district, Bihar) over a suspected land dispute. The injured farmer is admitted to Government Medical College and Hospital in Bhagalpur and is stable.
On 13 January 2024, a mob assaulted and killed a 53-year-old woman and set ablaze her home at Akasi village (Rohtas district, Bihar) over suspicion of practicing sorcery.

On 17 January 2024, two groups of construction workers, from the West Bengal state and Saharanpur District, UP, clashed with rods and sticks, near the Mandola area's Panchlok locality in Ghaziabad city (Uttar Pradesh) after the members of the Saharanpur group assaulted a member of the West Bengal group for objecting to them boarding their tractor. One person died and 5 were injured.

Around 15 January 2024 (as reported), local Adivasis staged a protest and blocked national highway 15 (coded to Namsai town, Namsai, Arunachal Pradesh) demanding authorities for the prompt arrest of attackers during the clash between All Tai Khamti Singpho Youths clashed with Adivasi Youths of Namsai (coded separately). Protesters also demanded medical compensation for the injured.

Around 19 January 2024 (as reported), the family members of a murdered Bajrang Dal member placed the body and held a protest at the old bus stand in Rajgarh city (Madhya Pradesh), demanding capital punishment for the culprits.

Around 4 January 2024 (week of), residents of Golf Links 1 staged a protest at Sector Omega 1 in Greater Noida city (Gautam Buddha Nagar, Uttar Pradesh) against the poor condition of the roads and sewerage system in the area.

Around 12 January 2024 (as reported), family members of the deceased staged a protest in Atrar Maaf village (Mahoba, Uttar Pradesh) demanding action against a policeman (sub-inspector) and another person whom the protesters accused of harassing and falsely implicating the deceased - who later committed suicide - in a fake robbery case. The policeman was removed from his post.

On 13 January 2024, 3 CPI-Maoist cadres, including Dumam Local Organization Squad (LOS) 'commander', surrendered to district police personnel (Dantewada, Chhattisgarh). 1 cadre was carrying a reward of Rs 5 lakh.

actor1: Police Forces of India (2014-) Special Operations Group
notes: On 18 January 2024, the Special Operations Group (SOG) personnel and Naxal-Maoist rebels exchanged fire for three long hours, during a clash, in Budhakhol forest under Kantamal police limits (Boudh district, Odisha), during a combing operation. The Naxal-Maoist rebels fled from the site. The SOG troopers destroyed a Maoist camp and seized a cache of articles from the dump. There were no casualties.

actor1: Police Forces of India (2014-) Special Task Force
notes: On 15 January 2024, Assam police's Special Task Force (STF) arrested a senior leader of CPI (Maoist) from the Inter-State Bus Terminus (ISBT) in Guwahati city (Kamrup Metropolitan, Assam).

On 14 January 2024, overnight, All Tai Khamti Singpho Youths clashed with Adivasi Youths of Namsai in Namsai town (Namsai, Arunachal Pradesh) using sharp weapons like machete and guns over a personal dispute that started between two people from the respective communities. The feud turned into a communal clash that injured over 15 people and damaged several vehicles. A curfew was immediately announced after the event.

On 16 January 2024, a mob of locals vandalized the residence of a man and destroyed his car along with a part of his house at Jamalpur area in Dholai village (Cachar, Assam) over suspicions of the murder of another local. Locals claimed that the suspected man and 20 of his friends attacked 3 locals over a long standing rivalry and one of them was killed.

On 12 January 2024, neighbors attacked and critically injured a 70 year old woman with a sharp weapon at Bhadura village under Masturi police station area (Bilaspur district, Chhattisgarh) on the suspicion of practicing sorcery.

On 14 January 2024, three upper caste men abused and assaulted a 17-year-old Dalit boy in Chandlodia locality of Ahmedabad city (Ahmadabad, Gujarat) for moving around in the lane of an upper caste neighborhood.
On 12 January 2024, owners of a textile weaving unit assaulted a worker in Pipodara (Surat, Gujarat) for an unknown reason. This led to a demonstration by the workers (coded separately).

On 17 January 2024, a group of herdsmen assaulted an anti-cattle squad from the Surat Municipal Corporation (SMC) members with sticks in the Pandesara area of Surat city (Gujarat) while the squad was rounding up stray cattle. One employee sustained serious head injuries and was immediately admitted to the new civil hospital.
On 18 January 2024, a mob assaulted a 'drunk' ambulance driver in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) after the driver mowed down six pedestrians injuring them.

On 18 January 2024, two groups clashed with stones, sticks, and sharp objects in Nemawar village under Khudel tehsil (Indore, Madhya Pradesh) over a land dispute. Six persons were seriously injured in the cash.

On 18 January 2024, a former sarpanch and supporters assaulted the incumbent woman Sarpanch (the head of local government body) and her husband in Chasakhanda panchayat (Jajapur district, Odisha) over an ongoing political rivalry. The incumbent Sarpanch and her husband are admitted to the district hospital.

On 13 January 2024, 25-30 young people assaulted and hurled casteist remarks at a young man from the Dalit community at the Khurram Nagar area in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) over a dispute that took place during a cricket match. Assailants are suspected of urinating on the young man - the police have denied the claim.

On 18 January 2024, family members of the rape accused pelted stones at a police team (on duty) in Machhlishahr town (Jaunpur, Uttar Pradesh) when the police arrived in the area to arrest the accused. 2 policemen were injured.

On 15 January 2024, displaced locals living near Silsako lake staged a protest using placards and burned a traditional bonfire in Guwahati city (Kamrup Metropolitan, Assam) against the state government for the forced eviction of residents living around Silsako lake. Protesters also demanded compensation and rehabilitation.

On 16 January 2024, villagers under the aegis of Chhattisgarh Kisan Sabha (an AIKS affiliate) and Bhoo-visthapit Rojgar Ekta Sangh (united front for employment for the displaced) held a sit-in-protest at the South Eastern Coalfields' GM office in Kusmunda town (Korba, Chhattisgarh), demanding resolution of pending employment cases.

On 18 January 2024, members of the Jayati-Jayati Hindu Mahan Sangathan and Gau Seva Dal (cow service groups) staged a protest in Dharoli village (Jind, Haryana) demanding actions against the manager of the cow shelter whom the protesters accused of turning the shelter into a slaughterhouse house after videos of scattered skins and skeletons of cows were circulated online.

On 14 January 2024, local people (likely from the Hindu community based on the issue - which involves a Hindu temple) staged a protest near Dadh village (Kangra, Himachal Pradesh) demanding action against officials from the Forest Department for removing solar lights from the roads leading to an ancient Hindu temple.

On 16 January 2024, unemployed young people gathered, including members of ABVP, under the banner of various student outfits and staged a protest outside the Deputy Commissioner's (DC) office in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) against the state government's decision to recruit guest teachers.

On 16 January 2024, unemployed young people gathered under the banner of various student outfits and staged a protest outside the Deputy Commissioner's (DC) office in Solan city (Himachal Pradesh) against the state government's decision to recruit guest teachers.

On 16 January 2024, local people and the Pradhan (village chief) of Batthan panchayat staged a protest in Thural village (Kangra, Himachal Pradesh) against illegal mining in the Neugal River despite repeated complaints. The protesters accused the Mining and Forest departments of colluding with the illegal miners.

On 18 January 2024, hundreds of junior office assistant (IT) candidates staged a protest, raising slogans, outside the State Secretariat in Shimla city (Himachal Pradesh) demanding the declaration of the examination (conducted in 2021) results for the posts of junior office assistant (IT).

On 15 January 2024, members of INC, during a 12-hour bandh (shutdown strike), blocked the National Highway-49 at Nilabenisahi village (coded as Deogarh, Odisha) over irregularities at paddy mandis and anti-farmer policies of the state government.

On 19 January 2024, hundreds of AKSU and KPP(U) members jointly held a protest by walking on a railway track near Jaldhaka rail bridge (coded as Jaldhaka town, Jalpaiguri district, WB) against BJP and TMC to demand a separate Kamtapur state and that the BJP-led central government initiate peace talks with KLO.

On 19 January 2024, about 500-700 unemployed young people gathered under the banner of United Unemployed Morcha and staged a protest march, raising slogans, to the Deputy Commissioner's (DC) office in Nahan town (Sirmaur, Himachal Pradesh) against the state government's decision to recruit guest teachers.

On 17 January 2024, AAP members held a protest as part of 7-day 'Badlav Yatra' (transformation march) at Jammu city (J&K) against the BJP-led central government over demonetization, proposed agricultural laws, claimed crony capitalism and control of constitutional agencies among other issues.
On 16 January 2024, women led by a former panch (village government head) held a protest at Kulwanta town (Udhampur district, J&K) over lack of water supply in the area for two months.
On 15 January 2024, MSJK members held a protest at Jammu city (J&K) against Pakistan and demanded that India take military action to control of Pakistan administered Kashmir.

On 17 January 2024, more than 200 members of ABVP held a protest outside Banganga police station in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) demanding action against police officials for assaulting ABVP members (coded separately).
On 11 January 2024, farmers held a protest in Kumariyarao village of Sonkatch tehsil (Dewas, Madhya Pradesh) against the installation of an electricity tower in the area.

On 16 January 2024, hundreds of farmers staged a protest at a collectorate in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) over their demands to increase compensation for their lands marked for acquisition for the Indore-Budhni railway line and outer ring road.

On 15 January 2024, over 4,000 guest teachers gathered at the agricultural produce market and held a parade with bowls in their hands to beg in Barwani town (Madhya Pradesh) to protest against the non-payment of their last six months' salaries. After the parade, the guest teachers reached the collectorate and submitted a memorandum.

On 18 January 2024, a large group of Valmiki community members reached the police station and held a protest in Moghat road in Khandwa city (Khandwa, Madhya Pradesh) demanding immediate action against the teacher for assaulting a student from the community.

On 17 January 2024, the members of the Maharashtra Mandir Mahasangh and Balumama temple Sarankshak Kruti Samiti (Hindu group) staged a one-day dharna (sit-in protest) at Kranti Jyoti Chowk in Gargoti town (Kolhapur district, Maharashtra) to protest against the potential takeover of the Balumama temple at Adamapur by the government.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of farmers staged a sit-in protest in Kantia village under Jatni police limits (Khordha district, Odisha) to protest against the 'katni chatni' (deduction of a certain quantity of the farm produce by the procurer to make up for possible moisture content in the procured crop) system.

On 13 January 2024, members of the Berozgar Sanjha Morcha (Unemployed United Front) staged a protest march, raising slogans, and burnt papers containing false promises (Larian Di Pand), along the Sangrur-Patiala Road towards the Chief Minister's residence in Sangrur city (Punjab) against the state government whom the protesters accused of making false promises, and demanded the provision of government jobs.

On 14 January 2024, passengers staged a protest at Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport in Amritsar city (Punjab) against Air India whom the protesters accused of giving false information regarding the departure time of the flight.

On 15 January 2024, members of labor unions gathered under the banner of Punjab Pradesh Palledar Mazdoor Union and staged a protest rally, raising slogans, along the Sangrur-Patiala highway and near the Chief Minister's residence in Sangrur city (Punjab) demanding the establishment of a labor board, work guarantee, fixed wage rates, and the removal of the contractual system in food grain procurement agencies.

On 15 January 2024, members of the Bilaspur District Truck Operators Co-op Transport Society staged a dharna (sit-in protest) along the Kiratpur Sahib-Una National Highway near Kiratpur Sahib town (Rupnagar, Punjab) against the Kiratpur Sahib Truck Operators Society (Dehni) over a dispute regarding the loading of materials.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Computer Teachers Union staged a protest by driving e-rickshaws in Patiala city (Punjab) against the state government for failing to fulfill their demands which included the provision of benefits under the sixth pay commission and the merger of computer teachers with the education department.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Mid-Day Meal Workers Union staged a protest rally near the residence of an AAP state minister in Patiala city (Punjab) demanding the provision of winter clothing and a revision in salaries.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Jat community under the aegis of Bharatpur-Dholpur Jat Aarakshan Sangharsh Samiti staged a massive protest near Jaicholi railway station (Bharatpur, Rajasthan) demanding reservation under central OBC quota for the community.

On 16 January 2024, Rajasthan civil services examination aspirants staged a protest outside the state legislative assembly in Jaipur city (Rajasthan) demanding the authorities reschedule civil services examination (Mains) Examinations.

On 12 January 2024, Rajasthan civil services examination aspirants including students staged a protest at the Rajasthan University (RU) main gate in Jaipur city (Rajasthan), demanding that the mains examination be postponed. Police tried to remove protesters from the site, however, the protest continued.

On 18 January 2024, provisionally selected candidates under the aegis of nursing officer and paramedical recruitment Sangharsh Samiti 2023 held a protest outside the State Institute of Health and Family Welfare (SIHFW) in Jaipur city (Rajasthan) demanding appointment to the posts.
On 15 January 2024, relatives of a murdered man held a protest in Shahpura town (Jaipur, Rajasthan) and demanded compensation for the family.

On 19 January 2024, family members of two deceased kids and others from the neighborhood staged a protest, after kids died trying to escape from dogs, in the Baner area of Jodhpur city (Rajasthan) demanding the dogs were caught. The dog squad of the municipal corporation apprehended the pet dogs.

On 13 January 2024, family members of the deceased staged a protest and placed the deceased's body along the road outside the Nayagaon police station in Dharsepur village (Etah, Uttar Pradesh) against the death of a man whom the protesters claimed was murdered.

On 14 January 2024, hundreds of local people, including family members of the deceased, staged a protest near a hospital in Asaura village (Hapur, Uttar Pradesh) against the death of a young man whose bike collided with another bike along the Meerut Road.

On 15 January 2024, members of the Mool Nivas Bhu Kanoon Samanvay Sangharsh Committee (Original Residence Land Law Coordinating Committee) staged a protest by burning a copy of the Land Law Ordinance and dropping copies of permanent residency certificates in the Saryu River in Bageshwar town (Uttarakhand) over various demands including the designation of Gairsain as Uttarakhand's permanent capital, better employment opportunities, and protection of native residence.

On 19 January 2024, Adivasi community members (tribal group) held a protest at Malandighi village (Paschim Bardhaman district, WB) over people from their community not being employed by a gas lifting company operating in the area.

On 19 January 2024, AKSU and KPP(U) members jointly held a protest by blocking a railway track at Betgara town (Jalpaiguri district, WB) against BJP and TMC to demand a separate Kamtapur state and that the BJP-led central government initiate peace talks with KLO.

On 16 January 2024, pilgrims (likely Hindu based on the custom) held a protest by blocking roads at Rudranagar city (South 24 Parganas district, WB) over delay in getting a naval vessel for travel back to source after the pilgrimage was over.

On 16 January 2024, pilgrims (likely Hindu based on the custom) held a protest by blocking roads at Krishnanagar city (Nadia district, WB) over delay in getting a naval vessel for travel back to source after the pilgrimage was over.

On 13 January 2024, workers and INTTUC members held a protest at Raninagar town (Jalpaiguri district, WB) to demand that workers be retained after a new management (Moon Beverages) took over control of an industrial site earlier held by Coca Cola.
On 13 January 2024, locals staged a protest at Doshdorga town (Jalpaiguri district, WB) to demand installation of lights post a road accident in the area due to foggy conditions.

On 17 January 2024, members of ABVP held a protest outside Banganga police station in Indore city (Madhya Pradesh) demanding the registration of an FIR against an accused involved in a molestation case. Police resorted to lathi-charge (baton charge) against the protestors. This incident led to protests (coded separately).

On 13 January 2024, a large number of teacher recruitment candidates staged a protest outside the Chief Minister's (CM) residence in Lucknow city (Uttar Pradesh) demanding a meeting with the CM to discuss their long-standing demand for the release of appointment dates for candidates whose names have been announced. Protesters were detained by police and later released at Eco Park.

On 16 January 2024, unemployed young people gathered under the banner of various student outfits and staged protests across the Himachal Pradesh state (coded as Shimla city) against the state government's decision to recruit guest teachers.

On 16 January 2024, a mortar shell misfired from an artillery gun by Indian Army during a practice session and landed on the house of a civilian at Paharpur village (Udalguri, Assam) but did not explode. Residents managed to escape and no one was injured when the shell tore through the roof.
On 15 January 2024, locals vandalized the fire tender involved in a fatal accident and blocked the road in Patna city (Bihar), during a demonstration, demanding the arrest of the driver.

On 13 January 2024, a group of over 250 migrant workers threw stones and clashed with police in the Pipodara GIDC (Surat, Gujarat) during a demonstration against the assault of a laborer by the owners of the textile weaving units (coded separately). Police used at least 15 tear gas shells and detained about 140 persons. At least five police vans were damaged.

On 13 January 2024, pro-Palestine protesters held a march in Carrick-on-Shannon (Leitrim) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine.

On 13 January 2024, 1,000 pro-Palestine protesters held a march in Cork (Cork) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine.

On 13 January 2024, pro-Palestine protesters held a march in Ennis (Clare) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine.

On 13 January 2024, pro-Palestine protesters held a march in Killorglin (Kerry) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine.

On 13 January 2024, pro-Palestine protesters held a march in Longford (Kerry) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine.

On 13 January 2024, thousands of pro-Palestine protesters held a march and rally in Dublin (Dublin) to call for an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and sanctions on Israel. The protest was organized by IPSC as part of a global day of action in solidarity with Palestine. Several unspecified political parties, trade unions, students, and community groups from across the country joined the demonstration.

source: Osaka Action that Doesn't Allow the Construction of Military Bases at Henoko
notes: On 13 January 2024, about 30 people protested in front of Osaka station in Osaka, Osaka against the construction of a U.S. military base in Henoko, Okinawa.

source: Asahi Shimbun; Mainichi Shimbun; Osaka Action that Doesn't Allow the Construction of Military Bases at Henoko
notes: On 13 January 2024, more than 200 people, including foreign exchange students from countries such as Bahrain and Tunisia, a movie producer, a movie director, and a translator from the U.S., protested in front of Osaka station in Osaka, Osaka against the Israeli attack on Gaza. They called for an immediate ceasefire in the armed conflict between Hamas and Israel and the freedom of Palestine. The event was called 'Gaza Emergency Action Kansai.'

source: Osaka Action that Doesn't Allow the Construction of Military Bases at Henoko
notes: On 12 January 2024, more than 30 people protested in Osaka against the initiation of stone placement in Oura Bay, Okinawa, to construct a U.S. military base.

On 13 January 2024, people protested in front of the building of Cine Wind in Niigata, Niigata, calling for an immediate ceasefire in the armed conflict between Hamas and Israel in response to the Israeli invasion of Gaza.

On 15 January 2024, members of the Helicopter Base Opposition Group protested in canoes and boats on the sea of Henoko in Nago, Okinawa (coded at Coast of Nago, Okinawa) against the land reclamation on the Oura Bay side of Henoko to construct a U.S. military base. Five canoes entered a restricted area to stop the carriers which were transporting stones for the construction of the base, but were stopped by the Coast Guard (based on the context, the protest was likely dispersed).
On 12 January 2024, people protested at Funamori Park in Hachioji, Tokyo, against the use of nuclear plants.

On 17 January 2024, people, including a member of the 'Realization of the Voice of the Indigenous Ainu People! Executive Committee,' gathered in front of Osaka station in Osaka, Osaka, to protest against a member of the House of Councilors from the LDP for repeatedly making discriminatory remarks against the Ainu people and Zainichi Koreans. They called on the LDP and the government to address and rectify acts of discrimination.

On 16 January 2024, people protested in 5 canoes and 1 boat on the sea of Henoko in Nago, Okinawa (coded at Coast of Nago, Okinawa) against the land reclamation on the Oura Bay side of Henoko to construct a U.S. military base.
On 17 January 2024, about 20 people protested in front of the Camp Schwab gate in Nago, Okinawa against the construction of a U.S. military base in Henoko, Okinawa.
On 13 January 2024, people protested in Kyoto, Kyoto against the Israeli attack on Gaza. They called for the freedom of Palestine and an immediate ceasefire in the armed conflict.

On 15 January 2024, people protested in front of Nakano station in Tokyo - Nakano against the missile evacuation drill in Nakano, in which the Japan Self-Defense Force participated. They perceive the drill as preparation for potential armed conflict.
On 14 January 2024, hundreds of people marched in Ginza, Tokyo - Chuo against vaccines and the Pandemic Treaty. They claimed that the treaty violated Japan's autonomy.

source: Sapporo Action that Asks for the Immediate Cancellation of Landfill of Henoko
notes: On 14 January 2024, people protested in front of the Sapporo PARCO building in Sapporo, Hokkaido against the construction of a U.S. military base in Henoko, Okinawa.

On 18 January 2024, 40 people, including members of the Women's Democratic Club and 'Sayonara Nuclear Power Plant, Biwako Network,' marched in Mihama, Fukui against the resumption of operation of Mihama Nuclear Power Plant Unit 3.

source: Citizens' Network for Evacuation from Radiation
notes: On 13 January 2024, people protested in front of the Shinjuku ALTA building in Tokyo - Shinjuku against the use of nuclear plants and the release of treated water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the sea. The Citizens' Network for Evacuation from Radiation organized the event.

On 13 January 2024, people protested in Ikebukuro, Tokyo - Toshima against various topics, including the political funds scandal involving the LDP, the government's authorization of weapon exports, the militarization of Okinawa, the release of treated water from the Fukushima Daiichi Nuclear Power Plant into the sea, gender inequality, and the proposed abolition of the current health insurance card system. Additionally, they called for the freedom of Palestine.
On 16 January 2024, people protested in front of the headquarters of Taisei Corporation in Tokyo - Shinjuku, calling on the company to stop the construction of a military base in Henoko, Okinawa.

On 18 January 2024, workers of the Kazakhstan Railways company gathered on the premises of Bopay train station near the village of Kuryk in Karakiya district of Mangystau region demanding 50 percent pay raise and bonuses.

On 18 January 2024, workers of the West Oil Software company protested outside their employer's office in Zhetibay township in Karakiya district of Mangystau region demanding that the authorities resolve their dispute while local residents gathered outside the town administration calling for the authorities to assist workers with resolution of their demands. Similarly, workers of the Zhetibay train station gathered on the premises in Zhetibay township in protest of low wages demanding 50 percent pay raise and bonuses.

On 19 January 2024, supporters of Marat Zhylanbayev, a detained leader of the unregistered political party Alga Kazakhstan, gathered outside the city court near Almaty district of Astana city demanding his release from custody due to political persecution of the opposition party.

On 19 January 2024, workers of the Kazakhstan Railways state-owned company gathered on the premises of TCHE-15 operational locomotive depot in Beyneu township of Beyneu district in Mangystau region demanding 100 percent pay raise and bonuses.

On 19 January 2024, workers of the SHCH-10 signal and communications facility, and VCHD-33 operational train car depot of the Kazakhstan Railways state-managed company gathered on the premises of Sayotes train station in Mangystau region demanding 100 percent pay raise and bonuses.

On 19 January 2024, workers of the West Oil Software company protested outside their employer's office in Zhetibay township of Karakiya district in Mangystau region demanding that the authorities resolve their labor dispute and expressing their support for protesting workers of the Kazakhstan Railways state-owned company.

On 15 January 2024, more than 600 people in Khnat commune carrying banners staged a protest outside Siem Reap provincial hall in Sla Kram, Siemreap (Siem Reap) demanding the national government and UNESCO to halt newly enforced restrictions on buildings and repairs imposed by the APSARA Authority, responsible for overseeing the Angkor Archaeological Park.

On 16 January 2024, members of the Busan Civil Society Organization for Kori Nuclear Power Plant Unit 2 Closure held a press conference with banners in front of the Busan Metropolitan City Hall (Busan - Yeonje). They urged the local government to scrutinize any cumulative stress in nuclear power plant facilities over the safety concerns of the recent earthquake in Japan.

On 17 January 2024, members of the Citizens' Coalition for Economic Justice (CCEJ) held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the CCEJ office (Seoul City - Jongno). They urged the authorities to exclude 34 lawmakers, citing their lacking abilities as lawmakers, including the number of bills they have proposed as chief authors.

On 16 January 2024, youth members of the Democratic Party of Korea (DPK) in Gangjin County held a press conference with banners at the Gangjin-gun Office (Namseong-ri, Gangjin-eup, Gangjin-gun). They condemned the county council for proposing the motion of non-confidence resolution on the nation's youngest chairperson of the council, saying it was an unprovoked mistrust and political repression of a fellow council member.

On 15 January 2024, members of the Progressive Party held a press conference with banners in front of the Dongnimmun Gate (Seoul City - Seodaemun). They condemned the Seodaemun-gu Office for forcefully removing their banners, calling for an immediate investigation of the First Lady.

On 18 January 2024, members of the DPK, Justice Party, and Progressive Party held a press conference with a banner and picketed at Yangsan City Hall (Yangsan). They protested against the president's veto of the bills, including bills on a special investigation over the development scandal in Daejang-dong and the first lady's alleged involvement in stock manipulation.

On 19 January 2024, members of the Korean Peasants League (KPL) held a press conference with a banner in front of the Jeju Special Self-Governing Provincial Government (Jeju). They urged the authorities to legislate an ordinance to support farmers amid the increase in the purchase price of agricultural materials.

On 19 January 2024, protesters, including religious, civic, labor, education, art groups and members of the Bereaved Family Council for the 10.29 Itaewon Disaster held a press conference and picketed at Seoul Press Center (Seoul City - Jung). They urged authorities to enact a special law to further probe into the cause of the disaster within this year and prepare measures to prevent the recurrence of such tragedy.

On 16 January 2024, members of the Joint Action Against Media Control, composed of five journalists and civic groups, held a press conference and picketed in front of the Seoul Metropolitan Police Agency (Seoul City - Jongno), condemning the police's search and seizure of the Korea Communications Standards Commission (KCSC). They called for the investigation of the KCSC chairman, suspected of instigating civil complaints.

On 17 January 2024, protesters, including members of the Cultural Action, Citizens' Coalition for Democratic Media, and National Union Of Mediaworkers (under KCTU), held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the Korea Broadcasting Hall (Seoul City - Yangcheon). They urged the Korea Communications Standards Commission chairperson to step down for his alleged manipulation of civil complaints.

On 13 January 2024, about 100 protesters affiliated with the KCTU Korean Public Service and Transportation Workers' Union and the bereaved families of a taxi driver who burned himself to death, denouncing overdue wages and demanding the implementation of a complete monthly wage system for taxi drivers, held a rally in front of the Seoul Gangseo-gu Office (Seoul City - Gangseo). They urged for strict punishment of the taxi company, acknowledging that the cause of the deceased's death was workplace bullying.

On 15 January 2024, members of the Korean Confederation of Trade Unions (KCTU) South Gyeongsang branch held a press conference with banners in front of the Gyeongsangnam-do Provincial Government Office (Changwon). They urged opposition parties to make a progressive coalition to win the general election. They criticized the current administration's reduction of the budget for the stabilization of the people's livelihoods.

On 15 January 2024, unionized employees of Pureun Dure Cooperative Association (KCTU affiliate) held a press conference holding banners in front of the Pureun Dure headquarters (Incheon - Yeonsu). They urged the company to abolish the forced shift of part-time workers to different branches without employee consent and suspend hiring contract workers.

On 16 January 2024, members of the Korean Confederation of Trade Unions (KCTU) South Gyeongsang branch, Korean Metal Workers' Union (KMWU), and World Without Serious Accidents held a press conference with banners in front of the Ministry of Employment and Labor Tongyeong Branch Office (Juklim-ri, Gwangdo-myeon, Tongyeong). They urged authorities to conduct special labor supervision and investigate the managers of the Daewoo Shipbuilding (Hanwha Ocean) amid five industrial accidents that occurred since the enforcement of the Serious Accidents Punishment Act.

On 16 January 2024, members of the Korean Mart Labor Union (KCTU affiliate) held a press conference with banners in front of the Dongdaemun-gu Office (Seoul City - Dongdaemun), opposing the change of large retailers' mandatory closures to weekdays. They called for ensuring the retail workers' right to health and rest.

On 16 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated National Democratic Post Office Union (KPTU Post) held a press conference with banners in front of the Gwanghwamun Post Office (Seoul City - Jongno). They condemned the Korea Post for its poor working environment and demanded wage increases.

On 17 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean General League of Unions held a press conference with a banner and picketed at Wonju City Hall (Wonju). They urged the authorities to strictly punish Wonju City Facility Management Corporation for allegedly dismissing the head of the labor union as part of labor union oppression.

On 18 January 2024, the School Irregular Worker's Union (under KCTU) Busan branch members held a press conference with a banner in front of the Busan Metropolitan City Office of Education (Busan - Busanjin). They urged the authorities to withdraw their plan to expand the Neulbom School system, a full-time child-care system, citing it would bring confusion and conflict at schools due to unpreparedness.

On 18 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Government Employees' Union (KGEU) Chungbuk branch held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the National Election Commission Chungbuk Office (Cheongju). They urged the authorities to raise allowances for government employees mobilized for voting and ballot counting duties.

On 18 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Government Employees' Union (KGEU) and Confederation of Korean Government Employees' Unions held a press conference and picketed in front of the National Election Commission (Gwacheon). They urged the authorities to stop the forced mobilization of government employees for the generation election and provide them with appropriate payment.

On 18 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Government Employees' Union (KGEU) Gwangju branch held a press conference with a banner and picketed in front of the National Election Commission Gwangju Office (Gwangju - Seo). They urged the authorities to raise allowances for government employees mobilized for voting and ballot counting duties.

On 18 January 2024, members of Kakao Krew Union (KCTU affiliate) held a demonstration holding pickets at Kakao Agit (Seongnam). They condemned the executives for the recent mobile device forensics of Kakao Mobility employees, citing it as illegal.

On 18 January 2024, members of the KCTU-affiliated Korean Mart Labor Union held a press conference with a banner and picketed at the Korean Federation of Service Workers' Union office (Seoul City - Seodaemun). On 15 January 2024, farmers protested outside the Ministry of Agriculture in Bucharest, demanding measures in support of the agricultural field, including the reducing of the insurance fees, fuel prices, as well as the tighter control over agricultural imports from Ukraine.

On 15 January 2024, farmers and truck drivers drove their vehicles in Constanta in protest of the high insurance fees, high fuel prices, and facilitated access for Ukrainian truckers in Romanian ports. An AUR MP left the protest shortly after joining and being sent off by the farmers.

On 17 January 2024, around 100 farmers and truckers drove their vehicles in Siret against high insurance fees, the high fuel price, and the high taxes.

Around 1 January 2024 (as reported), Russian police arrested upwards of 3000 undocumented migrants in St. Petersburg (Admiralteyskiy, Saint Petersburg) after checking their residence permits on the city streets during the city's New Year's celebration. Almost 100 of them are facing deportation.

Property destruction: On 7 January 2024, 3 men set on fire the apartment door of a Sputnik-channel host in St. Petersburg (Admiralteyskiy, Saint Petersburg). The men claim that they were guided by an unidentified individual who offered them $1000 for the act. The motives of the individual were presumably political, as Sputnik is part of the propagandistic Russia Today holding.
On 13 January 2024, Ukrainian military shelled with 3 mortar rounds the village of Maryino (Shebekinskiy, Belgorod). No casualties.
On 13 January 2024, Ukrainian military shelled with 2 mortar rounds the village of Murom (Shebekinskiy, Belgorod). No casualties.

On 14 January 2024, Russian police briefly detained in a politician Tver (Tver) after she announced the founding of her own political party. She attempted to run for president as an independent anti-war candidate, but was denied a place on the ballot by the Central Election Commission.
On 14 January 2024, over 30 people protested in Ufa (Republic of Bashkortostan) in support of an activist who was facing time in prison for his eco-activism.

On 15 January 2024, around 5000 people protested in front of the Baymak district court in Baymak (Baymakskiy, Republic of Bashkortostan) in support of an activist who was facing time in prison for his eco-activism. They also demanded that Putin fire the head of the Republic who initially filed a complaint with the court about Alsynov's work.
Interception: On 15 January 2024, Russian military intercepted 3 Ukrainian missiles above Fatezhskiy district (coded to Fatezh (Fatezhskiy, Kursk)), No casualties.

Property destruction: On 7 January 2024, an unidentified perpetrator demolished the entrance of the Pride Info Center in Belgrade - Stari Grad. This is another instance where the LGBTQ+ community and their facilities are targeted in Serbia.

On 16 January 2024, health workers held a protest in front of the entrance of District General Hospital in Nuwara Eliya town (Nuwara Eliya, Central), demanding the Disturbance, Availability, and Transport (DAT) allowance of Rs 35,000 and against the unjust economic policies of the government.
