Property destruction: On 19 January 2024, unknown persons set fire to engine, three boats and four nets belonging to a Tamil fisherman at Udappu village in Puttalam town (Puttalam, North Western).

The protests were triggered by the recent announcement of the ruling Smer party to abolish a special prosecutor's office, which is responsible for investigating high-profile corruption cases.

On 12 January 2024, activists, including representatives of unionized refugees, gathered for a rally in Ljubljana, protesting against scheduled deportation of one of their members to Croatia.
On 12 January 2024, farmers held minor protests with bonfires along highways across the Slovenian Styria (coded to Maribor), declaring support to their counterparts in Germany.

On 13 January 2024, about 100 activists, mostly Ukrainians, gathered for a rally in Ljubljana, declaring their support to Ukraine and condemning the Russian aggression. A minor group of counter-activists gathered nearby, supporting Russia and condemning the actions of the US and Israel as well as the political representation of Ukraine. Only verbal skirmishes were reported.
On 16 January 2024, students gathered in Ljubljana for a demonstrative reading of Palestinian poetry, condemning what they see as genocide in Gaza and declaring their support to Palestine.
On 17 January 2024, students gathered for a rally in Ljubljana, calling for official recognition of Israeli actions in Gaza as 'genocide' and for independence of Palestine.

On 8 January 2024, people held a torchlight procession in Lulea, Norrbotten, to demand the creation of a law that would protect children from forced contact with parents who could harm them physically or psychologically. The demands were prompted by the tragic death of an 8-year-old child in January 2023.
On 12 January 2024, Women's Strike movement activists demonstrated in Malmo, Skane, in support of women's rights and to oppose violence against women.

On 13 January 2024, around 30 people demonstrated in Lulea, Norrbotten, to show support and solidarity with the people of Palestine amid ongoing Israeli offensive in Gaza. Activists from the Young Left of Sweden, the Revolutionary Communist Youth of Sweden (RKU), Socialist Alternative (ISA) and the Communist Party of Sweden (K) were present, among others.
On 13 January 2024, people demonstrated in Orebro to raise awareness about children and civilian casualties in Gaza and to call for a permanent ceasefire in the Hamas-Israel conflict.

On 14 January 2024, around 3000 people marched in Gothenburg (Goteborg) to show support and solidarity with the people of Palestine and to condemn the Swedish government's political stance on the ongoing conflict in Gaza.

On 14 January 2024, around 60 demonstrators, including LGBTQ+ rights activists, gathered in Uppsala to oppose a documentary about transgender people produced by the national broadcaster, claiming that the documentary was transphobic and biased. Activists from the association Everything For Everyone, among others, took part in the event.
On 19 January 2024, Women's Strike movement activists demonstrated in Falun, Dalarna, in support of women's rights and to oppose violence against women.
On 19 January 2024, Women's Strike movement activists demonstrated in Jonkoping in support of women's rights and to oppose violence against women.

actor2: Police Forces of Thailand (2023-2025) Border Patrol Police
notes: On 16 January 2024, a bomb planted by Malay Muslim separatists went off along a road in Ai Yaeng village, Si Sakhon, Si Sakhon (Narathiwat), targeting a Border Patrol Police armored vehicle carrying the officers who were on duty protecting university teachers distributing aid to flood victims. The bomb damaged the officers' pickup truck but did not cause any injuries.

On 13 January 2024, National Children's Day, members of Amnesty International Thailand gathered and held placards at the Parliament in Bangkok - Dusit to demand the Prime Minister and the Justice Minister to cease intimidating and prosecuting children under 18 who have exercised their right to freedom of expression and peaceful protest from 2020 to the present.

On 13 January 2024, members of ConForAll gathered placards at Government House in Bangkok - Dusit to submit a letter to the Prime Minister and protest against the proposed referendum question for drafting a new constitution in Thailand. They fear that the question, which prohibits amendments to specific sections of the constitution, may not be accepted by all sectors of society. The People's Group is demanding a meeting with the Prime Minister and the Cabinet to discuss potential issues with the proposed referendum question.

On 15 January 2024, people gathered to protest with placards outside of the Taichung District Prosecutor's Office in Dadu (Taichung City). They criticized a defendant involved in a case of document forgery and financial embezzlement.

On 15 January 2024, the convener of the TOPEP and other party members held a press conference with placards in Taipei City (Taipei City). They called for new election legislation to protect the rights of small parties.

On 13 January 2024, members of various trade groups marched with placards in New Taipei City (New Taipei City). They called on the incoming administration to improve trade relations with mainland China and work towards establishing free trade agreements with various regional groups around Taiwan.

On 15 January 2024, eight legislator-elects from the Taiwan People's Party held a press conference with placards at the Legislative Yuan in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They called for several legislative amendments, including the establishment of a parliamentary investigation system, strengthening rights and education surrounding consent rights, and pushing for more transparency regarding the use of funds.

On 17 January 2024, members of the BSAA gathered to protest with banners and placards at the Kuomintang headquarters in Taipei City - Zhongshan district (Taipei City). They called on KMT chairman to step down in the wake of the recent KMT presidential loss.

On 17 January 2024, members of the NFTU held a press conference with a banner in Taipei City (Taipei City). They demanded that the Ministry of Education work towards securing and improving the disciplinary powers afforded to teachers, and not just reviewing campus safety measures.

On 18 January 2024, over 100 members of a fishers organization gathered to protest with banners and placards at an activity center in Ziguan district (Kaohsiung City). They protested against proposed naval development that would severely affect the environment of the fisheries and their livelihoods, and against the navy's failure to keep promises of compensation.

On 18 January 2024, members of various civil society organizations, including lawyers, gathered to protest with banners and placards in front of the Supreme Prosecutor's Office in Taipei City - Zhongzheng district (Taipei City). They demanded that the office re-examine the conviction of a former police officer in the late 90s based on allegedly wrongful kidnapping charges.

On 18 January 2024, members of an LGBTQ+ hotline organization gave a press conference with placards in Taipei City (Taipei City). They explained the ways in which transgender individuals continue to receive maltreatment and misunderstanding in Taiwanese society, and called on the government, universities, and other institutions to move away from binary gender-based identification systems in order to work towards more inclusivity to transgender Taiwanese.

On 18 January 2024, environmental groups gathered to protest with banners and placards outside of Taichung City Hall in Taichung City (Taichung City). They criticized the planned extension of an industrial park into Taichung's Dadu mountain.

On 19 January 2024, members of a civic congressional watch group held a press conference with placards at their headquarters in Taipei City - Da'an district (Taipei City). They demanded that all political parties set aside their political differences in order to maintain accountability to the Taiwanese people, and provide blueprint plans for reforming the next national congress.

On 19 January 2024, a TPP legislator-elect and others gave a press conference with banners and placards in Taipei City (Taipei City). They called on the Control Yuan to release a statement of action regarding the President-elect's controversial housing situation.

Elections/referendum: On 13 January 2024, presidential and legislative elections were held in Taiwan (coded at Taipei City). The DPP candidate won the presidency. However, DPP lost the legislative majority.

On 14 January 2024, community members held a 'Freedom to Read-In' rally at the rotunda of the state Capitol in Boise (Idaho) to oppose legislative efforts to limit certain books at public libraries. The Idaho Library Association, Meridian Library Alliance, North Central Idaho Alliance, Community Library Network Alliance and Rediscovered Books were present.

On 13 January 2024, hundreds of students, parents and teachers marched from Hotel Captain Cook to Town Square Park in Anchorage (Alaska) to call for an increase in the state's Base Student Allocation for education funding. The National Education Association Alaska and Great Alaska Schools were also present.

On 16 January 2024, Housekeys Action Network Denver members and supporters held a rally outside of the City and County Building steps in Denver (Colorado) to support legislation to limit homeless encampment closures during winter.

On 17 January 2024, Washington State University's Coalition of Academic Student Employees members held a picket outside of Martin Stadium in Pullman (Washington) to demand greater healthcare benefits as contract negotiations continue.

On 15 January 2024, Macy's workers with UFCW 3000 held a 'Unfair Labor Practice' strike outside of the Alderwood Mall location in Lynnwood (Washington) to demand an increase in pay and benefits during labor contract negotiations.

On 18 January 2024, almost 20 people dressed in bridal gowns held a demonstration in the state Capitol in Olympia (Washington) to support efforts to ban child marriages in the state. Unchained At Last, Zonta International and the AHA Foundation were present.

On 6 January 2024, 30 demonstrators rallied at May Day Plaza in Minneapolis (Minnesota) to demand that the US Supreme Court continue to allow access to the abortion drug mifepristone amid the review of a ruling which could limit the drug's accessibility. Those present included members of the MN Anti-War Committee, MN Abortion Action Committee (MNAAC), Twin Cities Coalition for Justice 4 Jamar, and MN Immigrant Rights Action Committee.

On 15 January 2024, students with the Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity staged a vigil, rally, and march on the campus of Eastern Illinois University in Charleston (Illinois) in honor of Martin Luther King Jr. Day and carried signs demanding an end to fighting in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

On 18 January 2024, pro-Palestinian demonstrators rallied inside and outside the Indiana State Capitol in Indianapolis (Indiana) to protest the passing of a bill which re-defined antisemitism in educational settings, claiming it disallowed for criticism of Israel as a state in the same way other countries are criticized, and called for a free Palestinian state amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. One demonstrator was removed from inside the Capitol, but there were no arrests. Those present included students.

On 16 January 2024, student workers with the Indiana Graduate Workers Coalition (IGWC) staged a rally on the campus of Indiana University in Bloomington (Indiana) to demand graduate workers receive a higher minimum wage and for the IGWC to be officially recognized by the administration.

On 13 January 2024, demonstrators staged a pro-Palestinian rally in Fort Wayne (Indiana) to support a free Palestine and condemn US support of Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Those present included members of Fort Wayne for Peace.

On 13 January 2024, about 60 demonstrators with Michiana Friends of Palestine staged a pro-Palestinian rally in South Bend (Indiana) to demand that South Bend Common Council support a resolution to call for a ceasefire in Gaza and support Palestine amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023.

The protesters wore clothing with Nazi symbols and held antisemitic signs, including one that said, 'End Jewish supremacy today.' Demonstrators also called a Black man a racial slur when he confronted them. The protesters eventually relocated to city hall. Multiple demonstrators appeared to be armed with handguns.

The demonstration was organized by Practice Mercy Foundation, a Christian organization dedicated to protecting immigrants on the Texas-Mexico border. After the Brownsville demonstration, the protesters marched across the border to hold a prayer vigil in Matamoros, Mexico.

notes: On 18 January 2024, about two dozen pro-Palestine protesters gathered in the capitol rotunda in Iowa City (Iowa) to demonstrate their support for Palestine, to demonstrate against Israel, to call for a ceasefire, and to demonstrate against the resolution recently passed by the Iowa House of Representatives declaring its support for Israel amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. Protesters included members of Iowa City Action for Palestine.

On 16 January 2024, demonstrators rallied at a city council meeting in Berkeley (California) to call for a ceasefire in Gaza amid the Israel-Hamas war which began in October 2023. The council met in private chambers due to the disruption.

Around 3 January 2024 (month of), in Pichucalco, Chiapas, a person was killed and a decapitated human head was found inside a cooler on the road to Teapa. The victim was a police officer who was kidnapped on 22 December 2023 along with another man, who still is missing. 1 fatality.

Around 16 January 2024 (as reported), a Kaduna militia abducted a man who volunteered to deliver ransom to them in Birnin Gwari LGA (Kaduna). The victim is a retired school teacher and an unspecified amount was demanded as ransom from his family. The victim was released after he an unknown amount was paid as ransom, he was also thoroughly beaten before he was released, due to the delayed payment of the ransom [coded separately].

source: Daily Independent (Nigeria); Daily Trust (Nigeria); Sahara Reporters; Vanguard (Nigeria)
notes: On 18 January 2024, a suspected Fulani militia shot and killed two soldiers and two civilians in Okokolo (Agatu, Benue). The soldiers were responding to the activities of the marauders when they were ambushed. The militia opened fire indiscriminately on some villages, forcing the villagers, including women and children, to flee their homes and scamper for safety. One soldier was also injured in the ambush. Some sources report that 4 soldiers and 1 NSCDC operative were killed, while the commander of operation whirl punch claimed that two soldiers, 1 NSCDC operative were killed, while two other soldiers were wounded.

source: Baghdad Today; National Iraqi News Agency; ROJ News; Sotaliraq
notes: Interception: On 19 January 2024, Iraqi army shot down an unidentified drone near Buhriz district, Diyala.

actor2: FARC Dissident - Second Marquetalia: Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia Dissident Faction (Segunda Marquetalia)
source: Diario La Libertad; El Espectador; Vanguardia Liberal
notes: On 1 January 2024, in the rural area of Roberto Payan municipality (Narino), for the second consecutive day, military forces clashed with members of the Second Marquetalia FARC dissident group in the Yayaral vereda. Although the government had claimed that a leader of the armed group and one of his bodyguards were killed, the group's leader posted a video days later announcing that he was alive. He also claimed that a member of the armed group, two civilians, and a teenager were killed by the military over two days of clashes between 31 December and 1 January 2024. 4 fatalities split over two events.

On 19 January 2024, in San Roque (Magdalena), unidentified armed individuals opened fire on a police vehicle injuring two officers and one civilian who was being transported in the vehicle. There were no fatalities.

On 16 January 2024, in Chinandega province, Chinandega, the police arrested a Mexican priest. The detention occurred after the government cancelled the legal status of 16 non-governmental organizations, one of which the priest was part of. There is no information on the motives of the arrest. Another report indicates that he was later exiled along with other priests, including one of Nicaragua citizenship and another of Mexican nationality.

source: El Nuevo Dia (Puerto Rico); El Vocero de Puerto Rico
notes: On 17 January 2024, in Ponce, Ponce, three gang members in a vehicle opened fire on a man driving a car near the Mercurio street, injuring him (drive-by). The victim was described as the owner of a drug selling point. Following the attack, police forces intervened and clashed with the gang members. During the police chase, a gangster was shot and injured. Two other gangsters were arrested. The criminals set fire to several vehicles before being arrested.

On 9 January 2024, in Carora (Lara), a Lara state police officer shot and killed an adolescent boy that was driving a motorcycle in the 14 de Febrero Avenue, after the victim disobeyed the order of stopping from the officer. The boy was driving without a helmet, he was shot dead in an area close to his home. The policeman had fired warning shots, and two of them hit the victim in the head, which may have been accidental. The officer fled the area leaving his service motorcycle behind. Relatives of the victim set fire to it in retaliation. The day after, another group of residents of the area and relatives of the adolescent victim set fire to a police outpost nearby (coded separately). Four Lara state police officers, including the perpetrator, turned themselves in shortly after, and remain in CICPC police custody. 1 fatality.

Other: On 26 January 2024, in Caracas - Libertador (Distrito Capital), the Supreme Court of Justice (TSJ) upheld the 30 June 2023 decision of the Comptroller General of the Republic of disqualifying the VV presidential election candidate, Maria Corina Machado Parisca, for 15 years. The TSJ accuses the candidate of corruption, conspiracy, and treason.

source: Daily Sun (South Africa); GroundUp; SABC News
notes: On 17 January 2024, about 30 parents gathered outside Florida High School in Johannesburg - Fleurhof (City of Johannesburg, Gauteng) to demand their children be placed in Florida Park High School instead of Die Burger Secondary School.

notes: On 24 January 2024, in Villa Gobernador Galvez (Rosario, Santa Fe), protesters, including members of the Association of State Workers (ATE) and Argentine Workers' Central Union - Autonomous (CTA-A), marched from San Martin Square to Las Madres Square against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70 /2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy and the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State, and restrictions on demonstrations. The mobilization took place within the framework of a national strike in rejection of the policies promoted by President Javier Milei's government.

source: El Faro Colon Buenos Aires; Rosario3
notes: On 24 January 2024, in Casilda (Caseros, Santa Fe), residents and members of social and union organizations protested in front of Banco Nacion, at the intersection of Casado and Buenos Aires streets, against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70/2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy and the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State, and the restrictions on demonstrations. The Classist Union Current (CSC) Excluded Workers Movement (MTE), Metallurgical Workers Union (UOM), and Argentine Workers' Central Union - Autonomous (CTA-A), among other organizations, called for the demonstration. The mobilization took place within the framework of a national strike in rejection of the policies promoted by President Javier Milei's government.

On 24 January 2024, in General Pico (Maraco, La Pampa), residents, artists, health workers, and members of social, political, and union organizations demonstrated in San Martin Square against the Decree of Necessity and Urgency 70/2023 (DNU), which deregulated the economy and the so-called Omnibus law, which proposes reforms in the State. Additionally, protesters expressed concern about protecting the rights to education, health, protest, and the country's natural resources and sovereignty. The mobilization, called by the Argentine Workers' Central Union (CTA), Patria Grande Front, Association of State Workers (ATE), and Excluded Workers Movement, among other groups, took place within the framework of a national strike in rejection of the policies promoted by the government of President Javier Milei.

On 23 January 2024, in Banfield (Lomas de Zamora, Buenos Aires), dozens of people, including friends, neighbors, family members, and police officers, protested at the funeral caravan of the police officer's daughter, murdered the day before during an attempted assault, and demanded justice. The Minister of Security, Patricia Bullrich (PRO and JxC), and the Vice-President, Victoria Villarruel (Liberty Advances), joined the collective action.

On 22 January 2024, in Veintiocho de Noviembre (Guer Aike, Santa Cruz), nurses at the San Lucas Hospital protested the politicization of the health institution. They denounced the lack of supplies and the prioritization of hiring non-medical personnel.

On 22 January 2024, in La Plata (Buenos Aires), workers at a delivery company protested in front of the Municipality to demand greater security after a delivery man was shot during an attempted robbery of his motorcycle.
